overview: Adelaide International School offers a limited number of merit-based scholarships to eligible students each year. The scholarship recognises students with strong academic performance, leadership qualities, and community involvement. Successful applicants can receive recognition of their achievement and may receive a reduction of up to 50% of tuition fees.
description: Adelaide International School provides merit-based scholarships to students entering the school who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and achievement. The scholarship is awarded based on individual merit and not all applicants receive scholarships. Scholarships are based on full course tuition fees, not on any discounted fees. Generally, a scholarship will not exceed 50% of course fees, though scholarships in excess of 50% may be granted in exceptional circumstances. The selection process involves assessment of academic performance through the two most recent school reports, which must demonstrate at least average to high competence with passing grades in English being essential. Applicants must provide evidence through a letter from their school principal detailing unique circumstances that merit consideration for enrolment, plus certificates or letters of commendation from teachers or school principals. Scholarships do not apply to fees other than tuition fees; uniforms, homestay fees, and material fees are not subject to discount. The application requires completion of the AIS Scholarship Application Form available on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all prospective students; Must be of at least average to high competence academically; Passing grades in English are essential; Scholarships awarded based on individual merit; Not all applicants will receive scholarships; Scholarships awarded to applicants who best meet the application criteria
special_conditions: Scholarships are based on full course tuition fees, not on any discounted fees. In general, a scholarship will not exceed 50% of course fees. A scholarship in excess of 50% will only be granted in exceptional circumstances. Scholarships do not apply to any fees other than tuition fees including uniforms, homestay fees, and material fees. The terms and conditions applicable to payment of fees will apply to scholarship holders. Applicants must download and complete the AIS Scholarship Application Form to apply. Selection is based on evidence of academic competence from school reports, letter from school principal regarding unique circumstances, and certificates or letters of commendation.

overview: Adelaide Botanic High School offers the Health and Sciences (STEM) Pathways Program as a special entry program to families from all over South Australia, regardless of residential address. The program provides access to learning and facilities that support students on a Health and Sciences (STEM) high-level pathway who are seeking extension and stretch opportunities.
description: The Health and Sciences (STEM) Pathways Program at Adelaide Botanic High School provides up to 20 positions annually for out-of-zone students who demonstrate high-level interest and success in STEM areas. Students gain access to choice in a range of STEM-underpinned subjects, explicit development of problem-solving, collaboration, creativity, entrepreneurship and critical thinking skills evidenced in an ePortfolio, and mentoring by targeted school, industry or university students/staff. Applicants must provide a written component and copy of school report, submit a short 30-second video talking about personal interest in STEM, and sit the ACER Higher Ability Skills Test in Adelaide on Saturday, 28 February 2026 at a cost of $115.00. The program offers right of entry to Adelaide Botanic High School for out-of-zone students, targeted opportunities in Years 7-9 to participate in STEM events and competitions, Middle Years Studio participation, and support, challenge and skills to be successful and well prepared to enter high level tertiary Health and Sciences (STEM) courses.
eligibility_criteria: Students who reside outside the school zone; Demonstrated high-level interest and success in one or more STEM areas; Must be in Year 5 (in 2025) for 2026 entry; Must intend completing SACE Stage 2 (Year 12) at Adelaide Botanic High School; Must intend studying senior Mathematics, Science and/or Technology subjects; Must uphold the school's values and actively promote the program to the wider school community
special_conditions: Registrations completed via Adelaide Botanic High School website accessible from mid December to mid February. Applicants must sit the ACER Higher Ability Skills Test in Adelaide on Saturday, 28 February 2026. Students must provide a copy of Year 5 Semester 2 school report and a 30-second introduction video as part of the online application. Program offers right of entry to Adelaide Botanic High School for out-of-zone students. Recipients must maintain commitment to senior Mathematics, Science and/or Technology subjects and participate in national competitions and STEM-related extension activities.

overview: Adelaide High School offers Special Language Entry for students who demonstrate strong interest and potential in language learning. The program provides access to Australia's only Special Interest Language School, enabling students to develop world-class communication skills and engage with global communities.
description: The Special Language Entry Program at Adelaide High School provides students with the opportunity to study two of eight languages offered at the school until Year 10, and at least one language through SACE Stage 2. Adelaide High School is South Australia's only Special Interest Language School. Students selected for this program commit to extensive language study and may participate in international study tours. The selection process involves submission of an online application with written responses, school reports and relevant supporting information, and a formal interview. Selection is based on demonstrated interest, motivation, and potential in language learning.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry; Online application required; School reports and supporting information required; Interview required; Commitment to study two languages until Year 10 and at least one until SACE Stage 2
special_conditions: Students must commit to study two languages at Adelaide High School until Year 10 and at least one language until the end of SACE Stage 2 level. Interviews are scheduled for early April 2026.

overview: Adelaide High School's Cricket Program provides talented cricketers from outside the school zone with the opportunity to enrol and develop their skills at the school. The program incorporates a talent identification process and provides access to accredited coaching and professional facilities.
description: The Adelaide High School Cricket Program is designed for talented cricketers who wish to pursue elite cricket development. Students selected through the program commit to prioritizing cricket during their enrolment at Adelaide High School, though they are encouraged to participate in different winter sports. The selection process includes a skill assessment conducted at Adelaide Oval where applicants demonstrate their cricket abilities in batting, bowling, and fielding. Shortlisted students are offered interviews, and successful applicants must commit to the Cricket Program for the duration of their enrolment. Additional fees apply to students enrolled in the Cricket Program. The program is open to both students from outside the school zone and current Adelaide High School students.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry for external applicants; Demonstrated cricket talent and ability; Skill assessment at Adelaide Oval required; Interview required; Current school students may also participate; Outside school zone students eligible
special_conditions: Additional fees apply to students enrolled in the Cricket Program. Cricket involvement takes precedence over other sports, though students are encouraged to participate in different winter sports. Skill assessment held at Adelaide Oval on 31 March 2026. Shortlisted applicants only are invited for interviews.

overview: Adelaide High School's Rowing Program offers talented rowers from outside the school zone the opportunity to enrol and join the school's proud rowing tradition. The program incorporates talent identification selection and provides access to professional rowing facilities and coaching.
description: The Adelaide High School Rowing Program provides students with elite rowing opportunities and access to the school's historic rowing tradition dating back to 1911. The program includes training at the River Torrens boatshed and a shared facility at West Lakes. Students selected through the talent identification process commit to prioritizing rowing during their enrolment, though they are encouraged to participate in different winter sports. The selection process involves a skill assessment at Adelaide High School where students demonstrate their rowing abilities and potential. Shortlisted students are offered interviews, and successful applicants must commit to the Rowing Program. A Come and Try Rowing Program is also available in Term 3 for interested students. The program is open to both students from outside the school zone and current Adelaide High School students.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry for external applicants; Demonstrated rowing talent and ability; Skill assessment required; Interview required; Outside school zone students eligible; Current school students also eligible
special_conditions: Skill assessment held at Adelaide High School on 1 April 2026. Rowing involvement takes precedence over other sports, though students are encouraged to participate in different winter sports. Shortlisted applicants only are invited for interviews. A Come and Try Rowing Program is available in Term 3 for co-curricular participation.

overview: Aberfoyle Park High School's IGNITE program is a Year 7-10 specialist learning program designed for gifted and talented students. The program provides students with an environment to explore and develop their academic gifts while supporting the development of fundamental life skills. As the official Department for Education school for gifted students in the southern metropolitan area of Adelaide, IGNITE serves approximately 50 students per year.
description: The IGNITE Program is a Year 7 to 10 specialist learning program catered to the needs of gifted students. The program provides students with an environment to explore and develop their academic gifts, while supporting the development of fundamental life skills. Unlike independent schools, this is a government school program with no direct financial cost for tuition beyond standard school materials and services fees ($655 in 2025, reduced to $455 with government rebate). Students work on a curriculum designed specifically for gifted learners and are immersed in classes with their intellectual peers. Selection is through an ACER Higher Ability Selection Test (HAST) held annually in February. Students can accelerate at a rate appropriate to their ability and have access to enrichment activities and programs. The program includes a two-year specialist music program and recognition of academic benefits from learning an additional language (Spanish or Japanese). Core curriculum covers English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities with integrated learning across themes. Based on assessment results, further assessment of suitability may be involved.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students regardless of school zone; Year 6 students eligible to sit test for Year 7 entry; Year 7 students eligible for Year 8 entry; Must sit ACER Higher Ability Selection Test; Year 5 students may apply with signed letter of recommendation from primary school principal; Selection based on demonstrated ability in reading comprehension, mathematical reasoning, abstract reasoning, and written expression
special_conditions: Registration for ACER HAST test is conducted through ACER portal. Registrations close Sunday 8 February 2026. ACER registration fee applies (approximately $160 for general applicants, with $135 refund available for School Card holders). Students receive results within nine to ten weeks. The program operates in tandem with standard South Australian Curriculum and SACE requirements. This is a Department for Education funded program providing specialized support for identified gifted and talented students.

overview: All Saints Catholic Primary School offers a lower income fee concession for families that qualify as low income based on government criteria. The school assists families with demonstrated financial need through the State Government's School Card scheme.
description: All Saints provides financial assistance through a lower income fee concession for families who qualify under State Government criteria. To be eligible for the lower income fee, families must qualify as a low income family, determined by the family's gross income and set by the State Government. Proof of income is required and applicants must apply via the State Government's School Card scheme. The Business Manager is available to assist families with questions about applying for the lower income fee and can support families requiring assistance meeting payments.
eligibility_criteria: Family must qualify as low income as determined by gross income threshold set by State Government; Proof of income required; Application via State Government's School Card scheme
special_conditions: Proof of income required. Application must be made through the State Government's School Card scheme. Contact the Business Manager for assistance.

overview: All Saints Catholic Primary School offers sibling discounts for families with multiple children enrolled at the school. This program provides fee reductions for additional siblings.
description: All Saints provides sibling discounts for families with multiple children attending the school. This financial assistance program helps reduce the overall tuition burden for families enrolling more than one child. The Fees & Concessions page indicates that sibling discounts are available as part of the school's concessions policy. Families interested in learning more about the specific discount structure are encouraged to contact the Business Manager.
eligibility_criteria: Multiple siblings enrolled at the school; Enrollment at All Saints Catholic Primary School required for each eligible sibling
special_conditions: Applies to families with multiple children enrolled at All Saints Catholic Primary School.

overview: Antonio Catholic School recognises families experiencing financial hardship and provides support through the School Card Scheme. This government-administered program offers eligible low-income families a significant reduction in tuition costs to ensure all children can access quality Catholic education regardless of financial circumstances.
description: The School Card Scheme is administered by the South Australian Education Department and provides financial assistance to eligible low-income families. Families approved under this scheme receive a 40% discount on tuition fees. The school's staff, School Advisory Board, and Finance Committee are dedicated to ensuring that no child misses out on a quality Catholic education due to financial hardship. Eligible families should contact the school's Finance Officer or Principal to discuss their qualification and begin the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Low-income families; Must be approved by South Australian Education Department; Financial hardship circumstances
special_conditions: Application requires verification through South Australian Education Department. The school encourages eligible families to contact the Finance Officer or Principal for assistance with the application process.

overview: Antonio Catholic School offers sibling discounts to families with multiple children enrolled at the school. This program recognises the financial burden of educating multiple children and provides fee relief to support family affordability.
description: Sibling discounts apply to both fee categories for families with two or more children enrolled at Antonio Catholic School. The discount applies to each additional sibling beyond the first child. The school continues to offer reduced fees as part of its commitment to the Making Catholic Education More Affordable initiative by the South Australian Commission for Catholic Schools, ensuring Catholic education remains within reach for every family. Families should contact the school office on 8325 4500 for detailed information about the specific discount amount and how it applies to their situation.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children enrolled at Antonio Catholic School; Applies to both fee categories
special_conditions: Discount applies per sibling after the first child. The specific discount amount should be discussed with the Finance Officer at the school office on 8325 4500.

school_name: Australian Science and Mathematics School - Tonsley Technical College

overview: The School Card Scheme provides financial assistance to eligible low-income families attending government schools in South Australia. Families who qualify for School Card can receive help with Materials and Services Charges (school fees). The scheme is administered by the Department for Education and must be applied for annually.
description: The School Card Scheme offers financial assistance with school fees (Materials and Services charges) for students attending government schools. The ASMS 2025 Materials and Services Charge is $915. School Card provides fee assistance for families from four years of age receiving full-time schooling. The scheme must be applied for on an annual basis. Eligible families can access support towards educational expenses through this means-tested program administered by the South Australian Department for Education. Applications are completed online and processed on an annual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible low-income families attending government schools; Open to all year levels at ASMS; Must reapply annually; Administered by Department for Education
special_conditions: School Card must be applied for every year. The ASMS understands financial strains families are facing and maintains consistent fee levels. Direct debit payment plans are available weekly, fortnightly, or monthly.

program_name: STEM Scholarships for High School Students
overview: The South Australian Department for Education offers STEM Scholarships to support high school students from under-represented groups in science and mathematics. These scholarships aim to encourage girls, students from low socioeconomic backgrounds, and Aboriginal learners to pursue STEM subjects at SACE Stage 1 and 2 level.
description: Scholarships up to the value of $10,000 are available to support high school students from underrepresented groups (low socioeconomic status, girls and Aboriginal learners) in the study of STEM subjects at SACE Stage 1 and Stage 2 level. The fund covers the cost of tutors, laptops, excursions, or any other strategy that directly supports the student to achieve in their chosen STEM subjects and pathways. Preferred subjects include specialist mathematics, mathematical methods, digital technologies, chemistry, physics, and VET courses in Electrotechnology, Engineering Pathways or IT. Recipients are expected to participate in mentoring programs and other activities that support STEM careers awareness.
eligibility_criteria: High school students from under-represented groups (low socioeconomic status, girls, Aboriginal learners); Open to SACE Stage 1 and Stage 2 students; Students must be eligible for School Card or in financial need (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students do not need to hold School Card); Committed to completing STEM subjects at SACE stage 1 and/or stage 2 level; Willing to participate in mentoring programs and STEM careers awareness activities
special_conditions: Fund covers tutors, laptops, excursions, or strategies supporting STEM achievement. Recipients must be committed to STEM subjects and willing to participate in mentoring programs and STEM careers awareness activities. Government administered program.

overview: Banksia Park International High School offers The Future Academy as a selective entry program for students entering Year 7. The program is designed to develop entrepreneurial skills and mindset through a specialised curriculum pathway. Students are selected based on an application process and commit to follow the entrepreneurial pathway from Years 7 to 10.
description: The Future Academy is a selective entry program available to students currently in Year 6 who will begin high school in 2027. Students from both inside and outside the school zone are eligible to apply, with a commitment to follow the entrepreneurial pathway from Years 7 to 10. In The Future Academy, students engage with design thinking and entrepreneurial mindset to tackle real-world challenges, collaborate with peers, and bring ideas to life. The program offers industry engagement opportunities, guest speakers, excursions, and uses a capability tracker to guide student progress from novice to expert. The program provides no direct monetary financial benefit, but is a specialized curriculum track that replaces one semester of HASS (Humanities and Social Sciences). Selection is through an online application form process. The program is designed to nurture skills and talents to prepare students for future challenges, with particular focus on innovation, entrepreneurship, and community impact.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2027; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Students from both inside and outside school zone eligible; Commitment to follow entrepreneurial pathway from Years 7 to 10; Completion of online application form required
special_conditions: Selective entry process. Students must commit to follow the entrepreneurial pathway from Years 7 to 10. By application only.

overview: Banksia Park International High School offers the Global Citizens Medal to recognise students who value themselves and their contributions to the community. The program encourages students to develop as well-rounded and important citizens who prepare for a future of meaningful impact.
description: The Global Citizens Medal program, introduced in 2003, is intended to encourage students to value themselves and their contributions to the community and to prepare for a future as well-rounded and important citizens. Applicants for the Global Citizens Medal make presentations to a panel about an issue that they feel passionate about. This program has received external recognition including a $10,000 grant in the 2004 National Awards for Quality Schooling with commendation from the Australian Government for the school's achievements in civics and citizenship. The program provides recognition and awards rather than direct monetary scholarships. Selection is through presentation and panel assessment of student passion and community contribution. The program is available to all students throughout their secondary schooling and recognises leadership, community engagement, and citizenship qualities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Willingness to make presentation to panel; Passionate about an issue or cause; Available throughout secondary schooling years
special_conditions: Applicants must make presentations to a panel about an issue they feel passionate about. Award is recognition-based rather than monetary.

overview: Banksia Park International High School offers an Instrumental Music Program providing free lessons to students across Years 7-12. The program offers instruction in a range of instruments and provides opportunities to participate in school ensembles and performances.
description: Banksia Park International High School provides Instrumental Music lessons at no cost to parents and caregivers through the Instrumental Music program. Lessons are provided on a small group basis in Years 7-11, with individual lessons available for Year 12 students undertaking Solo Performance. Instructions are organised by instrument type with limited places available for certain instruments offered at the school. Entry into some ensembles is by audition. Students are individually assessed by personal instrumental music program teachers, with reports provided each semester alongside school reports. Students are expected to attend rehearsals and performances, which occur at least twice per year at school grounds and in the wider community. The program provides substantial value through free instrumental tuition, which would normally cost families hundreds of dollars per year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-12; Limited numbers of places for certain instruments; Audition may be required for some ensembles; Commitment to attend rehearsals and performances
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Lessons at no cost to parents/caregivers. Continuing students given priority for instrumental music lessons. Limited numbers of places for certain instruments. Entry into some ensembles by audition. All students involved in ensembles expected to attend public performances. Individual assessment by instrumental music teachers.

overview: Banksia Park International High School offers a Specialist Soccer Program for students in Years 9-12. The program develops soccer skills, knowledge of the game, fitness, refereeing, coaching and sports administration for students of all abilities and backgrounds.
description: The Specialist Soccer program is available for students from Years 9 to 12 and offers students of all abilities and backgrounds the opportunity to learn about the game of soccer (international football). The program runs in line with the Football Australia (national soccer) curriculum and is inclusive to all students, not just elite players. Banksia Park International High School has ties with Football South Australia (FSA) and national A-League club Adelaide United. These partnerships have enabled students to access FSA-delivered and accredited courses, watch Adelaide United matches, and previously a student from the school won a scholarship to travel with Adelaide United for an Asian Champions League away game in Japan. Adelaide United attend the Banksia Cup and have run training sessions with students. The program includes small-sided games, individual skills, fitness testing, full game principles including tactics and formations. Students learn refereeing and coaching qualifications and sports administration skills. The program provides non-monetary benefits through partnerships and professional development opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 9-12; Open to all abilities and backgrounds; No financial or academic threshold specified; Available to all interested students
special_conditions: Program inclusive to all abilities, not just elite players. Partnerships with Football South Australia and Adelaide United provide additional learning opportunities. Students may have access to professional coaching and match attendance experiences.

school_name: Bowden Brompton Community School - Beach Campus
Bowden Brompton Community School - Little Para Campus
Brinkworth Primary and Early Childhood Centre
Bute Early Learning and Primary School
Central Yorke School - Point Pearce Campus

program_name: South Australia STEM Scholarship for High School Students
overview: Cowell Area School students are eligible to apply for the South Australia STEM Scholarship, which supports high school students from under-represented groups in science and mathematics subjects. The scholarship is designed to help students pursue STEM subjects at SACE Stage 1 and 2 level through financial support for educational resources and mentoring.
description: The South Australia STEM Scholarship for high school students is offered by the Government of South Australia Department for Education. It provides funding up to $10,000 to support students from under-represented groups (particularly low socioeconomic status students, girls, and Aboriginal learners) in pursuing science and mathematics subjects. The fund covers costs including tutors, laptops, excursions, and any other strategy that directly supports the student to achieve in their chosen STEM subjects and career pathways. Students must be committed to completing STEM subjects at SACE Stage 1 and/or Stage 2 level, preferably in specialist mathematics, mathematical methods, digital technologies, chemistry, physics, and/or undertaking VET courses in Electrotechnology, Engineering Pathways or IT. Recipients are required to participate in mentoring programs and STEM career awareness initiatives. The scholarship continues for the duration of SACE study as long as students maintain enrollment and achievement in STEM and/or VET subjects.
eligibility_criteria: High school students attending government schools in South Australia; From under-represented groups including low socioeconomic status, especially girls and Aboriginal learners; Eligible for a school card or in financial need (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students do not need to hold a school card); Committed to completing STEM subjects at SACE Stage 1 and/or Stage 2 level, preferably specialist mathematics, mathematical methods, digital technologies, chemistry, physics and/or undertaking VET courses; Willing to participate in mentoring programs held outside the student's school; Willing to attend STEM scholarship induction with teacher-mentor; Willing to participate in STEM career awareness initiatives including those in the defence industry sector
special_conditions: Scholarship may end if the scholarship recipient stops attending a government school or ceases enrollment and achievement in STEM and/or VET subjects. Recipients must demonstrate ongoing enrolment and achievement in their STEM and/or VET and participation in STEM career awareness initiatives. The scholarship is contingent upon participation in mandatory mentoring programs and maintaining engagement with STEM career pathways.

overview: Concordia College offers Academic Scholarships to prospective and continuing Year 7 to Year 12 students based on ACER testing results. These scholarships recognize exceptional academic achievement and provide partial remission of tuition fees for up to six years, depending on year of entry.
description: The ACER (Australian Cooperative Entry Program) Scholarship Test identifies the highest achieving students within a group of high achievers. The test evaluates performance across three criteria: comprehension and interpretation, written expression, and mathematics. Academic scholarships provide partial remission of tuition fees for up to six years, depending on the year of entry. A limited number of scholarships are awarded to prospective or continuing Year 7 and Year 8 students based on ACER test results. The selection process is based entirely on performance in the ACER testing, which is held on a specified Saturday each year at the Concordia College Chapel in Highgate. New students entering at Years 9-12 may also be eligible to apply based on individual circumstances and outcomes.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective or continuing Year 7 to Year 12 students; Must sit the ACER Scholarship Test; Open to all school sectors; Test based on three criteria: comprehension and interpretation, written expression, and mathematics
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Registration fee of $150 covers the ACER examination fee charged by the Australian Council for Educational Research and incidentals. No refund of entry fee for students unable to sit the examination. ACER reports candidate results to each school and results are available online. Successful applicants are reviewed by the Concordia Scholarship Review Committee.

overview: Concordia College's Year 7-12 Excellence Scholarship recognizes outstanding new and continuing students excelling in academics, performing arts, sports, and/or community contribution. The scholarship provides 50% remission of tuition fees for up to six years, depending on year of entry.
description: This scholarship recognizes students who display exceptional talents across multiple areas including academic achievement, performing arts, sports, and community service. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated excellence in one or more of these areas. The scholarship provides 50% remission of tuition fees for up to six years, depending on the student's year of entry. The application process includes submission of detailed information about achievements and awards in sport, music, debating, leadership, community service and other relevant areas. Shortlisted applicants may be required to attend an interview. Financial circumstances may also be taken into consideration for continuing student applications. Continuing students receiving the scholarship must not be in receipt of any other financial support from Concordia College, and must maintain satisfactory academic progress.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new or continuing students in Years 7 to 12; Must demonstrate excellence in academics, performing arts, sports and/or community contribution; Shortlisted applicants may be interviewed; Continuing students may not receive other financial support from Concordia College
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Shortlisted applicants may be required to attend an interview. All details remain confidential to the Concordia Scholarship Committee. Financial circumstances may be taken into consideration. Continuing student applicants cannot be in receipt of any other financial support from Concordia College.

overview: Concordia College offers Music Scholarships to musically gifted new and continuing Year 7 to Year 12 students. These scholarships provide remission of tuition fees based on ability, with instrumental bursaries available, for up to six years depending on year of entry.
description: Music Scholarships are offered to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability across various instruments and vocal performance. Recipients receive remission of tuition fees based on their level of musical achievement, with instrumental bursaries also available to support instrument purchase or lessons. The scholarships are available for up to six years depending on the student's year of entry at the college. Musical talent is assessed through audition process. Up to six music scholarships are available annually. Instrumental bursaries complement the tuition fee remission to provide comprehensive musical support. The scholarships recognize various levels of musical ability and can provide either partial or full remission of tuition fees depending on the student's demonstrated excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to musically gifted new or continuing students in Years 7 to 12; Audition required; Open to all instruments and vocal performance; Bursaries available for instrumental support
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Subject to audition. Instrumental bursaries are available for students receiving music scholarships. Recipients must demonstrate continued musical excellence. Scholarships provide remission based on ability level demonstrated at audition.

overview: The Senior School Entry Scholarship at Concordia College is available to new students commencing Year 10, recognizing all-rounders with passion in academics, sports, and extra-curricular activities. The scholarship provides up to 50% tuition fee remission for up to three years.
description: This scholarship targets students entering Year 10 who demonstrate excellence across multiple areas including academics, sports, and extra-curricular activities. The program is designed to recognize well-rounded students who have shown commitment and achievement in various aspects of school life. Recipients receive up to 50% tuition fee remission for up to three years (from Year 10 through Year 12 completion). The application process assesses the student's overall profile, including academic performance, sporting achievements, and involvement in school activities and broader community engagement. Successful applicants are selected based on their demonstrated all-round excellence and potential to contribute positively to the Senior School community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students only; Must be commencing Year 10 in 2027; Must demonstrate excellence in academics, sports, and extra-curricular activities; All-rounder profile required
special_conditions: Available only to new students entering Year 10. Scholarship provides support for three years (Year 10-12). Selection based on all-round excellence across academics, sports, and extra-curricular activities.

overview: Through the generous support of the Otto family, Concordia College offers the Senior School Technology Scholarship for new and continuing students in Year 10, 11, or 12 who demonstrate outstanding characteristics in Technology-related subjects. The scholarship provides 25% remission of tuition fees for the duration of senior schooling.
description: This scholarship, sponsored by the Otto family, supports students who display genuine interest and ability in technology-related subjects including Design, Technology & Engineering, Digital Technology, and Media Studies. Recipients must demonstrate not only excellence in their chosen technology subject but also show qualities of involvement and commitment within their school and/or wider community. The scholarship provides 25% remission of tuition fees and is offered for the duration of the student's senior schooling at Concordia for a maximum of three years until completion of Year 12. The successful candidate must continue their technology subject to the end of Year 12, ensuring sustained engagement with their area of specialization.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new or continuing students in Year 10, 11, or 12; Must display outstanding characteristics in a Technology subject (Design, Technology & Engineering; Digital Technology; or Media Studies); Must demonstrate genuine interest and ability in technology subject; Must show involvement and commitment in school and/or wider community; Must continue technology subject to end of Year 12
special_conditions: Sponsored by the Otto family. Recipient must continue technology subject to end of Year 12. Maximum duration is three years until completion of Year 12. Selection considers both academic ability and community involvement.

overview: Concordia College offers Academic Scholarships annually to prospective or continuing Year 7 to Year 12 students based on ACER testing results. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to both new and continuing students in Years 7 to 12, with awards determined by performance on the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER) Scholarship Test. The scholarships provide partial remission of tuition fees for up to six years, depending on year of entry. Selection is based entirely on ACER test results. Scholarships are awarded annually and students must sit the ACER Scholarship Test held in February at the Concordia Campus in Highgate. Shortlisted candidates are notified with scholarship offers announced in March.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective or continuing Year 7 to Year 12 students; Must sit ACER Scholarship Test; Academic performance based on ACER test results; Open to new and continuing students
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: ACER registration fee of $150 covers examination fee and incidentals. No refunds provided for students unable to sit examination. Results available online in March. Scholarships reviewed annually and dependent on satisfactory academic progress.

overview: Concordia College offers the Year 7-12 Excellence Scholarship to recognise new or continuing outstanding students in Years 7 to 12 who are excelling in academics, performing arts, sports and/or community contribution. The scholarship provides 50% remission of tuition fees for up to six years, recognising all-round excellence.
description: The Year 7-12 Excellence Scholarship recognises exceptional students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in one or more areas: academics (consistently high performance in standardised tests and school assessments), performing arts (music, drama, dance), sports (regional, state or national level competition), and community contribution (volunteer work and leadership). Successful candidates must provide evidence of commitment and achievement in their chosen areas through school reports, certificates, and a written statement. The scholarship provides 50% remission of tuition fees and is awarded for up to six years depending on year of entry. Scholarships are subject to annual review based on satisfactory academic progress, continued contribution to the College, and conduct of high standard.
eligibility_criteria: New or continuing students in Year 7 to Year 12; Exceptional talent and commitment in academic, performing arts, sports, or community contribution areas; Consistently high academic performance or strong standardised test results for academic category; Outstanding achievements in chosen discipline; Financial circumstances may be considered; Current students must not receive other financial support from Concordia
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications made online at www.concordia.sa.edu.au/enrolment/scholarships. Shortlisted applicants may be required to attend interview with Head of College, Principal or School Leader. Scholarships reviewed annually and dependent on satisfactory academic progress, contribution to College, and conduct. Student will not be in receipt of any other financial support from Concordia College for current student applications only.

overview: Concordia College offers Music Scholarships to musically gifted new or continuing Year 7 to 12 students. The scholarships provide remission of tuition fees based on ability, with instrumental bursaries available, for up to six years depending on year of entry.
description: Music Scholarships are designed for students who demonstrate outstanding ability or potential on their chosen instrument(s). Applicants must demonstrate sound application to academic studies and positive involvement in music groups and activities. The scholarship provides tuition fee remission based on demonstrated musical ability, with additional instrumental bursaries available to support musical development. Awards are available for up to six years depending on year of entry. Selection is based on audition held in February at the Music Centre at Concordia Campus, with candidates required to prepare two contrasting pieces. Students must provide school reports, copies of music certificates, and a written statement of achievements in music (maximum 200 words). Scholarships are reviewed annually and dependent on satisfactory progress reports, conduct, and commitment to the co-curricular music program.
eligibility_criteria: New or continuing Year 7 to 12 students; Outstanding ability or potential on chosen instrument(s); Sound application to academic studies; Positive involvement in music groups and activities; Must not be in receipt of any other financial support from Concordia College; Subject to audition in February
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications made online at www.concordia.sa.edu.au/enrolment/scholarships. Auditions held in Music Centre at Concordia Campus during school hours in February. Students must provide accompanist. Parents encouraged to attend audition. Shortlisted candidates invited to audition. Scholarships reviewed annually dependent on satisfactory progress reports, conduct, and commitment to music program.

overview: The Senior School Entry Scholarship is available to new students commencing Year 10 in 2027, recognising all-rounders with a passion in academics, sports and extra-curricular activities. The scholarship provides up to 50% tuition fee remission for up to three years.
description: The Senior School Entry Scholarship recognises all-rounder students entering Year 10 who demonstrate sound application to academic studies, active involvement in co-curricular activities (including sport, debating, music, and community service), and outstanding personal qualities. Successful applicants will provide evidence of past achievements and future goals through a written statement of up to 400 words, copies of two most recent school reports, NAPLAN reports, and awards or certificates of achievement. Selection is based on academic application, involvement in co-curricular activities, and personal qualities. The scholarship provides up to 50% tuition fee remission and is awarded for up to three years. Shortlisted applicants may be required to attend an interview with the Principal or Senior School Leader. Results are announced from February the year prior to commencement.
eligibility_criteria: New students commencing Year 10 in 2027; Sound application to academic studies; Active involvement in co-curricular activities (sport, debating, music, community service); Outstanding personal qualities; Must demonstrate past achievements and future goals; Interview may be required for shortlisted candidates
special_conditions: Applications made online. Requires copies of two most recent school reports, NAPLAN reports, written statement (maximum 400 words), and awards/certificates. Shortlisted applicants may be required to attend interview with Principal or Senior School Leader. Results announced from February prior to commencement. All details remain confidential to Scholarship Committee.

overview: Through the generous support of the Otto family, Concordia College offers a Senior School Technology Scholarship for new or continuing students in Year 10, 11 or 12 who display outstanding characteristics in technology-related subjects. The scholarship provides 25% remission of tuition fees.
description: The Otto Senior School Technology Scholarship recognises students in Years 10-12 who display outstanding ability and genuine interest in technology-related subjects including Design, Technology & Engineering, Digital Technology, or Media Studies. Successful applicants will demonstrate sound academic application, involvement and commitment within their school and/or wider community, and must commit to continuing a technology subject through to the end of Year 12. The scholarship provides 25% remission of tuition fees and is awarded for the duration of the student's senior schooling at Concordia for a maximum of three years until completion of Year 12. Applicants must meet the eligibility criteria and demonstrate both academic ability and community involvement related to technology interests.
eligibility_criteria: New or continuing students in Year 10, 11, or 12 in 2027; Outstanding characteristics in technology-related subjects (Design, Technology & Engineering, Digital Technology, Media Studies); Sound academic application; Involvement and commitment within school and/or wider community; Must continue a technology subject to end of Year 12; Genuine interest and demonstrated ability in technology subjects
special_conditions: Successful candidate must continue a technology subject to the end of Year 12. Scholarship provided for duration of senior schooling for maximum of three years until completion of Year 12. Offered through generous support of Otto family.

overview: Dominican School offers fee remissions to families experiencing financial hardship. The school believes that a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing a Catholic education.
description: Dominican School provides fee remissions and financial assistance programs to support families who require help with school fees. The program is designed to ensure that no family is prevented from enrolling their child at the school due to financial constraints. Families experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to contact the Principal, Angela Scodella, or Finance Officer, Jo Freer to confidentially discuss additional support through fee remissions. The school offers flexible payment plans as well to assist families with their school fees obligations.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Open to all year levels at the school
special_conditions: Families must contact the Principal or Finance Officer to confidentially discuss their financial situation and arrange fee remissions. The school operates on a transparent, case-by-case basis to determine appropriate support.

overview: Dara School maintains a Scholarship Fund to support gifted students from diverse backgrounds. The fund aims to ensure that intellectually gifted students have access to quality education at Dara School, breaking down financial barriers and promoting inclusivity among exceptional young minds.
description: Dara School, as a registered charity, invites donations to support the Scholarship Fund which helps ensure that gifted students from diverse backgrounds have access to quality education. The fund specifically aims to break down financial barriers and promote inclusivity for gifted learners. The exact financial benefits, selection criteria, and award amounts are not publicly detailed on the school's website. Interested families are encouraged to contact the school directly for information about scholarship opportunities and availability. Donations to the Scholarship Fund are tax-deductible at $2 or more.
eligibility_criteria: Open to intellectually gifted students; must demonstrate advanced intellectual ability or potential; selection based on school's assessment of giftedness
special_conditions: School notes that rebates and discounts apply to tuition fees only. No student may receive more than one rebate simultaneously; where multiple rebates apply, the highest proportionate rebate or discount will apply to the account. Contact the school directly for current scholarship availability and application processes.

Eastern Fleurieu R-12 School - Strathalbyn R-6 Campus

overview: Emmaus Christian College offers the ACER Scholarship through the Australian Co-operative Entry Scholarship Program (ACEP) to recognise academic excellence in incoming Year 7 students. The scholarship is administered by ACER and is open to both new and continuing students.
description: The ACER Scholarship is administered through the Australian Co-operative Entry Scholarship Program (ACEP) and provides recognition for academic merit at Year 7 entry. Applicants must sit the ACEP examination with the specific date to be confirmed by the school. A non-refundable examination fee of $110 (inc. GST) is required to sit the test. New students will be asked to provide copies of recent school reports for reference when scholarships are being considered. The financial value of the scholarship is not specified on available sources.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new or continuing students entering Year 7 at Emmaus Christian College; Must sit the ACEP examination; Recent school reports required for new students
special_conditions: Examination date to be confirmed. New students must provide copies of recent school reports for reference consideration.

overview: Emmaus Catholic School offers music scholarships each year focusing on wind instruments. The scholarships enable committed students to learn a musical instrument at the school with associated fees being catered for and a remission on their school fees.
description: These music scholarships are offered annually to students demonstrating commitment to music learning at Emmaus Catholic School. The scholarships cover associated instrumental fees and provide a remission on school fees for up to four years of schooling. Students must participate in the ensemble program and attend instrumental lessons as conditions of the scholarship. Scholarships were previously available for students in Years 2-5, though specific current year eligibility should be confirmed with the school directly. Prior experience on wind instruments is not required. The school is recognised as the school of choice for music in the southern region and offers a comprehensive music curriculum and instrumental program.
eligibility_criteria: Students interested in learning wind instruments; Commitment to ensemble program participation; Willingness to attend instrumental lessons; No prior experience required; Ability to commit to full duration of scholarship (up to four years)
special_conditions: Scholarship is conditional upon enrolment in the ensemble program and participation in instrumental lessons. Students must maintain satisfactory progress in their musical development. Scholarships are available for up to four years of schooling.

overview: Emmaus Catholic School participates in South Australia's School Card scheme, providing lower income fee assistance to eligible families. The school believes that financial circumstances should not be a barrier to a Catholic education.
description: Emmaus Catholic School supports families experiencing financial difficulty through participation in the South Australian School Card scheme. Families with a gross annual income below approximately $74,189 (for a family with one school-age child in 2025) may qualify for reduced school fees. Proof of income is required, and applications are made through the State Government's School Card scheme. The school's finance manager is available to assist families with questions about eligibility and application processes. For families not eligible for School Card but facing financial hardship, the school encourages direct discussion with administration to explore other support options.
eligibility_criteria: Family gross income below approximately $74,189 for one school-age child (amount varies based on number of dependent children); Proof of income required; Application via State Government School Card scheme; Families facing financial difficulty outside income limits may discuss alternative support with the school
special_conditions: Income limits are set by the State Government and vary annually. For 2025, the indicative limit was approximately $74,189 for a single school-age child. Families are encouraged to contact the school's finance manager if they have concerns about paying school fees.

overview: Encounter Lutheran College offers the Principal's Scholarship to recognise Indigenous Australian students who demonstrate the motivation and capacity to complete Year 12. This scholarship honours the college's commitment to supporting Ngarrindjeri culture and education, and celebrates the historical connection between the college and the Ramindjeri people.
description: The Principal's Scholarship is available to Indigenous Australian applicants who have the motivation and capacity to complete Year 12. Applicants must be enrolled at Encounter Lutheran College to be eligible. New enrolments to the college in Term 4 are welcome to apply for a scholarship and award will be at the discretion of the Principal. Interested students should download the 2026 Principal's Scholarship Application Form from the college website. The specific financial value of the scholarship is not publicly detailed on the scholarships page, but applicants are encouraged to contact the Enrolment Office for further information regarding the value and any other details. The scholarship supports Indigenous students throughout their secondary education pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Indigenous Australian; Must have the motivation and capacity to complete Year 12; Must be enrolled at Encounter Lutheran College; New enrolments in Term 4 may apply at the discretion of the Principal
special_conditions: Applicants must download and submit the 2026 Principal's Scholarship Application Form. New enrolments to the college in Term 4 are welcome to apply for a scholarship, with award being at the discretion of the Principal.

overview: Encounter Lutheran College offers the Meyer Scholarship as its way of honouring Pastor H.A. Eduard Meyer's commitment to Ngarrindjeri culture and education. The scholarship celebrates the legacy of Meyer's work with the Ramindjeri people and recognises the college's historic connection to the region and the importance of cultural preservation and reconciliation.
description: Encounter Lutheran College offers the Meyer Scholarship to honour the remarkable legacy of Pastor H.A. Eduard Meyer, a Lutheran Missionary and linguist who was the first non-Aboriginal person to master, record and translate the language of the Ramindjeri People of Encounter Bay and Port Elliot into English. From 1840 to 1848, Meyer and his wife Friederike lived and worked with the Ramindjeri people, opening the first school in Victor Harbor in 1844. Meyer translated the commandments and delivered sermons in the local Ramindjeri language, publishing a grammar and wordlist that left a considerable linguistic legacy for the Ramindjeri and Ngarrindjeri people. To apply for the Meyer Scholarship, interested students should contact the Enrolment Office for further details regarding eligibility criteria, application procedures, and financial value. The scholarship reflects the college's commitment to Indigenous reconciliation and cultural respect.
eligibility_criteria: Interest in or connection to Ngarrindjeri culture; Contact with Enrolment Office required for application details and eligibility criteria
special_conditions: Applicants must contact the Enrolment Office for further details regarding the Meyer Scholarship application process, eligibility criteria, and financial value.

school_name: Emmaus Christian College -  Brooklyn Park Campus
Faith Lutheran College - Tanunda Lutheran Campus

overview: Endeavour College offers the Academic Scholarship to Year 9 students entering Year 10 who demonstrate exceptional academic talent. The scholarship recognises outstanding achievement and seeks to support and promote talented young people to fully achieve their God-given gifts and abilities.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to Year 9 students entering into Year 10 and covers 25% to 50% of tuition fees. The scholarship is open to both internal applicants (already enrolled at Endeavour College) and external students (at other schools). Applicants must sit the ACER Scholarship Examination at Endeavour College on the set examination date (17 May 2025). A limited number of scholarships will be awarded based on examination results, portfolio review and interview, at the sole discretion of the Principal. All scholarship recipients must actively participate in a minimum of two (2) co-curricular activities offered within the College. Scholarships are reviewed each semester and subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies. If a student transfers to another school before the end of Year 12, parents will be required to repay the scholarship percentage received from commencement to the conclusion of enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Year 9 students who are Australian citizens or Permanent Residents; Must be entering Year 10; Open to internal applicants (currently enrolled at Endeavour) or external students (at other schools); Must sit the ACER Scholarship Examination at Endeavour College on the set date; Scholarships form a confidential arrangement and must not be discussed with other parties
special_conditions: Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not apply to levies or other costs such as uniforms. A scholarship awarded by Endeavour College forms a confidential arrangement between the College and the family and must not be discussed with other parties. If confidentiality is broken, the scholarship may be revoked. If the student transfers to another school before the end of Year 12, parents will be required to repay the scholarship percentage received from commencement to the conclusion of the student's enrolment. In addition to scholarship requirements, all scholarship recipients are expected to actively participate in a minimum of two (2) co-curricular activities offered within the College. Recipients must maintain exemplary standard of uniform and personal presentation, be courteous and respectful to all members of the College community, consistently maintain exemplary homework, work ethic and grades, and be an active participant in College activities as determined by key College personnel.

Good Shepherd Lutheran School - Para Vista

overview: Investigator College's highest recognition of personal excellence. The Spirit of Investigator Scholarship is provided to students who embody dedication to learning, engagement in co-curricular or community activity, and commitment to academic excellence.
description: The Spirit of Investigator Scholarship is available to applicants entering Year 7 or above in 2026 and offers a 50% discount on school fees for the remainder of schooling, representing a potential saving of over $20,000. Selection is based on academic achievement, co-curricular achievement/community involvement, and effort and attitude. A maximum of one scholarship is awarded annually. Recipients must maintain a minimum standard in all three key areas, maintain full-time enrolment, maintain an impeccable behaviour and attendance record, and adhere to College rules and guidelines. The application process involves online submission via the College portal, followed by Senior Leadership Team review and interviews with selected applicants, with final selection announced before scholarship is officially awarded.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 to Year 12 in 2026; Applications exclusively open to students who do not currently attend Investigator College; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must show community and co-curricular engagement; Must demonstrate commitment and positive attitude
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are not granted unconditionally and are regularly reviewed. The recipient is expected to maintain a minimum standard in academic achievement, co-curricular achievement/community involvement, and effort and attitude. Scholarship Agreement must be signed by all parties before official award. Recipients must maintain impeccable behaviour and attendance record and adhere to College rules and guidelines.

overview: Investigator College's General Excellence Scholarships recognise students who display academic excellence, a hard-working attitude and co-curricular/community engagement. These scholarships motivate high-achievers to continue pushing themselves and role modelling exceptional standards within the College community.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is open to applicants entering Year 4 or above in 2026 and offers a 30% discount on tuition fees for up to three years. Applications are open to both current and new students, with students entering Year 4 to Year 12 eligible to apply. Current scholarship recipients can only apply if their scholarship lapses in 2026, as multiple scholarships cannot be held concurrently. Selection is based on academic achievement (recent academic results, NAPLAN data, school reports), participation beyond the classroom (co-curricular engagement and voluntary service), and drive and work ethic. The selection panel assesses motivation, perseverance, and a proactive mindset in both academic and co-curricular contexts. Applications close and are reviewed by the Senior Leadership Team, with shortlisted applicants invited for interviews.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4 or above in 2026; Open to current and new students; Students entering Year 4 to Year 12 in 2026 are eligible to apply; Current scholarship recipients can only apply if their scholarship lapses in 2026; Must demonstrate academic achievement; Must show participation in co-curricular activities or voluntary service; Must demonstrate drive and work ethic
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are not granted unconditionally and are regularly reviewed. Applications for 2026 start are now closed. Consideration is also given to candidates with demonstrated financial needs who would not otherwise be able to attend. Scholarship duration is up to three years. Recipients must maintain minimum standards in academic achievement, co-curricular achievement/community involvement, and effort and attitude.

overview: Investigator College's Sports and Co-Curricular Scholarships recognise both new and current students who demonstrate outstanding effort or achievement in sport, athletics or other extra-curricular activities. The College is committed to supporting the physical, social, emotional, psychological and spiritual needs of each learner.
description: The Sports and Co-Curricular Scholarship is available to both new and current students as they commence or continue their education through the Middle and Senior Schools (Year 7–Year 12). Recipients are recognised for their exceptional effort or achievement in sports, athletics or other extra-curricular activities. The scholarship entitles the recipient to a special remission on annual tuition fees, up to a maximum of 20%. Selection criteria include evidence of consistent effort in academic studies; evidence of outstanding achievement or success in one or more sports, athletic events or extra-curricular activities over the past two years; and a commitment to continuing to pursue opportunities to compete and achieve in their nominated sport or activity in the future. Applicants must provide a personal reference or recommendation from a coach, teacher, school leader, or community representative.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both new and current students; Students entering Year 7 to Year 12; Must demonstrate consistent effort in academic studies; Must show outstanding achievement or success in sports, athletics or extra-curricular activities over past two years; Must demonstrate commitment to continuing participation in nominated sport or activity
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications for 2024 school year are now closed. Applicants must attach a personal reference or recommendation from a coach, teacher, school leader, or relevant community representative to support their application.

overview: Investigator College values creativity and encourages the development of skills, talents and abilities in artistic disciplines including music, dance and theatre. The Performing Arts Scholarship recognises and rewards student effort in music, dance, theatre or dramatic art forms, aiming to encourage the continual growth of the Performing Arts program.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship is available to both new and current students as they commence or continue their education through the Middle and Senior Schools (Year 7–Year 12). Recipients are recognised for their exceptional effort, talent or achievement in music, dance, acting or theatrical arts. The scholarship entitles the recipient to a special remission on annual tuition fees, up to a maximum of 20%. Selection criteria include evidence of consistent effort in academic studies; evidence of outstanding achievement, talent or success in one or more performing arts disciplines over the past two years; and a commitment to continuing to pursue opportunities to compete and achieve in their nominated performing art(s) in the future. Applicants must provide a personal reference or recommendation from a coach, teacher, school leader, or community representative to support their application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both new and current students; Students entering Year 7 to Year 12; Must demonstrate consistent effort in academic studies; Must show outstanding achievement, talent or success in performing arts disciplines (music, dance, acting, theatrical arts) over past two years; Must demonstrate commitment to continuing participation in nominated performing art
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications for 2024 school year are now closed. Applicants must attach a personal reference or recommendation from a coach, teacher, school leader, or relevant community representative to support their application.

overview: Available to current students beginning Year 5 or Year 6 at Investigator College, the Spirit of Investigator Scholarship recognises exceptional students who embody the best of the College's aspirational values. Just as Matthew Flinders launched his expedition to circumnavigate Australia in 1802, these scholarships recognise exceptional students as they embark on the next phase of their educational journey and prepare to join the Middle School.
description: The Spirit of Investigator Scholarship is available to current students as they begin Year 5 or Year 6 at Investigator College. Recipients are recognised for their ability to shine in a given area, their willingness to consistently apply themselves in different contexts across the Investigator College Community, and their willingness to explore new skills and experiences. The scholarship entitles the recipient to a special remission on annual tuition fees, up to a maximum of 20%. Selection criteria include evidence of excellence or exemplary effort in academic studies across two or more subject areas, and evidence of strong participation in co-curricular and community activities. Applicants must provide a personal reference or recommendation from a coach, teacher, school leader, or community representative. Applications were made through the online portal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current students; Students beginning Year 5 or Year 6; Must demonstrate excellence or exemplary effort in academic studies across two or more subject areas; Must show strong participation in co-curricular and community activities
special_conditions: Applications for 2024 school year are now closed. Applicants must attach a personal reference or recommendation from a coach, teacher, school leader, or relevant community representative to support their application.

overview: IQRA College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability in their annual performance. The award is open to students from Year 3 to Year 11.
description: The IQRA College Academic Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students who attain the highest total academic score across all subjects during the schooling year. Recipients of the scholarship receive tuition fee reduction for the following school year. The scholarship recognises sustained academic excellence and achievement across the curriculum. Selection is based on comprehensive academic performance during the schooling year. The financial benefit includes fee reduction that varies based on the overall academic achievement level. Scholarship recipients are announced at the end of each school year with benefits commencing in the following year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 3 to Year 11; Must attain the highest total academic score in the schooling year; Current and new students eligible; Scholarship awarded for the following school year
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded for the following school year to the student who attains the highest total score in the schooling year. Two scholarships available for current students and one for new and returning students.

overview: IQRA College offers the Quran Scholarship to recognise and support students demonstrating excellence in Quranic Studies. The scholarship is available for students from Year 3 to Year 11 who show exceptional commitment to Quranic learning and memorisation. This program reflects the college's Islamic values and commitment to supporting students in their spiritual development.
description: The IQRA College Quran Scholarship recognises outstanding achievement and commitment to Quranic Studies. This scholarship is open to students from Year 3 to Year 11 who demonstrate exceptional ability and dedication in Quranic learning. Recipients receive tuition fee reduction as recognition of their achievement. The scholarship selection is based on academic performance in Quranic Studies and demonstrated commitment to Quranic memorisation or excellence in this subject area. The college offers dedicated Quranic Studies and Arabic programs as specialist subjects. The financial benefit provides partial fee reduction for successful applicants in the following school year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 3 to Year 11; Must demonstrate exceptional achievement in Quranic Studies; Commitment to Quranic memorisation or excellence required; Open to both current and new students; Scholarship awarded for the following school year
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship awarded to students demonstrating exceptional achievement in Quranic Studies. School offers dedicated Quranic Studies and Arabic programs as specialist subjects.

overview: IQRA College offers the Sports Scholarship to recognise and support students demonstrating excellence in sporting achievement. The scholarship is available for students from Year 3 to Year 11 who show exceptional athletic ability and commitment to their chosen sport. This program supports talented young athletes in balancing their academic and sporting pursuits.
description: The IQRA College Sports Scholarship recognises outstanding sporting achievement and excellence. This scholarship is open to students from Year 3 to Year 11 who demonstrate exceptional ability in sports and athletics. Recipients receive tuition fee reduction as recognition of their sporting achievement. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence at school or external level, athletic ability, and commitment to sport. The scholarship provides partial fee reduction for successful applicants in the following school year. Students must maintain their eligibility through continued sporting participation and satisfactory academic performance. The school supports participation in various sports programs as part of co-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 3 to Year 11; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting achievement; Athletic ability and commitment to sport required; Open to both current and new students; Scholarship awarded for the following school year
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Scholarship awarded to students demonstrating exceptional sporting achievement. Recipients must maintain eligibility through continued sporting participation and satisfactory academic performance.

overview: IQRA College offers the Mathematics Scholarship to recognise and reward students achieving the highest standard in Mathematics. The scholarship is open to students from Year 3 to Year 11 who demonstrate exceptional mathematical ability. Recipients receive tuition fee reduction as recognition of their mathematical excellence.
description: The IQRA College Mathematics Scholarship is awarded to students between Year 3 and Year 11 who attain the highest score in Mathematics for the schooling year. The scholarship recognises exceptional mathematical achievement and proficiency. Recipients receive tuition fee reduction for the following school year as recognition of their outstanding performance. Selection is based on annual Mathematics assessment and performance across the year. The scholarship reward reflects the school's commitment to supporting academic excellence in key curriculum areas. Students are assessed on comprehensive Mathematics performance throughout the academic year. The specific financial benefit in the form of fee reduction is awarded to the high achiever for the following school year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 3 to Year 11; Must attain the highest total score in Mathematics for the schooling year; Selection based on annual Mathematics performance; Scholarship awarded for the following school year
year_levels: Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Awarded to the student between Year 3-11 who attains the highest score in Mathematics for the schooling year. Scholarship benefit applies to the following school year.

Kangaroo Island Community Education - Parndana Campus

overview: Kadina Memorial School awards the Southwood Medal to the Year 12 student who achieves the highest academic score at the end of their schooling. The medal recognises outstanding academic excellence and achievement in the senior year.
description: The Southwood Medal is awarded annually to the Year 12 student who achieves the highest overall academic performance. The award was initiated in 1924 by Albert Ray Southwood, a distinguished scholar of Wallaroo Mines District School, to recognise exceptional achievement. The medal is a prestigious honour presented to the dux of Year 12. Recipients are typically recognised during school assemblies and in school publications. The award acknowledges academic excellence across the entire secondary schooling journey and represents a tradition of more than a century of recognition at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Highest overall academic score in Year 12; Enrolled full-time at Kadina Memorial School
special_conditions: Award is given to the highest scoring student in Year 12 based on final academic results. The medal is presented annually by representatives of the Southwood family, maintaining a century-long tradition of support.

overview: Kadina Memorial School recognises the Year 12 student who achieves the second-highest academic score with Runner-Up Dux status. This award acknowledges outstanding academic achievement among senior students.
description: The Runner-Up Dux award is presented to the Year 12 student achieving the second-highest overall academic score. This recognition is awarded alongside the Southwood Medal dux award and acknowledges students who have demonstrated outstanding academic performance throughout their secondary education. The award is presented during school ceremonies and recognition events.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Second-highest overall academic score in Year 12; Enrolled full-time at Kadina Memorial School
special_conditions: Award is based on final academic results and overall performance in Year 12.

overview: Kadina Memorial School awards dux recognition to outstanding students in Years 3-6 of the primary years sub-school. The awards recognise academic excellence in the primary years.
description: Dux awards are presented to recognised high-achieving students in Years 3, 4, 5, and 6.
