These awards acknowledge students who have demonstrated exceptional academic performance and achievement throughout their primary years at the school. The awards are presented during school assemblies and published in school newsletters. The recognition programme encourages students to aspire to academic excellence while celebrating their achievements within their year level.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Years 3-6 at Kadina Memorial School; High academic performance; Full-time enrolment
special_conditions: Awards are determined by school assessment of academic performance. Typically presented at end of year or during recognition ceremonies.

program_name: Governor of South Australia Commendation Award
overview: Kadina Memorial School students are eligible for nomination for the Governor of South Australia Commendation Award, recognising outstanding achievement in Year 12 and leadership. This state-level award honours students who demonstrate exceptional academic and personal excellence.
description: The Governor of South Australia Commendation Award is a prestigious state-level recognition presented to selected Year 12 students who have demonstrated outstanding academic achievement, often including merit-level work in specialised subjects and exceptional overall performance. Students are nominated by their school based on excellence across academic pursuits and potentially community contribution. The award is conferred by the Governor of South Australia and represents recognition at the highest state level. Recipients are typically students who have achieved high ATARs, subject merits, and demonstrated leadership qualities.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Outstanding academic achievement; ATAR score over 90 or subject merits; Demonstrated leadership or community contribution; Nomination by school
special_conditions: Students are nominated by Kadina Memorial School based on their academic achievement and contributions. Award is conferred by the Governor of South Australia.

overview: Kapunda High School offers the Jim Bettison Scholarship to support former students pursuing tertiary studies or recognised vocational training. The scholarship recognises the aspirations of students who plan to continue their education and training after completing secondary school.
description: The Jim Bettison Scholarship is worth $8,000, payable over four years at $2,000 per annum. The scholarship is contingent upon award requirements being met and satisfactory completion each year of a chosen course or training placement. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic and conduct standards throughout their tertiary studies or training. Applicants are required to complete a formal application and undertake an interview with the Selection Panel. An information session for Year 12 students occurs early Term 3, and applications are submitted online via the Country Education Foundation of Australia website by the end of September prior to the year of planned study or trade training placement.
eligibility_criteria: Former students of Kapunda High School who attended the school for at least the last two years of their secondary education; Aged under 21 years as at 1 January of the year for which applying; Intending to undertake tertiary studies or recognised vocational training; Must complete formal application and interview
special_conditions: Payable over four years at $2,000 per annum. Contingent upon award requirements being met and satisfactory completion each year of a chosen course or training placement. Recipients may also be eligible to receive additional financial assistance through the Country Education Foundation of Australia Education Partnerships with individual universities through a Matching Grants arrangement. Recipients must notify the Kapunda High School Centenary Education Foundation if they receive any other grant/scholarship/funding from another source. Recipients must agree to assist with and attend local foundation fundraising and promotional events if available at the request of the foundation. Recipients must report to the foundation every six months on how their goal is progressing, how the grant has assisted them and their marks/grades for the semester if applicable.

overview: Kapunda High School offers Country Education Foundation Grants to support current senior students planning tertiary studies or vocational training. The grants provide financial assistance for students who outline specific educational and training goals and areas where they require help to achieve these aspirations.
description: The Country Education Foundation Grants program currently supports between 10 to 15 students annually. Grants provide financial assistance to current senior students who submit an application outlining their educational and training goals and a specific area where they may require help to achieve these goals. The financial value varies depending on individual circumstances and available funding. Recipients and scholarship winners may also be eligible to receive additional financial assistance through the Country Education Foundation of Australia Education Partnerships with individual universities through a Matching Grants arrangement. Applicants are required to complete a formal application and undertake an interview with the Selection Panel. Applications are submitted online via the Country Education Foundation of Australia website by the end of September prior to the year of planned study.
eligibility_criteria: Current senior students of Kapunda High School; Planning tertiary studies or recognised vocational training; Must complete formal application and interview; Must outline specific educational and training goals
special_conditions: Grants provide reimbursement for pre-agreed expenses or payments to goods and services providers directly. Recipients must notify the Kapunda High School Centenary Education Foundation if they receive any other grant/scholarship/funding from another source. Recipients must agree to assist with and attend local foundation fundraising and promotional events if available at the request of the foundation. Recipients must report to the foundation every six months on how their goal is progressing, how the grant has assisted them and their marks/grades for the semester if applicable. Funds support diverse education and career pursuits including apprenticeships, traineeships, cadetships, degrees, diplomas and certificate level studies.

school_name: Kilparrin Teaching and Assessment School and Services
King's Baptist Grammar School - Wynn Vale Campus

program_name: The Smith Family Learning for Life Scholarship
overview: Kilkenny Primary School partners with The Smith Family to offer the Learning for Life Scholarship to students from disadvantaged backgrounds. The scholarship provides financial assistance to help families pay for educational essentials including uniforms, textbooks, excursions and other school-related costs. Students receive ongoing support from a Family Partnerships Coordinator throughout their education.
description: The Smith Family Learning for Life Scholarship is a comprehensive educational support program that provides financial assistance to families experiencing disadvantage. Families who meet eligibility criteria receive payments to help cover school essentials such as uniforms, stationery, textbooks, shoes, school camps and excursions. The scholarship includes support from a Family Partnerships Coordinator who works with families to address barriers to education. Students can receive support throughout their entire educational journey from primary school through to tertiary education. The program requires families to demonstrate a strong commitment to education through minimum 90% school attendance and maintain partnership agreements with The Smith Family regarding appropriate use of funds. Financial support is available per student per year, with typical awards ranging from approximately $478 annually.
eligibility_criteria: Families must hold a current Pension Concession Card or Health Care Card; Demonstrate a strong commitment to education (minimum 90% school attendance); Referred by the school to The Smith Family; Student must attend Kilkenny Primary School; Family must live in a community where The Smith Family has a presence
special_conditions: School referral required - families cannot apply directly; must be referred by Kilkenny Primary School. Families must enter into a partnership agreement with The Smith Family and demonstrate commitment to using funds for educational expenses only. Receipt of educational expenses must be kept and provided as requested. Partnership paperwork including student profiles and attendance reports must be returned regularly. Students on the program will be supported throughout their entire education journey. Once enrolled, support continues even if the family situation changes.

school_name: King's Baptist Grammar School - Mount Barker Campus

overview: Kildare College offers the Delany Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7. The scholarship provides fee remission based on excellent performance in the ACER Scholarship test, supporting the most academically talented students.
description: The Delany Academic Scholarship offers 50%-100% fee remission based on academic excellence as demonstrated through performance on the ACER Scholarship test. The scholarship is tenable for six years, covering the student's full secondary education at Kildare College. The scholarship is reviewed on a yearly basis based on academic performance, ensuring recipients maintain high standards throughout their enrolment. Selection is determined by performance on the ACER assessment administered by the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently in Year 6 or equivalent; Selection based on ACER Scholarship test performance demonstrating academic excellence
value_aud: 50% Fee Reduction or Full Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarship is tenable for six years. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic performance, which is reviewed annually. Scholarship is subject to annual review based on academic progress.

overview: Kildare College offers the St Brigid Performing Arts Scholarship to recognise excellence and potential in the performing arts. The scholarship provides substantial fee remission for students enrolling in Year 7 who demonstrate significant talent in music, drama, or dance.
description: The St Brigid Performing Arts Scholarship provides 50%-100% remission of tuition fees for students enrolling in Year 7, for the entirety of their secondary education at Kildare College. The scholarship is available to students demonstrating excellence in performing arts disciplines including music, drama, and dance. Scholarship continuation is reviewed annually, with recipients required to maintain engagement in their performing arts pursuits. Selection is based on audition or portfolio assessment of performing arts ability.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated excellence or significant talent in performing arts (music, drama, or dance); Must maintain participation in performing arts activities at the College
value_aud: 50% Fee Reduction or Full Fee Remission
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 are now closed. Scholarship continuation is reviewed annually. Recipients must continue to engage with performing arts programs at the College.

overview: Kildare College offers the Brigidine Education Relief Fund Bursary to support students experiencing financial hardship. The bursary assists families with education costs including uniforms and tuition fees, recognising the importance of accessible Catholic education.
description: The Brigidine Education Relief Fund Bursary provides financial assistance of up to $1,500 annually to support the cost of uniforms and/or part of the tuition and resource fees. The bursary is considered on the recommendation of the Principal to the Kildare Education Ministries Board based on evidence of financial hardship. Eligible circumstances include evidence of low family income (supported by healthcare card or social security), refugee or immigrant families, families experiencing disabilities, and families in temporary hardship or experiencing family breakdown. The bursary supports multiple categories of hardship to ensure broad access to education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 2026; Evidence of low family income supported by healthcare card or other social security entitlements; Refugee or immigrant families; Students independently supporting themselves; Families with disabilities; Families experiencing temporary or permanent hardship; Families experiencing family breakdown with consequent loss of income or relocation expenses
special_conditions: Bursary is awarded on recommendation of the Principal to the Kildare Education Ministries Board. Individual schools will continue to provide fee relief as negotiated with individual families in accordance with current practice. The bursary subsidises up to the total value of $1,500 for uniform and/or part of tuition and resource fees.

program_name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship
overview: Kildare College offers a scholarship specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises the importance of supporting Indigenous students in accessing quality Catholic secondary education.
description: This scholarship is awarded to students meeting all of the following criteria: students entering Year 7 in 2026, who identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander, and who commit to complete their secondary education at Kildare College. The scholarship provides support for eligible Indigenous students, though the exact value of fee remission is determined based on individual circumstances and recommendation by the Principal. Selection is based on the student's Indigenous status and demonstrated commitment to completing secondary education at the College. The scholarship reflects the College's commitment to equity and support for Indigenous students.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 2026; Must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must commit to complete secondary education at Kildare College
special_conditions: Award is made on meeting all criteria: students entering Year 7 2026, Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander status, and commitment to complete secondary education at Kildare College.

overview: The Kingston Community School Student Scholarship provides educational support to past, current, and future students of Kingston Community School. Managed through the Stand Like Stone Foundation, this scholarship is awarded on the basis of equity to help reduce financial barriers to education.
description: The Kingston Community School Student Scholarship Sub Fund was established by Brian and Sue Page in recognition of their family's connection to the school and their shared commitment to supporting youth through education. The scholarship supports secondary and tertiary education in the Limestone Coast. Scholarships may assist with secondary education costs or support tertiary pathways such as university, TAFE or other approved courses. The fund is designed to reduce financial barriers and help students pursue their goals with confidence and support. The scholarship value and selection process are determined by the Stand Like Stone Foundation Educational Scholarship Advisory Committee, with applications evaluated on the basis of equity as defined by the foundation's guidelines.
eligibility_criteria: Open to past, current, or future students of Kingston Community School; Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident; Must be enrolled in or intending to enrol in a recognised course of study in the calendar year of application; Selection based on reasons of equity; Preference for students experiencing socio-economic disadvantage or hardship
special_conditions: All successful applicants are required to sign the Stand Like Stone Foundation Educational Scholarship Conditions and provide a report on the use of the funds. Applications are completed online. The scholarship can support secondary education costs or tertiary pathways including university, TAFE or other approved courses. Applicants are encouraged to seek advice from Centrelink regarding the impact of scholarships on benefits.

overview: Lobethal Lutheran School provides a tuition fee discount of $500 for families who qualify for the Department for Education School Card scheme. This financial assistance is available to eligible families to help support their child's education costs.
description: Lobethal Lutheran School offers a $500 tuition fee discount to families that qualify for the Department for Education (DfE) School Card scheme. The discount is granted to families according to guidelines set by DfE and must be applied for annually using the DfE's online application form. The school is automatically advised when eligibility is confirmed. This financial assistance helps reduce the burden of tuition fees for eligible families experiencing financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Must qualify for Department for Education School Card scheme; Must apply annually; Determined by DfE guidelines
special_conditions: Must be applied for annually using the Department for Education online application form. The early payment discount (4%) will not apply in conjunction with School Card or Bursary discounts where tuition fees are paid in full by 3 February.

overview: Lobethal Lutheran School offers family and sibling discounts for families with more than one student enrolled. This discount helps reduce overall tuition costs for families with multiple children attending the school.
description: Lobethal Lutheran School provides family and sibling discounts to reduce tuition fees for families with more than one student enrolled at the school. The discount applies where both enrolling parents/caregivers are the same for each student and all students are billed on one account. For families with children at both Lobethal Lutheran School and Cornerstone College, an additional combined discount of $1,000 is provided. This represents a significant financial benefit for multi-child households.
eligibility_criteria: Must have more than one student enrolled; Both enrolling parents/caregivers must be the same for each student; Students must be billed on one account
special_conditions: Families with children at both Lobethal Lutheran School and Cornerstone College receive an additional discount of $1,000 total, comprised of $200 off the eldest child's fees at Lobethal Lutheran School and $800 off fees at Cornerstone College. Early payment discount does not apply with sibling/family discounts.

overview: Lobethal Lutheran School is committed to working with families experiencing financial hardship to negotiate fair and equitable fee arrangements. The school has a structured process to assess financial circumstances and provide appropriate assistance.
description: Lobethal Lutheran School encourages any family experiencing financial hardship to contact the Business Manager for a confidential discussion about their personal situation. The school has a structured process in place whereby families can provide evidence of financial hardship to access an appropriate level of financial support. Lobethal Lutheran School recognizes that families have different financial circumstances and is committed to working with them to negotiate fee payments which are fair and equitable to all parties, supporting the education of their children while maintaining the school's financial stability.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled or prospective families; Experiencing financial hardship; Willingness to provide evidence of financial circumstances; Consultation with Business Manager required
special_conditions: Families must make direct contact with Business Manager. Process is confidential. Financial hardship must be evidenced. Arrangements are negotiated on a case-by-case basis. Non-payment of agreed fees is a breach of the enrolment contract and can affect continued enrolment.

overview: McAuley Community School grants sibling discounts for families with multiple students enrolled. This program assists families managing education costs for more than one child at the school.
description: McAuley Community School provides sibling discount programs for families with two or more children enrolled at the school in a given school year. The discount is applied to tuition fees and is available to assist families managing the costs of educating multiple children. Specific discount amounts vary and can be discussed with the Finance Office. The program is available to all families regardless of year level, and discounts are applied automatically upon verification of sibling enrollment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more students enrolled at McAuley Community School in the same school year; Discounts do not apply to siblings enrolled at Alive Catholic Early Learning Centre
special_conditions: Discounts apply only to tuition fees enrolled at McAuley Community School. Discounts must be requested and verified each year. No discount applies to Alive Early Learning Centre siblings.

overview: McAuley Community School offers reduced tuition fees for lower income families approved for South Australian School Card. Students from eligible families receive significantly reduced annual fees based on household income thresholds.
description: McAuley Community School provides the lower income families fee for approved School Card recipients. Eligible families pay reduced tuition fees of $2,340 annually for Reception to Year 4 students and $2,840 for Year 5 to Year 6 students, compared to standard tuition rates. Families must complete School Card application forms available at the beginning of each year. Income eligibility thresholds apply (2019-2020 threshold was $60,867 for one child). Forms must be completed and resubmitted annually to maintain eligibility. The program requires a simple application process with eligibility determination based on household income.
eligibility_criteria: Approved for South Australian School Card; Household income below state threshold; Application forms must be completed annually; Families must demonstrate financial need
special_conditions: Applications must be completed and resubmitted annually. Eligibility is based on current household income and number of children. Forms available from Business Manager. Income threshold applied each year.

overview: McAuley Community School provides discretionary fee assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship beyond standard discount programs. Families can apply directly to the school administration for consideration of additional support.
description: Parents and caregivers requiring financial assistance beyond sibling discounts or School Card eligibility can apply for additional fee remission. The application process involves completing a formal Application Form requiring disclosure of financial information and conducting an interview with the Principal and/or Business Manager. The school reviews applications on their individual merits and advises families of the outcome and any agreed payment plan for the year. All applications for fee assistance must be reviewed and resubmitted on an annual basis. The school aims to work with families to find sustainable payment solutions during periods of financial difficulty.
eligibility_criteria: Financial circumstances have changed significantly; Genuine financial hardship demonstrated; Not eligible for other fee assistance programs; Application form completion required; Interview with Principal or Business Manager required
special_conditions: Requires completion of Application Form with financial information disclosure. Mandatory interview with school administration. All applications must be reviewed annually. The school board must approve all fee decisions. Payment plans are negotiated individually with families.

overview: Marryatville High School provides the School Card Grant to assist students from low income families. This means-tested scheme helps families with the cost of materials and services charges for full-time students.
description: The School Card grant is a means-tested scheme to provide assistance towards the cost of materials & services charges for full time students of low income families. The school card grant must be applied for each year. This is a state-wide South Australian Government program, not a school-specific scholarship. Financial value varies based on family income assessment and must be reapplied annually. The grant covers materials and services charges only, not tuition fees. Families must demonstrate financial need through means testing.
eligibility_criteria: Full-time student at Marryatville High School; Low income family status; Must reapply annually; Assessed on means test
special_conditions: School Card grant must be applied for each year. Assistance is towards materials & services charges only, not general tuition fees. Eligibility determined by Department for Education based on family income assessment.

overview: Marryatville High School offers the Special Interest Music Program as a selective entry program for musically talented students. The award-winning program is recognized both nationally and internationally and provides specialist music education for students with professional music potential.
description: Marryatville's Special Interest Music Centre was established in 1976 and is one of four specialist centres in Adelaide. The program provides models of excellence in music education in South Australia and is recognized both nationally and internationally. Entry is by audition and selection process. Students gain specialist music tuition, access to multiple ensembles including orchestras, concert bands, jazz bands, and choirs, and opportunities for international performance tours. The program includes theoretical study of music as well as practical performance. Financial value not specified on available pages; students must cover costs of materials and may access reimbursements for private tuition on primary instrument (conditions apply).
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated musical talent and ability; Entry by audition; Year 7 entry available for 2027; Entry available for middle and senior school students; School location requirement may apply
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Entry is highly competitive and based on musical ability demonstrated through audition. Students must have demonstrated commitment to engaging with the academic program. Partial reimbursements of fees for private tuition on primary instrument available (conditions apply). Contact Music Centre for application details.

overview: Marryatville High School operates the Special Interest Tennis Program, established in 1995, for students training at elite levels in tennis. The program allows students to combine academic school studies with high-level tennis coaching from accredited coaches.
description: The Special Interest Tennis Program has been operational at Marryatville High School since 1995. The program allows students to combine academic school studies, with high-level tennis coaching. Students receive a broad academic education including specialist tennis coaching by accredited coaches during school hours, on the school's six hard courts and two synthetic courts. Entry requires either Australian tennis ranking (ATR) for boys of 2000 or less and for girls 1000 or less, or current Universal Tennis Rating (UTR). Students must demonstrate committed engagement with their academic program, provide a letter of recommendation from their school principal, and show willingness to be a role model. Annual subject levy of $1,000 applies. No financial scholarship or fee remission is specified.
eligibility_criteria: Universal Tennis Rating (UTR) or Australian Tennis Ranking (ATR): boys 2000 or less, girls 1000 or less; Demonstrated commitment to academic program from previous school; Letter of recommendation from school principal; Character strength and role model potential; Nominated tennis referee such as private coach or club president; Year 6 and 7 students selected for entry
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Annual Special Interest Tennis Subject Levy: $1,000 per year. Students enrolled in the special interest tennis program are expected to compete in Saturday morning tennis competitions for Marryatville High School during terms 1 and 4, which constitutes part of their term assessment. Students must purchase specific tennis uniform including Marryatville High School tennis shirt, jacket, navy blue shorts and tracksuit pants. Program established by agreement between Department for Education and Tennis SA.

overview: Mid North Christian College provides assistance for families experiencing financial hardship through a grant from the Mid North Christian College Foundation. The college offers confidential support to discuss options with families facing financial difficulty.
description: Mid North Christian College offers financial hardship assistance to eligible families experiencing financial difficulty for any reason. This support is funded by a grant from the Mid North Christian College Foundation. Families are encouraged to have a confidential discussion with the college to discuss available options and support. The college recognises the importance of educational access and provides tailored assistance based on individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship for any reason; Confidential application required; Must communicate directly with the college
special_conditions: Applications are handled confidentially. Support is assessed on a case-by-case basis dependent on individual family circumstances and foundation funding availability.

overview: Mid North Christian College offers sibling discounts to reduce fees for families with multiple students enrolled. The second student receives a 10% fee reduction, and third and subsequent students are charged at a flat rate.
description: The college provides a sibling discount program designed to support families with multiple children enrolled at the school. The second enrolled student in a family receives a 10% discount on fees, while the third and all subsequent students are charged at a flat fee of $310 per student per year. This program aims to make private education more accessible to larger families and recognises the additional financial burden of educating multiple children.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more students from the same family enrolled at the college; Second child eligible for 10% reduction; Third and subsequent children charged at flat rate
special_conditions: Discount applies automatically to second student in family. Third and subsequent students receive fixed rate of $310 per year. Discounts apply to annual school fees.

overview: Mid North Christian College offers fee discounts for families eligible for the School Card program. This government-supported scheme provides financial assistance to eligible families to help cover school-related expenses.
description: The college provides fee discounts for families who are eligible for the School Card program, a South Australian government initiative designed to support families experiencing financial hardship. School Card assistance can help with school-related expenses including fees. Eligible families should contact the college to discuss how School Card support can be applied to their fees and to understand what additional assistance may be available.
eligibility_criteria: Family must be eligible for South Australian School Card program; Families experiencing financial hardship; Must meet School Card eligibility criteria; Contact college for assessment
special_conditions: Eligibility determined by South Australian government School Card criteria. Families must apply through the School Card program separately and inform the college of approval.

overview: Mitcham Girls High School participates in the Zonta Club of Adelaide scholarship program for Year 11 and Year 12 students. The scholarship recognises girls demonstrating academic effort and achievement, along with personal commitment to community service. The Zonta Club of Adelaide provides annual scholarships for girls at Mitcham Girls High School.
description: The Zonta Club of Adelaide offers $1,000 scholarships annually to eligible girls at Mitcham Girls High School. Year 11 students nominated by the school receive $1,000, and contingent on their continued success, recipients are eligible to receive the scholarship again in Year 12. Selection criteria includes academic effort and achievement combined with demonstrated personal commitment to community service. The scholarship recognises girls pursuing education in traditionally male-dominated fields, as the Zonta Club is dedicated to achieving gender equality in education and supporting scholarships for women and girls pursuing careers in these areas. Two students per year are selected from Mitcham Girls High School to receive this award.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 student; Open to girls at Mitcham Girls High School; Selection by school nomination; Academic effort and achievement required; Personal commitment to community service required; Renewal contingent on continued success in Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is contingent on continued success. Recipients nominated by school rather than self-applying. Contingent upon remaining at school and maintaining satisfactory performance.

overview: Mercedes College offers the Year 7 Excellence Scholarship to talented students entering Middle School from outside the college. The scholarship recognises students with strong learning approaches, personal strengths, and potential to grow through a wide range of learning experiences and extra-curricular opportunities including music, sport, chess, dance, robotics, and debating.
description: The Year 7 Excellence Scholarship is available for entry into Year 7 and is open to students not currently enrolled at Mercedes College. This scholarship meets up to 50% of the annual tuition fee for Year 7, and continues for the following two years of Middle School (Year 8 to Year 9). The scholarship does not require specific academic results, sporting prowess, or musical ability as formal criteria, though these factors may be considered during assessment. Selection is based on a good all-round approach to learning, personal strengths, and potential to grow at Mercedes College. Shortlisted applicants are invited to an interview with the Principal and Head of Middle School, which may or may not include an aptitude test. The scholarship excludes Resources Fee and additional discretionary charges including curriculum extension activity costs, uniform, and book/stationery resources.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must not be currently enrolled at Mercedes College; Must demonstrate strong prior academic record/transcript to end of previous year of study; Must demonstrate pursuits in other areas such as sport, academic clubs, performing arts, visual arts, and/or music; Must demonstrate involvement in an area of action such as being a member of a community group or sporting club; Applicants must show positive approach and strong personal values that would contribute to Mercedes College community
special_conditions: Scholarship meets up to 50% of annual tuition fee for Year 7 and continues for Years 8 to 9. Scholarship excludes Resources Fee and additional discretionary charges including curriculum extension activity costs, uniform, and book/stationery resources. Shortlisted students are invited to an interview with Principal and Head of Middle School, which may or may not include an aptitude test.

overview: The Mercedes Old Scholars' Association (MOSA) offers a Senior School scholarship every three years to give a deserving student the benefit of a Mercedes College education who would not otherwise have the opportunity. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate commitment to blending academic achievement with personal and character development, with both merit and financial need considered in selection.
description: The MOSA Scholarship is offered every three years to one external student entering Year 10 and covers tuition fees for the complete three-year Senior School experience from Year 10 through to Year 12. This includes coverage of the tuition fees, capital levy, and resource fee for all three years. The scholarship recognises and supports students who demonstrate a commitment to education that blends academic achievement with personal and character development. Selection is competitive and considers both merit and financial need of applicants. The next MOSA Scholarship will be awarded to a student set to start Year 10 in 2027, with applications opening in Semester 1 of 2026. Shortlisted students will be invited to an interview with the Principal and Head of Senior School, which may or may not include an aptitude test. The scholarship is made possible through the generous support of Mercedes College's alumni network.
eligibility_criteria: Open to external students entering Year 10; Not currently enrolled at Mercedes College; Must demonstrate academic achievement; Must show personal and character development; Must show financial need; Must demonstrate commitment to Mercedes College education and values
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded every three years to one student. Covers tuition fees, capital levy, and resource fee for Years 10, 11, and 12. Recipients must be committed to education blending academic achievement with personal and character development. Shortlisted students invited to interview with Principal and Head of Senior School, which may or may not include aptitude test. Next scholarship awarded for Year 10 entry in 2027.

overview: Mercedes College offers one full three-year scholarship annually to an external student wishing to enter Year 10 with the intention of studying the International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma. The scholarship provides complete remission of 100% of tuition fees for Years 10, 11, and 12, enabling academically talented students to access a world-class IB education.
description: The Year 10 IB Diploma Scholarship is offered annually to one external student entering Year 10 who intends to study the IB Diploma program. This scholarship provides full remission of 100% of tuition fees for the three-year period covering Years 10, 11, and 12. The IB Diploma Programme is designed to develop students who have excellent breadth and depth of knowledge and who flourish physically, intellectually, emotionally, and ethically. This scholarship recognises students with demonstrated academic excellence and commitment to pursuing advanced international education. Selection is based on academic merit and the student's intention to complete the full IB Diploma. The scholarship is available to students not currently enrolled at Mercedes College and represents a significant investment in supporting talented students to access premium international education at Mercedes.
eligibility_criteria: Open to external students entering Year 10; Must not be currently enrolled at Mercedes College; Must intend to study the IB Diploma; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Must be seeking full-time secondary education
special_conditions: One scholarship offered annually to external student entering Year 10. Provides full remission of 100% of tuition fees for Years 10, 11, and 12. Recipient must intend to study IB Diploma program. Available only to students not currently enrolled at Mercedes College.

overview: Mercedes College offers up to three Catherine McAuley Justice Scholarships annually to internal students entering Year 10. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate commitment to justice, community leadership, and the values of Mercy tradition. The scholarships honour the legacy of Catherine McAuley and support students with strong character and community contribution.
description: The Catherine McAuley Justice Scholarships are offered annually to up to three internal students (current Mercedes College students) entering Year 10. Each scholarship provides remission of 100% of tuition fees for three years (Years 10, 11, and 12). The scholarships are named after Catherine McAuley, the founder of the Sisters of Mercy, and recognise students who embody justice, compassion, and leadership values central to the Mercy tradition. These scholarships are specifically designed for students already enrolled at Mercedes College who demonstrate exceptional commitment to justice and community. Parents of students entering Year 10 the following year are sent information via SEQTA Engage when their child is in Year 9, including detailed criteria and application instructions. Selection considers demonstrated commitment to justice, community leadership, and alignment with Mercy values and principles.
eligibility_criteria: Must be currently enrolled at Mercedes College; Open to internal students entering Year 10; Must demonstrate commitment to justice and community leadership; Must align with Mercy tradition values; Must show strong character development
special_conditions: Up to three scholarships offered annually to internal (current) students entering Year 10. Provides full remission of tuition fees for Years 10, 11, and 12. Information sent to parents via SEQTA Engage when student is in Year 9. Named after Catherine McAuley, recognising Justice and community leadership values.

overview: Mount Barker High School awards The David Floyd Smith Prize to recognise outstanding achievement and interest in agriculture and conservation. The prize is presented annually to a deserving Year 11 or 12 student who demonstrates exceptional commitment to these fields.
description: The David Floyd Smith Prize has been awarded since the end of 2015 to recognise students with a demonstrated interest in agriculture and conservation. The prize is awarded annually to a Year 11 or 12 student selected by the school. Selection criteria focus on the student's interest, commitment, and engagement with agriculture and conservation principles and practices. The exact financial value of the prize is not publicly specified on the school website. Award recipients are recognised at the school's end-of-year Presentation Assembly.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or Year 12; Demonstrated interest in agriculture and conservation; Currently enrolled at Mount Barker High School
special_conditions: Award recipients are recognised at the school's end-of-year Presentation Assembly. Recipients are selected by the school based on their demonstrated interest in agriculture and conservation.

overview: Mount Barker High School awards The Matt Baird Scholarship to students to support their development and help them meet their full potential. The scholarship has been awarded annually since early 2016 to recognise students demonstrating commitment to their academic and personal growth.
description: The Matt Baird Scholarship has been awarded annually since early 2016 to assist students in meeting their full potential. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate commitment to their academic development and personal growth. The exact financial value, duration, and selection process for the scholarship are not specified in the publicly available information. Award recipients are recognised at the school's recognition assemblies and end-of-year Presentation Assembly. The scholarship aims to provide support and recognition to eligible Mount Barker High School students.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Mount Barker High School; Demonstrated commitment to academic and personal growth; Selected by school administration
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded annually to selected students. Recipients are recognised at school assemblies and the end-of-year Presentation Assembly.

overview: Mount Barker High School offers the Roy Alexander Scholarship to support students in agriculture and rural studies. The scholarship provides hands-on training opportunities and recognises students interested in farming, stockmanship, and agricultural practices.
description: The Roy Alexander Scholarship is utilised in two distinct ways to support students with agricultural interests. Firstly, a former Mount Barker High School student is selected from the Year 12 cohort to attend an all-expenses-paid three-week hands-on training course in stockmanship of sheep and cattle and general farming skills in Hay, New South Wales, through the Hay Inc. program. The selected student attends this course the following year after graduation. Secondly, the scholarship funds the showing of Merino sheep at the annual Royal Adelaide Show, providing Year 9 students with an educational opportunity to learn about merino sheep history, animal husbandry, and sheep handling techniques using halters and leads.
eligibility_criteria: Year 9 (for Royal Adelaide Show participation); Year 12 (for Hay Inc. training course); Demonstrated interest in agriculture and livestock; Currently enrolled at Mount Barker High School
special_conditions: Year 12 recipients attend the three-week Hay Inc. training course in Hay, NSW the year following their graduation. Year 9 students participate in the Royal Adelaide Show Merino sheep program. Merino sheep are kindly donated annually by Judy and Keith Paech of Lucernbrae Callington.

overview: Mount Barker Waldorf School offers bursary support to families experiencing financial hardship beyond government assistance. The bursary provides fee remission as a special discount to eligible families enrolled at the school.
description: The bursary is a form of financial assistance available on a limited basis to families who demonstrate financial hardship beyond what is covered by the State Government School Card Discount. Families may apply for partial or full fee remission depending on their circumstances. Application forms are available directly from the school. The bursary is provided on an annual basis per school year. If family circumstances change throughout the year, additional applications can be submitted and will be assessed at that time. The amount of fee remission varies based on individual family circumstances and available funding. Terms and conditions apply to all bursary awards.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Mount Barker Waldorf School; Experiencing financial hardship above and beyond School Card Discount eligibility; Demonstrated commitment to Waldorf education philosophy; Application submitted to school administration
special_conditions: Application forms are available from the school. The bursary is given per school year. Applications for a bursary/fee remission are due by the end of March each year. If circumstances change throughout the year an application can be submitted and will be assessed. Terms and conditions apply. Please contact the front office via office@mbws.sa.edu.au or 08 8391 0411 to view the full terms and conditions.

overview: Mount Carmel College offers the School Card Scheme to provide financial assistance towards educational expenses for families who meet the eligibility criteria. This program delivers a 40% discount on tuition fees (Tier 2) for eligible families.
description: The School Card Scheme provides a 40% discount off tuition fees (Tier 2) for families who hold a Centrelink Concession Card. Eligible families are required to pay only a $50 non-refundable Acceptance Fee and must provide a copy of the Centrelink Card at time of payment, which must list the enrolling child's name. The discount applies to all years of enrolment, and families must reapply each year to ensure continued eligibility. The scheme is available to students from Year 7 through Year 12, with assistance continuing for the duration of enrolment at the College. The College encourages families experiencing financial difficulty to contact the Business Manager or College Accountant to discuss their situation.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold valid Centrelink Concession Card; Centrelink Card must list the enrolling child's name; Annual reapplication required; Families must meet criteria set by government assistance programs
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Non-refundable $50 Acceptance Fee required. Centrelink Concession Card must list the enrolling child's name. Families must reapply each year to maintain eligibility. Discount subject to annual government assessment of eligibility criteria.

overview: Mount Carmel College provides a Catholic Primary Sibling Discount for families who have a child enrolled in a Catholic Primary School. This discount recognises families supporting Catholic education across multiple institutions.
description: Families with a child enrolled in a Catholic Primary School (excluding Mount Carmel College Primary Campus students) may request the Catholic Primary Sibling Discount (CPS) be applied to their tuition fees. The discount amount and specific terms are detailed in the school's Fee Schedule. The discount is designed to support families who are investing in Catholic education at both primary and secondary levels. To apply, families should contact the College Accountant and request the CPS discount be applied in accordance with the Fee Schedule. The discount applies to secondary students in Years 7 through Year 12. Families must ensure they provide documentation of primary school enrolment to qualify.
eligibility_criteria: Child enrolled in Catholic Primary School; Excluding Mount Carmel College Primary Campus students; Secondary student in Years 7-12; Must request discount through College Accountant; Must provide proof of Catholic Primary enrolment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Discount applies only to families with siblings in Catholic Primary Schools, not Mount Carmel College Primary Campus. Must request through College Accountant. Discount amount specified in annual Fee Schedule. Families should contact the Accountant for current discount details.

overview: Mount Carmel College offers fee remission arrangements for families experiencing genuine financial difficulty in paying tuition fees. The College works with families in a compassionate and confidential manner to identify alternative financial arrangements.
description: Families experiencing genuine financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the College's Business Manager or College Accountant to discuss their situation confidentially. The College operates in the Josephite tradition and is committed to offering great support in helping families manage their financial contributions. Parents or guardians meeting with the principal or delegate for consideration will have any agreed arrangements formally communicated in writing, subject to periodic review, and may have terms and conditions applied. The College emphasises that it will work with families in a just, respectful, compassionate, transparent and considered manner in determining fee remission arrangements. Details of family financial circumstances are handled respectfully and with appropriate confidentiality. Alternative payment plans and arrangements may be negotiated to assist families unable to pay full fees upfront. Fee remission is available throughout the year for students in Years 7 through Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing genuine financial difficulty; Must initiate contact with College Accountant or Business Manager; Information handled confidentially; Alternative arrangements subject to review; Families must demonstrate good faith in making payments
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Families must contact College Accountant (Virginia Reimann) to discuss situation. All arrangements formally communicated in writing. Subject to periodic review and may have terms and conditions applied. Information treated with strict confidentiality. Families expected to act in good faith and honour agreed payment plans. Alternative payment options include Direct Debit, BPAY, EFTPOS, Centrepay, and FAT ZEBRA payment plans.

overview: Youth Allowance is a Commonwealth Government program providing direct financial assistance to secondary students aged 16 and over who are undertaking full-time secondary studies. Mount Carmel College supports eligible students in accessing this government support.
description: Youth Allowance provides financial assistance from the Commonwealth Government to students 16 years and over who are enrolled in full-time secondary studies. Students become eligible to receive payments on their 16th birthday, and Youth Allowance is payable fortnightly. The amount of allowance payable is determined by the family's income, assessed by Centrelink. This is a government-funded program, not a school-based scholarship, but it represents an important source of financial support for secondary families. Students and families are encouraged to investigate their eligibility through Centrelink. Further information is available via the Centrelink website. The College acknowledges this scheme as one of the key financial assistance options available to secondary families. Applications are made directly to Centrelink, not through the school.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be 16 years or older; Undertaking full-time secondary studies; Eligible from 16th birthday; Amount determined by family income assessment; Assessed and administered by Centrelink; Australian resident or eligible visa holder
special_conditions: Payable fortnightly once approved. Income-tested by Centrelink. Not a school-based program; application through Centrelink. Amount varies based on family circumstances. Students become eligible on their 16th birthday.

overview: Muirden Senior College offers a limited number of Academic Scholarships to outstanding Year 10 students. The scholarship recognises students who would benefit from the learning environment provided at Muirden and demonstrates exceptional academic potential.
description: The Academic Scholarship is provided to a limited number of outstanding Year 10 students who, in the Principal's opinion, would benefit from the learning environment provided at Muirden Senior College. These scholarships are provided for two school years (Year 11 and Year 12). The scholarship consists of a support package that may include financial assistance with tuition fees and access to the college's small class environment and specialist teaching support. Consideration of scholarship criteria occurs at the enrolment interview.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding academic achievement; Currently enrolled in Year 10; Principal's assessment at enrolment interview; Demonstrated ability to benefit from Muirden's learning environment
special_conditions: Scholarships are provided for two school years (Year 11 and Year 12). Consideration of criteria occurs at the enrolment interview. Specific financial value is determined by the Principal at the time of offer.

overview: Muirden Senior College provides 'Back to School' Scholarships for students who left school early and wish to complete senior secondary studies. The scholarship supports students in returning to education with a view to further tertiary study.
description: The 'Back to School' Scholarship is designed for students who left school early and now wish to complete senior secondary studies with a view to further study or training. These scholarships recognise the commitment and potential of students returning to education. The scholarship consists of a support package provided for one or two years, which may include financial assistance with tuition fees and support services. The scholarship acknowledges both the students' determination to complete their secondary education and the value they bring to the college community.
eligibility_criteria: Student who left school early; Seeking to return to complete senior secondary studies; Intention to continue to tertiary study or training; Eligible for Years 11 or 12 enrolment
special_conditions: Scholarships are provided for one or two years. Specific financial value is determined by the college. Applicants are assessed at the enrolment interview stage.

program_name: Murray Bridge Rotary Club Music Bursary Scholarship
overview: Murray Bridge High School offers the Music Bursary Scholarship in partnership with the Murray Bridge Rotary Club. This annual award of $1,000 recognises outstanding achievement in music by a senior student at the school.
description: The Murray Bridge Rotary Club has a long-standing partnership with Murray Bridge High School, supporting young musicians through a $1,000 Music Bursary Scholarship for one senior student each year. The scholarship is awarded to recognise musical talent, achievement and dedication to music programs at the school. The award value is $1,000 provided annually to one eligible student. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence in music performance and participation in school music programs including instrumental or choral ensembles. Recipients are typically Year 11 or Year 12 students who have made significant contributions to the school's music community. The scholarship supports students pursuing music education and excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to senior students (Year 11 or Year 12); Active participation in school music programs (instrumental or choral); Demonstrated musical achievement and talent; Currently enrolled at Murray Bridge High School
special_conditions: Award recipients must be enrolled senior students at Murray Bridge High School. Selection based on musical talent, experience and commitment to school music programs.

overview: Murray Bridge High School partners with the Ngarrindjeri community through Ngopun Together to provide an annual scholarship for eligible Ngarrindjeri Year 12 students. The scholarship recognises and supports Indigenous students completing their final year of secondary education.
description: A scholarship has been generously donated by families in partnership with Ngopun Together to support worthy Year 12 Ngarrindjeri students each year. This program reflects the school's commitment to supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students and strengthening connections with the Ngarrindjeri community. The scholarship provides financial support and recognition for Ngarrindjeri students completing Year 12 (SACE). Eligibility requires that the student identifies as Ngarrindjeri and is in Year 12. The exact financial value is not specified in public materials, but the scholarship is awarded annually to one eligible student. The program is coordinated through the school's partnership with community organisations including Ngopun Together and recognises the importance of supporting Indigenous educational pathways.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Identifies as Ngarrindjeri or Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander; Currently enrolled at Murray Bridge High School; Demonstrated commitment to education
special_conditions: Scholarship is offered annually to one worthy Year 12 Ngarrindjeri student each year. This reflects the school's commitment to supporting Indigenous students and strengthening community partnerships.

overview: Navigator College offers the Directions for Life Scholarships to new enrolments in 2027 from Year 7 to 11. These scholarships recognise students with exemplary attitude, high standards of academic achievement and behaviour, and positive contribution to all aspects of College life.
description: Navigator College are very excited to be offering a limited number of Directions for Life Scholarships to new enrolments in 2027 from Year 7 to 11. These scholarships will entitle their recipients to between 50% - 100% percent remission on tuition fees for 2 years should they be successful. Navigator College are seeking applicants with exemplary attitude, high standards of academic achievement and behaviour, and to contribute positively and enthusiastically to all aspects of College life. The selection process involves assessment of academic achievement, attitude, behaviour, and overall contribution to the college community. Applications should be directed to the College Registrar for further details regarding the application process.
eligibility_criteria: New enrolments in 2027; Year 7 to Year 11; Exemplary attitude and behaviour; High standards of academic achievement; Positive contribution to all aspects of College life
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for 2 years. Recipients must maintain exemplary behaviour and academic standards throughout the duration of the scholarship. The College reserves the right to withdraw scholarships for unsatisfactory conduct or academic performance.

school_name: Nazareth Catholic College - Flinders Park Campus
Nazareth Catholic College - Kidman Park Campus
Nazareth Catholic College - St Gabriel Centre Campus

overview: Oakbank School awards Onkaparinga Lions Club Scholarships to students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement and community engagement. These scholarships provide financial support for educational purposes to qualified secondary students.
description: The Onkaparinga Lions Club Scholarships are awarded to multiple students across different year levels at Oakbank School. Recipients receive $1,000 each for educational purposes whilst at Oakbank School during their year of study. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate strong character, commitment to learning, and positive contributions to the school community. Based on publicly available school newsletters, scholarships have been awarded to Year 10, Year 11, and Year 12 students. Selection criteria emphasize academic performance, community service, and ethical standards in alignment with Lions Club principles.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary school students at Oakbank School; Demonstrated academic achievement; Evidence of community service and positive contribution to school community; Strong character and ethical standards
special_conditions: Award is for educational purposes whilst enrolled at Oakbank School. Recipient must maintain satisfactory academic performance and community engagement standards.

overview: Oakbank School presents the Kavel Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in Year 12 students. This award acknowledges students who have demonstrated exceptional scholarly merit and commitment to their studies throughout their secondary education.
description: The Kavel Scholarship is awarded to Year 12 students at Oakbank School who demonstrate exceptional academic excellence. While specific financial details are limited in public documentation, the scholarship recognises outstanding student achievement and serves as an honour award for top-performing students. The scholarship has been awarded to students completing their final year of secondary education. Selection is based on sustained academic performance throughout the student's time at the school, with particular emphasis on Year 12 results and overall scholarly achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student at Oakbank School; Outstanding academic performance; Completion of final year secondary studies
special_conditions: Award recognises exceptional academic achievement in Year 12. Determined by school leadership based on academic performance.

overview: Oakbank School awards annual Dux Scholarships to the top-performing student in each year level from Year 7 to Year 12. These prestigious awards recognise students demonstrating exceptional academic excellence and scholarly achievement across all subject areas.
description: Dux Awards are presented annually at Oakbank School to honour the highest-achieving student in each year level. Students are recognised for Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, and Year 12. The Dux Award represents the school's acknowledgement of outstanding academic performance, with selection based on cumulative academic results across all subjects studied. These awards are presented during the school's awards ceremonies and are highly valued honours within the school community. Recipients have demonstrated consistent excellence throughout their school year and are recognised as exemplary scholars.
eligibility_criteria: Student enrolled at Oakbank School; Highest academic performance in their respective year level; Completion of year level studies
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Award is automatic based on academic performance. No application required. Students are selected by academic staff based on year-level academic results.

school_name: Open Access College - Port Augusta School of the Air Campus
program_name: STEM Scholarships for High School Students Under-Represented in STEM
overview: Open Access College offers STEM Scholarships to support public high school students from groups underrepresented in STEM. The scholarships recognise Aboriginal learners and students from socio-economically disadvantaged backgrounds who are pursuing excellence in STEM subjects and pathways.
description: Scholarships up to the value of $10,000 are available to support public high school students from groups underrepresented in STEM (Aboriginal learners and students in socio-economic need) to complete SACE Stage 1 and SACE Stage 2 STEM subjects, as well as VET courses relevant to the defence industry sector including Electrotechnology, ICT (IT or Cyber), or Engineering Pathways. The scholarships can be used to cover the cost of tutors, laptops, excursions, or anything that directly supports the student to achieve in their chosen STEM subjects or pathways. Applicants are required to nominate a Mentor Teacher who will support them for the duration of their scholarship. Willingness to attend the STEM Scholarships Induction Day with a teacher or parent(s) which will be held early February 2026 at Adelaide University is required.
eligibility_criteria: Public high school students; Students from Aboriginal learner backgrounds or socio-economically disadvantaged backgrounds; SACE Stage 1 and Stage 2 STEM subject completion or defence industry-relevant VET courses; Willingness to attend STEM Scholarships Induction Day in early February 2026; Must nominate a Mentor Teacher for support during scholarship period
special_conditions: Scholarships are specifically targeted at students from underrepresented groups in STEM including Aboriginal learners and students in socio-economic need. Recipients must attend the STEM Scholarships Induction Day at Adelaide University in early February 2026 with a teacher or parent. A Mentor Teacher must be nominated to support the student throughout the scholarship period. The funds can be flexibly used to support achievement in STEM subjects including tutoring, technology, excursions, and other direct educational supports.

program_name: School Card Scheme Low Income Fees
overview: Our Lady of Grace School offers reduced tuition fees for eligible low-income families through the South Australian School Card Scheme. The school works with families experiencing financial difficulty to ensure access to Catholic education.
description: Families who are eligible for the South Australian School Card Scheme receive low income fees at Our Lady of Grace School. Applications are available online through the SA Government website at https://www.sa.gov.au/topics/education-and-learning/financial-help-scholarships-and-grants/school-card-scheme. The school recognises that a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing a Catholic education. The school actively encourages families experiencing difficulty in managing fees to contact the finance office to discuss payment arrangements and available support options.
eligibility_criteria: Must be eligible for SA School Card Scheme; Demonstrated financial need; Enrolled or enrolling at the school
special_conditions: Eligibility is determined by SA Government School Card Scheme criteria. The school encourages families to contact finance@olog.catholic.edu.au if experiencing difficulty managing fees.

program_name: Sibling Discount and Early Payment Discount
overview: Our Lady of Grace School offers generous sibling and early payment discounts to families. These discounts are designed to support families with multiple children enrolled at the school.
description: Families enrolling multiple children at Our Lady of Grace School are eligible for sibling discounts applied to tuition fees. Additionally, early payment of tuition fees qualifies families for payment discounts. The school aims to work in partnership with families in an equitable school fee setting and collection process. These discounts complement the school's commitment to accessibility and affordability, ensuring that financial barriers do not prevent access to quality Catholic education for families with multiple children.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more siblings enrolled concurrently at the school; Early payment required for early payment discount; Families in good standing with the school
special_conditions: Discounts apply to families with multiple siblings enrolled. Amounts vary based on number of siblings and payment timing. The school offers free Reception Term 3 intake to qualifying families.

overview: Our Lady of Grace School offers free tuition fees for Reception students commencing in Term 3. This program removes financial barriers for families entering their child into Reception.
description: Reception students who commence at Our Lady of Grace School at the beginning of Term 3 receive free tuition fees for their first term. This initiative is part of the school's commitment to accessibility and affordability in Catholic education. The school believes that cost should not be a barrier to families seeking quality primary education. The program applies to new Reception students entering in Term 3 each year, as the school offers two Reception intakes annually.
eligibility_criteria: Child turning 5 years of age on or before 30 April in the year of entry; Commencing Reception in Term 3 intake; Enrolled at Our Lady of Grace School
special_conditions: Applies only to Reception students commencing in Term 3 intake. Free fees apply for the first term only. Standard fees apply from Term 4 onwards.

program_name: Flexible Fee Payment Plans and Financial Consultation
overview: Our Lady of Grace School provides personalised financial consultation and flexible payment plans for families experiencing difficulty meeting tuition obligations. The school believes family financial circumstances should not prevent access to Catholic education.
description: Families concerned about their ability to pay school fees are encouraged to have a discussion with the school. The Finance Office works directly with families to develop flexible payment arrangements and explore available support options. The school offers various fee structures and payment options through the Qkr! payment system to accommodate different family circumstances. The school philosophy emphasises partnership with families, ensuring equitable fee setting and collection processes that prioritise student access to quality Catholic education regardless of financial situation.
eligibility_criteria: Any family enrolling at the school; Demonstrated concern about ability to pay fees; Willingness to discuss financial circumstances with the school
special_conditions: Arrangements are individually negotiated between families and the school. Contact Kerri Rowell, Finance Officer, on 8177 9100 or finance@olog.catholic.edu.au for confidential consultation.

school_name: Our Lady of the River School
Our Lady of La Vang School

program_name: STEM Scholarships for High School Students Under-represented in STEM
overview: Open Access College offers STEM Scholarships to support public high school students from groups underrepresented in STEM, including Aboriginal learners and students in socio-economic need. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate commitment to completing SACE Stage 1 and Stage 2 STEM subjects or VET courses relevant to the defence industry sector.
description: Scholarships up to the value of $10,000 are available to support public high school students from groups underrepresented in STEM (Aboriginal learners and students in socio-economic need) to complete SACE Stage 1 and SACE Stage 2 STEM subjects and VET courses relevant to the defence industry sector including Electrotechnology, ICT (IT or Cyber), or Engineering Pathways. The scholarships can be used to cover the cost of tutors, laptops, excursions, or anything that directly supports the student to achieve in their chosen STEM subjects or pathways. Applicants are required to nominate a Mentor Teacher who will support them for the duration of their scholarship. Recipients must attend the STEM Scholarships Induction Day, held early February 2026 at Adelaide University.
eligibility_criteria: Public high school students; Aboriginal learners or students in socio-economic need; Enrolled in SACE Stage 1 or Stage 2 STEM subjects or VET courses in defence industry sectors (Electrotechnology, ICT, Engineering Pathways); Willingness to attend STEM Scholarships Induction Day in early February 2026; Must nominate a Mentor Teacher
special_conditions: Recipients must support the commitment to STEM education and complete the induction day at Adelaide University in February 2026. Scholarship can be used for tutors, laptops, excursions, or other direct support for STEM achievement.

overview: Open Access College offers Women of STEM Scholarships to support female Year 12 students entering their first year of a STEM-related degree at university. The scholarship recognises women pursuing higher education in STEM fields.
description: Women of STEM scholarships are open to Year 12 students who will enter their first year of a STEM-related degree at university. The scholarship supports women in advancing their STEM education at the tertiary level. Further specific details regarding financial value, duration, and selection criteria are available on the college's website or through direct inquiry with the scholarship coordinator.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 student; Will enter first year of STEM-related degree at university; Female student
special_conditions: Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

school_name: Our Lady of the Visitation School
overview: Our Lady of the Visitation School offers a Lower Income Fee to families who qualify as low income families based on gross annual income thresholds set by the State Government. The program recognises that financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing a Catholic education.
description: The Lower Income Fee program provides fee relief to families who meet the State Government's income threshold. In 2025, families with one school-age child must have a gross income below approximately $74,189 to qualify for the reduced fee. The program applies to tuition fees and covers all tuition, levies and charges for each child's education, though extra-curricular activities, camps, and uniforms are excluded and charged separately. Applicants should contact the finance manager to apply for the lower income fee and discuss eligibility. The school states that financial circumstances should not be a barrier to a student accessing a Catholic education, and encourages families with payment concerns to have a discussion with the school.
eligibility_criteria: Family gross income below approximately $74,189 for a family with one school-age child (threshold set by State Government); Income determined from 2023/2024 financial year tax records; Open to all students enrolled at the school
special_conditions: Families with more than one student attending the school are eligible for additional discounts. School Card remissions and other discounts do not apply to Preschool Fees. Payments can be made in full or by approved payment plan including weekly, fortnightly, monthly or termly options.

school_name: Our Lady Queen of Peace School
overview: Our Lady Queen of Peace School provides automatic fee assistance through the School Card Scheme for eligible families. Students who qualify for support under the SA Government School Card Scheme receive a 40% discount against tuition fees as part of the school's commitment to keeping Catholic education accessible.
description: The School Card Scheme is a means-tested assistance program administered by the Department for Education that provides support toward school books and other school charges. According to SA Catholic School Guidelines, students who qualify for support under the SA Government School Card Scheme are not required to pay full fees and therefore receive an automatic 40% fee discount against tuition fees. All other fees beyond tuition must be paid in full. The school's commitment to accessibility ensures that financial circumstances do not prevent families from accessing Catholic education. Families receiving this support maintain confidentiality of their arrangement.
eligibility_criteria: Must qualify for SA Government School Card Scheme; based on family income assessment; administered by Department for Education
special_conditions: Automatic discount applied for School Card recipients. All other fees beyond tuition must be paid in full. Assistance is awarded confidentially.

school_name: Our Lady Queen of Peace School
overview: Our Lady Queen of Peace School offers confidential financial assistance for families experiencing genuine hardship who do not qualify for the School Card Scheme. The school works directly with families to negotiate appropriate fee reductions tailored to individual circumstances.
description: Families who do not qualify for the School Card Scheme but are in genuine financial difficulty may apply for a reduction in school fees. When payment of fees causes hardship at any time, families can make a confidential appointment with the Finance Officer (Mrs Diana Frangos) or the Principal to discuss their situation. The school recognizes that circumstances vary for each family and is committed to working collaboratively to find solutions. Fee reductions are assessed on a case-by-case basis considering family financial circumstances. The school's pastoral approach ensures that financial constraints do not prevent access to quality Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Not qualifying for School Card Scheme; genuine financial difficulty; must have enrolled child at school; confidential assessment required
special_conditions: Requires confidential appointment with Finance Officer (Mrs Diana Frangos) or Principal. Individual assessment of circumstances required. Assistance amount varies based on family situation. Available at any time if hardship occurs during school year.

school_name: Our Lady Queen of Peace School
overview: Our Lady Queen of Peace School offers an Early Bird discount incentive for families who pay their annual school fees in full by a specified deadline. This discount rewards prompt payment and helps families plan their finances effectively.
