description: An Early Bird discount of 5% is applicable to fees paid in full by the deadline specified in school communications (historically in April). This discount provides a financial incentive for families to pay their full annual tuition fees early. The discount is applied directly to the total fee amount, reducing the overall cost of education. Families must pay the complete annual fee by the deadline to qualify. This payment option supports family financial planning and provides modest savings for those with capacity to pay upfront. The discount demonstrates the school's commitment to making Catholic education more affordable while maintaining financial sustainability.
eligibility_criteria: Must pay full annual fees by specified deadline; payment via approved methods
special_conditions: Requires full payment of annual fees by specified deadline (typically April). Discount applies to tuition fees only. Not available for families on payment arrangements.

overview: Our Lady of Hope School offers generous fee remissions for eligible low-income families to ensure that financial circumstances do not become a barrier to accessing a Catholic education. The school is committed to working with families to develop suitable arrangements that make education affordable and achievable.
description: Our Lady of Hope School provides generous fee remissions available for low-income families as part of their commitment to accessibility and affordability. The financial assistance program is designed to support families who have concerns about the affordability of school fees. Eligible families can receive partial or full fee remission depending on their financial circumstances. A confidential meeting can be arranged with the Principal and Business Manager to discuss and establish suitable fee arrangements. The school operates on the principle that enrollment will not be denied due to financial hardship, though families are asked to contribute toward fees where possible. The exact remission amount varies based on individual family income assessments and is available for the duration of the child's enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families experiencing financial hardship; Family income must be assessed; Families must contact the school to discuss financial circumstances; Confidential meeting with Principal and Business Manager required to establish suitable arrangements; Non-refundable assessment required
special_conditions: Enrolment will not be denied due to financial hardship. Assessment is conducted on a case-by-case basis. Fee remissions are available for families demonstrating financial need. Families must be willing to contribute toward fees where possible. Sibling discounts also apply for each additional child enrolled at the school.

overview: Our Lady of Hope School provides sibling discounts for families with multiple children enrolled at the school. This discount program reduces the overall tuition costs for families with more than one child attending the school.
description: Our Lady of Hope School offers sibling discounts that apply for each additional child enrolled at the school. This financial support initiative is designed to make education more affordable for larger families and to recognize the commitment of families with multiple siblings attending the school. The discount is automatically applied to eligible families and reduces the annual school fees. The specific discount percentage is determined based on family circumstances and school policy. This benefit is available in addition to other financial assistance programs offered by the school and helps make Catholic education accessible to families with multiple children.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children from the same family must be enrolled at the school; Discount applies to the second and subsequent children; Open to all families regardless of background or faith
special_conditions: Discount applies to each additional child enrolled at the school. Sibling discounts are applied automatically upon enrollment of multiple children from the same family. This discount is available in conjunction with other payment options and fee assistance programs offered by the school.

overview: Palmer Primary School participates in the School Card Scheme, which provides financial assistance to students from lower-income families. This program helps eligible families meet the cost of materials and services charges at the school.
description: The School Card Scheme provides financial assistance towards the cost of materials and services charges for full-time school students of lower income families. Families may be approved for School Card if eligible for a Centrelink benefit. Students receive assistance with educational materials and services charges rather than tuition fees, as this is a government school. The application process requires families to provide evidence of Centrelink eligibility. Assistance is available for all enrolled students meeting income criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Family must be eligible for a Centrelink benefit; must be full-time student enrolled at Palmer Primary School; income-tested based on Australian government Centrelink eligibility
special_conditions: Families must provide appropriate paperwork demonstrating Centrelink eligibility. Contact school staff member Kellie for application forms and further information.

school_name: Our Saviour Lutheran School Happy Valley Incorporated
Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College
overview: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College offers four major scholarships named after the House Patrons, recognising academic excellence and leadership. These scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and involvement in college life.
description: The Four Major Scholarships are offered to new students starting at OLSH College in Year 7 or Year 8. These scholarships recognise academic excellence and the values represented by each House Patron. The exact financial value of each scholarship is not specified on the website but represents a significant fee reduction. Selection is application-based only, with no academic test required. Scholarship is reviewed on a yearly basis, with recipients needing to demonstrate ongoing academic achievement and involvement in the life of the college. A minimum of 90% attendance is required each year; if attendance falls below this threshold, the scholarship will no longer apply. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 7 or Year 8; Application-based selection; Local students only; 90% attendance required each year
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed on a yearly basis. Recipients must demonstrate ongoing academic achievement and involvement in life at the college. Minimum 90% attendance required each year; if attendance falls below, the scholarship will no longer apply. Scholarship is offered to local students only.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College
overview: The St Cecilia Instrumental Music Scholarship recognises musical talent and commitment to instrumental performance. This scholarship supports students who are currently undertaking or intending to undertake music tuition at OLSH College.
description: The St Cecilia Instrumental Music Scholarship provides tuition fee remission for 30-minute lessons based on a maximum amount each term, and instrumental hire if needed for 1 year. Other incidentals and additional lessons are not covered by the scholarship. The scholarship is available to existing and new students starting at OLSH College from any year level studying a particular instrument. A number of St Cecilia Instrumental Music Scholarships are awarded annually. Tuition is available in many different instruments and scholarships may be awarded with regard to the number of students already learning this instrument. Recipients must attend their weekly lesson, participate in relevant school ensembles and any associated performances.
eligibility_criteria: Students currently undertaking or intending to undertake music tuition; Available to existing and new students from any year level; Students studying a particular instrument; Local students only
special_conditions: Tuition fee remission includes 30-minute lessons with maximum amount each term and instrumental hire for 1 year. Other incidentals and additional lessons are not covered. Recipients must attend weekly lessons, participate in relevant school ensembles and associated performances.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College
overview: The International Scholarship provides financial assistance to International students completing their final years at OLSH College. The scholarship recognises achievement and contribution through academic excellence and involvement in college and community activities.
description: The International Scholarship is offered to International students in their final year of secondary education at OLSH College. The scholarship recognises a student's achievement and contribution through a range of areas including academic achievement and involvement in a range of college and community activities. Being a recipient acknowledges that active involvement, persistence, academic commitment and hard work does pay off. The exact financial value is not specified but provides some financial assistance towards the completion of an international student's high school education. International students often require significant family financial sacrifice to undertake overseas education, and this scholarship is designed to acknowledge such commitment.
eligibility_criteria: International students; Entering Year 12 or in final year of study; Demonstrated academic achievement; Involvement in college and community activities
special_conditions: Scholarship is available to International students only. Recipient must demonstrate ongoing academic achievement and involvement in college and community activities.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College
overview: The John Brewer Memorial Scholarship is available to Year 12 students studying in Catholic schools. This scholarship is offered through the Knights of the Southern Cross and recognises academic commitment and achievement.
description: The John Brewer Memorial Scholarship from the Knights of the Southern Cross is available to Year 12 students currently studying in a Catholic school, including those at OLSH College. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate academic commitment and excellence in their final year of secondary education. The specific financial value and application requirements are managed through the Knights of the Southern Cross organisation. Students should inquire with the college for more details about application procedures and deadlines for this external scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Currently studying in a Catholic school; Academic commitment demonstrated
special_conditions: This scholarship is offered through the Knights of the Southern Cross. Applicants should contact the Knights of the Southern Cross organisation for specific application details and conditions.

school_name: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart College
overview: OLSH College offers flexible financial assistance packages to ensure that fees are not a barrier to a quality Catholic education. The college provides tailored fee structures to support families based on their individual financial circumstances.
description: OLSH College provides specialised financial assistance packages to complement its scholarship offerings and ensure the school provides an excellent and affordable education for all. The college operates a three-band fee structure approach recognising that families have varying financial capacities. Families experiencing financial hardship are invited to contact the Business Manager to discuss fee structures and payment arrangements that may be more suitable to their circumstances. The college is willing to assist financially to help students access education at OLSH. No application test is required; families simply need to discuss their situation with the Business Manager to explore available support options.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Financial hardship consideration; Discussion with Business Manager required
special_conditions: OLSH fees are set each year so families can access quality Catholic education at an affordable price. Fee structure has three band approach. Families should contact Business Manager to discuss arrangements. College is willing to assist financially to achieve educational access goals.

overview: Parafield Gardens High School participates in the South Australian Department of Education STEM scholarship program, which provides support to high school students who are under-represented in Science and Mathematics. The scholarship recognises students who wish to pursue STEM subjects including Engineering, Maths and Science in their senior years.
description: The Department of Education offers STEM scholarships for high school students under-represented in Science and Mathematics. These provide significant monetary support for educational resources to students who wish to pursue STEM subjects like Engineering, Maths and Science in their Senior Years. The application process requires students to complete a written application detailing their plans after school and reasons why they would be a good candidate. Applications are sent from all over the state and reviewed by the education department, with a limited number awarded annually. Recipients are invited to attend an induction day at the University of Adelaide to learn about STEM career pathways and attend workshops.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 11 students; Under-represented in Science and Mathematics; Pursuing or planning to pursue STEM subjects in senior years
special_conditions: Recipients are provided with professional development opportunities including induction days at the University of Adelaide and access to STEM-based workshops featuring keynote speakers from STEM fields. Scholarship includes educational resources and mentoring support.

overview: Parafield Gardens Primary School has a long standing partnership with The Smith Family to assist community members in accessing the Learning for Life Scholarship Program. This program provides financial assistance and educational support to students experiencing disadvantage, helping them to fully participate in their education.
description: The Learning for Life Scholarship Program, administered through The Smith Family, provides financial assistance to support students from low-income families. The program provides scholarship payments of approximately $502 per year per student to help cover educational expenses such as uniforms, shoes, stationery, backpacks, excursions and camps. Recipients work with a Family Partnership Coordinator who provides personal support and mentoring. In addition to financial assistance, The Smith Family runs Learning Centres weekly for families on scholarship to support literacy, numeracy, leadership and computer skills development. The school has direct access to a Family Partnership Coordinator who works on site once a fortnight, ensuring students and families receive coordinated support to engage fully in their education.
eligibility_criteria: Students from families receiving Centrelink concession cards or experiencing financial hardship; Families meeting The Smith Family's financial disadvantage criteria; Open to all year levels at the school
special_conditions: Families must be eligible based on financial disadvantage criteria assessed by The Smith Family. Scholarship recipients participate in Learning Centres and work with a Family Partnership Coordinator to support their educational engagement. Program is available throughout a student's schooling from primary through to tertiary education.

overview: The School Card Scheme is a government financial assistance program available to low-income families with children attending Parafield Gardens Primary School. This scheme provides financial help with school fees, materials and services charges to ensure all students can fully participate in school.
description: The School Card Scheme is a South Australian Department for Education program that offers financial relief for eligible low-income families. The scheme covers materials and services charges at the school. Where school fees are less than the School Card subsidy, families can also access additional help with other educational expenses such as uniforms, camps and excursions. Eligible families are not required to pay any gap between the materials and services fee set by the school and the school card allocation. Applications are made annually, with applications for 2026 currently open. Families must meet specific combined gross income limits based on the number of dependent children. The scheme is administered by the Department for Education, with applications processed through the school's front office.
eligibility_criteria: Low-income families with children attending government schools; Combined family gross income must be below the School Card limits; Families liable for student's school fees; Not available to preschool children; Year 7 and 10 students may also access free devices through the School Device Program if the school participates
special_conditions: Applications must be lodged at the school each year. Families need to provide Centrelink confirmation of customer details and concession card status. Department for Education may use Centrelink Confirmation eServices or direct contact with Services Australia to verify eligibility. Applications close 21 November for the following year. Different income limits apply based on school sector (government, Catholic, or independent).

overview: Pedare Christian College offers the Year 7 Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement and potential in students entering Year 7. The scholarship celebrates excellence in academic achievement and is open to both new and current Pedare students.
description: The Year 7 Academic Scholarship is administered through the Australian Cooperative Entry Program (ACEP) examination conducted by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). The value of the scholarship is up to 50% tuition fee remission at the discretion of the Principal. Applicants are required to demonstrate a range of skills including the ability to interpret, infer, deduce and think critically. The test is not curriculum-based and does not test the ability to retrieve learned knowledge. Scholarships are reviewed at each reporting period and on an annual basis to monitor the student's continuing performance. It is expected that recipients will complete their schooling at Pedare.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2027; Currently in Year 5 in 2025; Must register online for the ACER Level 1 Scholarship Test; Must demonstrate strong critical thinking and reasoning skills; Open to both new students and current Pedare students
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed at each reporting period and on an annual basis to monitor continuing performance. It is expected that recipients will complete their schooling at Pedare. For all scholarships and bursaries, other College charges such as the Resource Fee, Camp and eLearning Levy are to be met by the candidates' parents/caregivers. The ACER scholarship examination will be held at Pedare Christian College, 12-30 Surrey Farm Drive, Golden Grove on Saturday, 7 February 2026.

overview: Pedare Christian College offers Academic Scholarships for Years 10 and 11 to recognise academic excellence in senior secondary students. These scholarships are available to both new students entering the school and current Pedare students progressing to Years 10 and 11.
description: The Years 10 and 11 Academic Scholarship programme recognises students who demonstrate strong academic performance and potential. The value of the scholarship is up to 50% tuition fee remission at the discretion of the Principal. Applicants are selected based on their academic performance and critical thinking abilities assessed through the ACER scholarship examination. Scholarships are reviewed at each reporting period and annually to monitor continuing academic performance. It is expected that recipients will complete their schooling at Pedare. Applications for Years 10 and 11 in 2027 will open in Term 2, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 10 or 11 in 2026 who are new to Pedare; Applications for Years 10 and 11 in 2027 will open in Term 2, 2026; Strong academic record required; Open to both new students and current Pedare students
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed at each reporting period and on an annual basis to monitor the student's continuing performance. It is expected that recipients will complete their schooling at Pedare. For all scholarships and bursaries, other College charges such as the Resource Fee, Camp and eLearning Levy are to be met by the candidates' parents/caregivers.

overview: Pedare Christian College offers Music Scholarships to recognise outstanding individual musical talent and to support the commitment of the College to the Music curriculum and musical life of the College. These scholarships are awarded to students who display exceptional musical ability and maintain a sound academic record.
description: The Pedare Music Scholarship Program recognises individual musical talent and the commitment of the College to its Music curriculum and musical life. The value of the scholarship is up to 50% tuition fee remission at the discretion of the Principal. The Music Scholarships are open to both instrumentalists and vocalists who display outstanding musical ability and a sound academic record. Applicants may have a contemporary, jazz or classical background in music. The College has a strong music culture with Concert Bands, a Stage Band, Choirs, a Jazz vocal ensemble, String Ensembles, a string quartet, percussion and guitar ensembles. The Senior and Junior Musicals are performed biennially. Scholarships are reviewed at each reporting period and annually.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding musical ability required; Sound academic record required; Open to instrumentalists and vocalists; Contemporary, jazz or classical musical background accepted; Limited number awarded each year; Open to new and current Pedare students
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 Music Scholarships are now closed. Scholarships are reviewed at each reporting period and on an annual basis to monitor the student's continuing performance. It is expected that recipients will complete their schooling at Pedare. For all scholarships and bursaries, other College charges such as the Resource Fee, Camp and eLearning Levy are to be met by the candidates' parents/caregivers.

overview: Pedare Christian College offers Excellence Scholarships to acknowledge and encourage excellence across a variety of teaching and learning programs. These scholarships recognise and support students who demonstrate strong skills across multiple key areas and are available to students new to Pedare entering Years 10 or 11.
description: The Excellence Scholarship program recognises and supports students who demonstrate strong skills across a range of key areas including academics, sport, arts, music, robotics, community service, faith and leadership. The value of the scholarships is up to 50% tuition fee remission at the discretion of the Principal. Scholarships provide partial remission of tuition fees and are reviewed at each reporting period. The College particularly seeks students who are aspirational with a diversity of backgrounds, interests, skills and abilities to contribute to the life of the Senior School. A limited number of Excellence Scholarships are awarded each year. It is expected that recipients will complete their schooling at Pedare.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students new to Pedare in 2026 in Years 10 or 11; Demonstrated excellence across multiple areas including academics, sport, arts, music, robotics, community service, faith or leadership; Aspirational students with diverse backgrounds and interests; Sound academic record required
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed at each reporting period and on an annual basis. It is expected that recipients will complete their schooling at Pedare. For all scholarships and bursaries, other College charges such as the Resource Fee, Camp and eLearning Levy are to be met by the candidates' parents/caregivers. Contact the College Registrar, Mrs Julie Holland, on 08 8280 1700 or email jholland@pedarecc.sa.edu.au for more information and an Application Form.

program_name: Academic Excellence Scholarship for Overseas Study
overview: Para Hills High School offers scholarships available through a competitive application process for students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to their studies. This highly sought after opportunity has proven successful in fostering academic excellence and cultural exchange, particularly for overseas study experiences.
description: Scholarships are available through an application process for students who demonstrate high commitment to their studies. This opportunity is highly successful and sought after. The program includes access to international exchange opportunities, particularly the annual trip to Japan as part of the sister school exchange with Kashima. Scholarships support students' participation in overseas study programs. Selection is through a competitive application process evaluating academic commitment and achievement. The scholarship provides financial support for study abroad opportunities but specific monetary values are not published.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate exceptional commitment to studies; Must meet high academic achievement standards; Open to students in Years 7-12; Participation in competitive application process required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are available through a competitive application process. This is a highly successful and sought after opportunity. Specific application timelines and detailed conditions are available from the school.

program_name: South Australian Aboriginal Secondary Training Academy (SAASTA)
overview: Para Hills High School hosts the South Australian Aboriginal Secondary Training Academy (SAASTA), a selective entry program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. SAASTA offers young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students a range of highly engaging learning and personal development opportunities.
description: SAASTA is a selective entry program designed for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in senior years. The program provides a curriculum focused on healthy living, cultural identity, Aboriginal language, traditional sports and respectful relationships. SAASTA Connect is available to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in Years 8 and 9, providing a pathway for entry into the SAASTA program in senior years. The program incorporates learning on and about country, engagement with elders and the local Indigenous community, and includes the Ngarrindjeri language as a key part of the curriculum. No direct financial scholarships are mentioned, but the program provides significant educational and personal development support. Selection is through a selective entry process.
eligibility_criteria: Must identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander; SAASTA Connect available to Years 8-9 students; Entry into senior SAASTA program via pathway from SAASTA Connect; Open to students demonstrating commitment to cultural engagement and personal development
special_conditions: Selective entry program. SAASTA Connect curriculum includes culturally appropriate resources. The Ngarrindjeri language is taught in collaboration with Aboriginal persons with extensive knowledge of Ngarrindjeri culture. Students engage with local elders and Indigenous community. The school won the 2023 Public Education Award for Excellence in Collaborative Partnerships with 100% SACE achievement and attendance rate of 89%.

overview: Para Hills High School offers the Unlocking Potential (UP) program, a special interest pathway for students passionate about emerging technologies. The program is designed for students in Years 7 to 10 who want to extend their skills in robotics, 3D printing, Virtual Reality, Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence.
description: Unlocking Potential (UP) is a special interest program available to students in Years 7 to 10 who are passionate about emerging technologies and want to extend their skills beyond the standard curriculum. Students learn in anything from robotics to 3D printing to Virtual Reality, Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence. The program is available to a limited number of places for students within and out of zone. Both the UP program and PHHS Arts Company are available through an application process. Details of the application process will be available from the school. No direct financial scholarship or fee remission is mentioned as this is a government school. Selection is through a competitive application process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-10; Must be passionate about emerging technologies; Application process required; Limited places available for students within and out of zone
special_conditions: Limited number of places available. Students within and out of zone can apply. Application process details to be confirmed. This is a special interest pathway program, not a fee-based scholarship program.

overview: Para Hills High School offers the Para Hills Performing Arts Company, a special interest program available to students in Years 7 to 11. The program provides an opportunity for students to immerse themselves in the diverse and vibrant world of the performing arts.
description: The Para Hills Performing Arts Company provides an opportunity for students to immerse themselves in diverse and vibrant performing arts. In the program, students collaborate across year levels to make productions from start to finish. They undertake different roles, ranging from off-stage production elements to performance, developing relevant skills and knowledge for performing arts pathways. The program includes close work with the local community and integration of hands-on engagement with contemporary practitioners and industry professionals, giving students a comprehensive understanding of opportunities in the performing arts world. Limited places are available for students within and out of zone through a competitive application process. No direct financial scholarships are offered as this is a government school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-11; Application process required; Limited places available for students within and out of zone; Demonstrated interest and commitment to performing arts required
special_conditions: Limited number of places available. Students within and out of zone can apply. Applicants will engage with local community and contemporary practitioners. Program includes off-stage production roles and performance opportunities. Details of the application process will be available from the school.

school_name: SA School and Services for Vision Impaired

program_name: School Card - Low Income Family Support
overview: Rosary School participates in the State Government's School Card program to provide fee assistance for families experiencing financial hardship. The program offers reduced tuition fees for eligible low-income families, making Catholic education accessible regardless of financial circumstances.
description: The School Card is a state-based financial assistance program administered through the South Australian Government for families with financial need. To be eligible in 2026, a family's gross income must be below approximately $74,189 for a family with one school-age child, with varying thresholds based on the number of dependent children. Eligible families receive a reduced tuition fee and must apply on an annual basis with proof of income. The school's Finance Team provides support to families navigating the application process through the State Government's School Card portal. This program recognizes that family financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing quality Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Family gross income below approximately $74,189 for one school-age child in 2026; Income threshold varies based on number of dependent children; Proof of income required; Applications made annually; Must qualify as low-income family as determined by State Government thresholds
special_conditions: Applications must be made annually through the State Government's School Card system. Proof of income is required. Eligible families receive a lower tuition fee structure set by the school in accordance with government guidelines. The school Business Manager is available to assist families with all fee-related enquiries and application process.

overview: Rosary School offers sibling discounts for families with more than one student enrolled at the school. This program aims to reduce the overall financial burden on families with multiple children in the school.
description: Families with more than one student attending Rosary School are eligible for discounted tuition fees on additional siblings. The exact discount percentage is determined by the school and applied to the second and subsequent children's fees. This discount recognizes the financial commitment families make when supporting multiple children's education and provides cost relief. The discount applies to the regular tuition fee and is available alongside other payment options and financial assistance programs. Families should contact the school Business Manager for specific discount amounts and how they apply to their individual family situation.
eligibility_criteria: More than one student from the same family enrolled at Rosary School; Applies to second and subsequent siblings; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Discount is automatic for families with multiple students enrolled. Applies to tuition fees only and does not affect technology fees or other separate charges. Discount applies in conjunction with other fee payment options and financial assistance programs available to the family.

overview: Rosary School offers a 5% discount on net tuition fees for families who pay their annual fees in full by 28 February 2026. This program incentivizes early payment and provides financial relief to families able to pay upfront.
description: Families choosing to pay their total annual tuition fees in full by 28 February 2026 receive a 5% discount on net tuition fees. This discount does not apply to the annual Technology Fee of $100 per student or other separately invoiced charges such as extracurricular activities, camps, and uniforms. The early payment option provides an incentive for families managing their school fee budget and offers meaningful financial savings. This flexible payment approach acknowledges different family financial situations while encouraging advance planning. Payment must be received and cleared by the deadline. Families unable to pay in full by the deadline may elect regular direct debit payment options starting no later than 28 February 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Payment of total tuition fees by 28 February 2026; Discount applies to net tuition fees only; Open to all families; Does not apply to Technology Fee or other separately charged items
special_conditions: Discount available only for payment in full by 28 February 2026. Payment must be received and fully cleared by the deadline. Technology Fee ($100 per student) is not eligible for the discount and is invoiced separately. Extracurricular activities, camps, and uniforms are excluded from this discount as they are charged separately. Direct debit payments can be set up for families unable to pay in full.

overview: Rosary School offers flexible payment plan options through Direct Debit arrangements to help families manage annual tuition fees across the school year. Families can spread payments weekly, fortnightly, or monthly to suit their budget.
description: Families unable to pay fees in full by 28 February 2026 can establish a Direct Debit payment arrangement with regular deductions from their bank account. Payment plans allow families to spread annual tuition costs across weekly, fortnightly, or monthly installments, starting no later than 28 February 2026 and finalised by 31 October 2026. The Direct Debit Request Form must be completed by the school's deadline. This flexible approach acknowledges varying family financial circumstances and allows families to budget across the school year rather than paying a lump sum. Families can also use BPAY (Biller Code 299040) as an alternative payment method. The school Business Manager is available to assist families in setting up their preferred payment arrangement and answer all fee-related enquiries.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Direct Debit must commence no later than 28 February 2026; Payment to be finalised by 31 October 2026; Families must complete Direct Debit Request Form; Can choose weekly, fortnightly, or monthly payment frequency
special_conditions: Direct Debit arrangement is the preferred payment option for families unable to pay in full. Regular deductions are made from the family's bank account. BPAY is available as an alternative payment method with Biller Code 299040. Families must contact the School Business Manager on 8343 5700 to arrange their preferred payment plan. Payment must begin by 28 February 2026 and be finalised by 31 October 2026 for the current year.

overview: Roma Mitchell Secondary College offers scholarships for the International Baccalaureate Diploma Program to Year 11 and 12 students. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to the rigorous IB curriculum, with special consideration given to diversity and financial need.
description: Roma Mitchell Secondary College has scholarships available for the Diploma Program delivered to Years 11 and 12 students. The IB Diploma Program is a globally recognised curriculum that prepares students for success in university and beyond. Special consideration will be given to all or any of the following factors: social and cultural diversity; ability in demonstrating resilience; and financial need. Students must be currently enrolled in Year 10 or entering the program from other schools at the start of Year 11. The selection process involves academic assessment and demonstrated commitment to IB principles. For more information on IB scholarships, applicants should email Lorraine.Securo808@schools.sa.edu.au or submit an enquiry through the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 and Year 11 students entering IB Diploma Program; Must demonstrate strong academic ability; Special consideration given for social and cultural diversity; Ability to demonstrate resilience; Financial need may be considered
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to demonstrated commitment to the IB Diploma Program and maintaining academic standards throughout enrollment. Special consideration given to students demonstrating social and cultural diversity and financial need.

overview: Roma Mitchell Secondary College's Sports Academy offers competitive selection into high-quality mentoring and training programs for Years 7 to 12 students. The program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional sporting skills and the values of respect, diversity and excellence.
description: Roma Mitchell Secondary College's Sports Academy offers successful Year 7-12 applicants high-quality mentoring and training programs in soccer, basketball, volleyball, cricket and AFLW. Selection into the Roma Mitchell Sports Academy is highly competitive. Students who are accepted into the program demonstrate not only advanced sporting skills, but also the attitude and behaviours that reflect the values of the Academy. As part of the application process, students are required to participate in trials to demonstrate skill level, game sense, fitness, and coachability. Sports Academy students develop their sporting prowess through participation in a range of state and national level competitions, while undertaking a specialised curriculum that focuses on skill development, coaching, officiating, strength and conditioning, and preparing students for professional sporting pathways, tertiary study, or the workforce. The school works in partnership with industry sports associations including the South Australian Cricket Association (SACA), South Australian Women's Football (AFLW), Basketball South Australia (BSA), Volleyball South Australia (VSA) and Football South Australia (FSA).
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 to Year 12 students; Must demonstrate advanced sporting skills; Must demonstrate attitude and behaviours reflecting Academy values of Respect, Diversity and Excellence; Must successfully complete trials; Selection is highly competitive
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Participation in trials is mandatory. Students must maintain conduct standards reflecting school values. Specialized curriculum includes skill development, coaching, officiating and strength and conditioning. Students are mentored by highly skilled coaches and industry leaders. Uniform provided for program participants.

overview: Roma Mitchell Secondary College offers a Gifted and Talented Program for students in Years 8 to 12 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and intellectual potential. The program recognises students who show advanced cognitive skills and capacity for independent learning.
description: Roma Mitchell Secondary College delivers a Gifted and Talented Program to students in Years 8 to 12 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and intellectual potential. The program is designed to provide enriched and accelerated learning opportunities for highly able students. Students in this program engage with a specialised curriculum that challenges them to think critically, conduct research, and develop advanced problem-solving skills. Selection into the program is selective and competitive, based on demonstrated academic excellence and cognitive ability. The program aims to nurture curiosity, develop inquiry-based learning, and prepare gifted students for advanced academic pathways. Students benefit from specialized teaching approaches aligned to their individual learning needs and potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 8 to 12; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Must show advanced intellectual potential; Selection is competitive and based on academic performance and cognitive assessment
special_conditions: Program selection based on demonstrated academic excellence and cognitive ability testing. Enrollment is competitive. Students are expected to maintain high standards of engagement and academic performance throughout program participation.

program_name: South Australian Aboriginal Sports Training Academy (SAASTA)
overview: Roma Mitchell Secondary College hosts the South Australian Aboriginal Sports Training Academy (SAASTA), a program providing Aboriginal secondary students with unique sporting and educational opportunities in Years 10 to 12. The program recognises Aboriginal students and supports their development through sport, leadership and cultural connection.
description: The South Australian Aboriginal Sports Training Academy (SAASTA) at Roma Mitchell Secondary College provides Aboriginal high school students in Years 10 to 12 with a unique sporting and educational program. Students attend their SAASTA specialist academy 1 day per week and attend their home school (Roma Mitchell) for the remainder of the school week. Students spend half their academy day focused on their specialist area in sport, and the other half on their SACE curriculum. Subjects include Aboriginal Studies, Indigenous Leadership, Sports Science, Scientific Studies and Community Learning. The program aims to give students the skills, opportunities and confidence to dream, believe and achieve in the areas of education, employment, healthy living and connection with their culture. SAASTA supports students throughout secondary school and helps set them up for successful post-school transitions. Year 10-12 students enrolled in SAASTA are supplied with some items of the sport uniform. Students need to arrange their own transport to and from the program each week.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Aboriginal students in Years 10 to 12; Must be identified as Aboriginal; Competitive selection process; Students must demonstrate commitment to engaging in culturally responsive education program
special_conditions: Program attendance is 1 day per week at SAASTA specialist academy. Students attend home school (Roma Mitchell) for remainder of week. Students must arrange own transport to and from program. Some items of sport uniform are supplied. Program includes SACE-credited subjects. Students learn in culturally safe environment with other Aboriginal students and Aboriginal mentors.

overview: Rostrevor College offers the CMEK Scholarship to new or existing students entering Year 7 who demonstrate high achievement and outstanding academic aptitude. Supported by a bequest made to the College in 1959, this scholarship recognises exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning across all aspects of college life.
description: The Catherine Mary Enright Kelly (CMEK) Scholarship is supported by a bequest made to Rostrevor College in 1959 by Mr Thomas D Kelly M.S, F.R.C.S, F.R.A.C.S, F.I.C.S, an Old Collegian who graduated in the Honours Class of 1928. Mr Kelly named the bequeathment in honour of his late mother, Mrs Catherine Mary Enright Kelly. The scholarship is awarded to new or existing students entering Year 7 who demonstrate high achievement and outstanding academic aptitude across all aspects of College life. Recipients receive financial assistance towards tuition fees, the value and duration to be determined based on the bequest funds available. Selection involves assessment of the student's academic performance and all-round contribution to the College. The scholarship supports the College's mission to make a Rostrevor education accessible to Australia's young learners.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing student; Entering Year 7; Demonstrate high achievement and outstanding academic aptitude; Meet College enrolment criteria regarding philosophical and religious disposition
special_conditions: Applications for 2027 close at 4:30pm Wednesday 18 March 2026. Scholarship is subject to annual review of student's educational performance and general participation in College life. Student must maintain compliance with College conduct expectations.

program_name: Dr Bernard Cormie OAM Academic Scholarship – STEM
overview: Rostrevor College offers the Dr Bernard Cormie OAM Academic Scholarship – STEM to new or existing students entering Year 10 who demonstrate high achievement and outstanding aptitude for STEM subjects. Named in recognition of Dr Bernard Cormie OAM, who established the College's scholarship committee, this award supports students with exceptional ability in science, technology, engineering and mathematics.
description: The Dr Bernard Cormie OAM Academic Scholarship – STEM is named in recognition of Dr Bernard Cormie OAM, who formed the inaugural Rostrevor College Scholarship Committee to manage funds for academic scholarships bequeathed by Old Collegian, Dr Thomas Kelly ('28). Dr Cormie taught at Rostrevor in the 1960s before pursuing a career in medicine, and then volunteered on the Scholarship Committee for 30 years with his two sons attending Rostrevor in the late 1980s and early 1990s. This scholarship is awarded to new or existing students entering Year 10 who demonstrate high achievement and outstanding aptitude for STEM subjects. Recipients receive financial assistance towards tuition fees based on available bequest funds. The selection process evaluates the student's academic performance in science and mathematics, commitment to STEM disciplines, and overall contribution to the College. The scholarship reflects the College's commitment to nurturing talented students who might otherwise lack financial means to attend.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing student; Entering Year 10; Demonstrate high achievement and outstanding aptitude for STEM subjects; Meet College enrolment criteria regarding philosophical and religious disposition
special_conditions: Applications for 2027 close at 4:30pm Wednesday 18 March 2026. Scholarship is subject to annual review of student's educational performance and engagement with STEM subjects. Student must maintain compliance with College conduct expectations and continue to demonstrate aptitude in STEM disciplines.

overview: Rostrevor College awards the ROCA Scholarship to new or existing students entering Year 7 or 10 who are sons or grandsons of Old Collegians whose families may lack financial means to attend. The College also welcomes applications from students who are not descendants of Old Collegians but may have financial need.
description: The Rostrevor Old Collegians' Association (ROCA) Scholarship is awarded to new or existing students entering Year 7 or 10 who are sons and grandsons of Old Collegians to attend Rostrevor, whose family might not otherwise have the financial means to support them. Applications from students who are not the son or grandson of an Old Collegian, and who may not have the financial means to attend Rostrevor without assistance, are also welcome to apply. The scholarship reflects the College's values of a strong focus on community and remaining connected for life through the thriving Rostrevor Old Collegian network. Recipients receive financial assistance towards tuition fees, with the value and duration determined based on assessed financial need and available scholarship funds. The selection process considers both family financial circumstances and the student's potential to benefit from a Rostrevor education.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing student; Entering Year 7 or Year 10; Son or grandson of an Old Collegian, OR demonstrates financial need; Family circumstances may make full fee payment difficult; Meet College enrolment criteria regarding philosophical and religious disposition
special_conditions: Applications for 2027 close Friday 1 May 2026. Scholarship is subject to annual review of financial circumstances. Student must maintain compliance with College conduct expectations and satisfactory educational performance. Recipients are expected to engage positively with the College community and uphold the values of ROCA.

program_name: The Smith Family Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP) Scholarship
overview: Rostrevor College is a proud education provider for students offered places in The Smith Family's Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP). The program offers scholarships and leadership opportunities to young First Nations Australians, with a focus on Indigenous youth from remote communities to extend their educational choices and opportunities.
description: The Smith Family Indigenous Youth Leadership Program (IYLP) Scholarship provides comprehensive support to Indigenous students at Rostrevor College. Funded by the National Indigenous Australians Agency and The Smith Family, the IYLP provides a bridge in the gap in costs between other funding such as Abstudy, covering essential tuition fees and education costs. In addition, the fund may provide support to students for non-compulsory education expenses such as excursions, camps, and co-curricular activities. The program combines financial assistance with leadership development, offering young First Nations Australians the opportunity to extend their educational choices and participate fully in College life. Students are encouraged to apply approximately 12 months prior to commencement by lodging an application with The Smith Family.
eligibility_criteria: Young First Nations Australian; Preference for Indigenous youth from remote communities; Application to The Smith Family IYLP program; Concurrent enrolment application to Rostrevor College required
special_conditions: Students must apply approximately 12 months prior to commencement. Applications lodged directly with The Smith Family. An Enrolment Application Form for Rostrevor College must be submitted at the same time as the IYLP application. The scholarship includes financial support for both compulsory and non-compulsory education expenses.

overview: Rostrevor College encourages families eligible for the SA Government's School Card scheme to apply. The College provides a 40% concession on tuition fees to eligible families who meet the government's means-tested criteria for financial assistance.
description: The School Card Concession is a government means-tested scheme for low-income families administered through the South Australian Department for Education. Rostrevor College participates in this scheme and provides a 40% concession on tuition fees to families who are eligible. School Card application forms are available from the College Finance Office in January, and an application must be completed each year. Eligible families can significantly reduce their annual tuition fee commitments through this program. The College encourages all families who believe they may qualify to apply, as this is an important means of making a Rostrevor education accessible. For families qualifying for School Card, all other school fees and charges remain the responsibility of the family.
eligibility_criteria: Family meets government means-tested criteria for low-income support; Application completed in January; Must reapply each year; All years of enrolment
special_conditions: Government means-tested scheme. Applications must be completed each year. Only applies to tuition fees; other charges remain payable by family. For more information visit: https://www.sa.gov.au/topics/education-and-learning/financial-help-scholarships-and-grants/school-card-scheme

overview: Rostrevor College offers fee support and bursary assistance to families who do not qualify for School Card entitlements but who may find it difficult to meet full fee commitments. The College recognises various talents and capacities of students and their families' financial circumstances.
description: Rostrevor College is able to provide fee support for families who do not qualify for School Card entitlements but who may find it difficult to meet full fee commitments. Support may be provided via fee remission and/or extended payment terms. The College recognises various talents of a student and his capacity to benefit from the nurturing of these gifts in Rostrevor's educational environment. Bursaries are subject to the quality of the family and the student's compliance and contribution, the student's educational performance and general participation in College life. Families requesting financial assistance are asked to complete an Application for Fee Remission. This provides the College with information to ensure that assistance is provided to families most in need. A meeting with the Business Manager would usually be arranged for the same day as the enrolment. Once a student has commenced at Rostrevor College, bursaries are subject to an annual review of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Family does not qualify for School Card; Financial difficulty meeting full fee commitments; Completion of Application for Fee Remission; Meeting with Business Manager; Student demonstrates positive participation in College life; Satisfactory educational performance
special_conditions: Applications for fee remission must be submitted to the Enrolment Office. Bursaries subject to annual review of financial circumstances. Family must provide financial information for assessment. All fee arrangements remain confidential to Principal and Finance Office. Students receiving bursaries must meet philosophical and religious disposition criteria of the College. Families required to pay all other charges including uniforms, stationery, excursions, and music tuition costs.

overview: Salisbury East High School offers the Specialist Soccer program to develop and assist the performance and progress of students with a high-level interest in soccer. The program runs from Years 8 to 12 and is designed to reach their potential through integrated sport and academic learning.
description: The Specialist Soccer program was created in 2015 and runs from Years 8 to 12. Students in this program are designed to develop and assist the performance and progress of those involved to reach their potential. The program integrates soccer performance with concepts related to sport, physical activity, healthy lifestyles, and science/technology in sport. Applicants require a high-level interest in soccer and a desire to learn about related concepts. There is no stated monetary value for this program. Selection is based on demonstrated interest and aptitude in soccer combined with commitment to learning about sport science and technology. The program duration is Years 8 to 12 (for duration of enrolment in the program).
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 8; High-level interest in soccer required; Desire to learn about concepts related to sport, physical activity, healthy lifestyles, and science/technology in sport
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate a high-level interest in soccer and a desire to learn about concepts related to sport, physical activity, healthy lifestyles, and science/technology in sport.

overview: Salisbury East High School offers the Specialist Music program to students interested in developing their musical abilities. The program includes weekly lessons delivered by Instrumental Music teachers at no cost to eligible students.
description: The Specialist Music program provides students with access to weekly instrumental music lessons delivered by qualified Instrumental Music (IM) teachers at no cost. Students are eligible for professional-level music instruction as part of their school studies. The program operates across all year levels at the school and provides a non-monetary benefit through free instrumental music tuition. This represents a significant value to families who would otherwise need to pay for private music lessons. The program develops musical performance skills through guided instruction from specialist music educators. Continuation in the program is contingent on active participation and engagement with the music curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Interest in music performance required; Students must be enrolled at Salisbury East High School
special_conditions: Weekly lessons delivered by Instrumental Music teachers. Students are eligible for lessons at no cost.

overview: Salisbury East High School participates in the SA Government School Card scheme, which provides financial assistance to low-income families. The scheme helps eligible families pay for school fees, materials and services charges.
description: The School Card scheme is a South Australian Government program that provides financial assistance to low-income families to help pay for school fees, materials and services charges at government schools. Salisbury East High School accepts applications from eligible families and encourages parents to apply as soon as possible. The financial assistance is means-tested based on family income. For the 2025 school year, the Materials and Services Charge is $470, with a State Government rebate of $200, meaning the amount payable is $270 for families not eligible for School Card. Eligible families under School Card receive support with these charges. The scheme is administered by the South Australian Government and families can access support through the SA Gov website.
eligibility_criteria: Low-income family status; Must meet School Card income thresholds set by SA Government; Requires application to scheme; Applicable to all year levels
special_conditions: Application required; Income-based eligibility criteria apply; Families encouraged to apply as soon as possible; Payment plan arrangements available if needed; Administered through SA Government.

school_name: Saint Ignatius' College - Junior School Campus
overview: Saint Ignatius College offers Academic Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement or show significant potential in their studies. These scholarships are awarded to students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11 based on performance in the ACER Scholarship Test.
description: The College offers Academic Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement or show significant potential in their studies. Applicants are required to register online to sit an Australian Cooperative Entry Program (ACEP) Scholarship Test, which is administered and marked by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). The tests are not curriculum-based and do not test the ability to retrieve learned knowledge, nor are they diagnostic. Applicants are required to demonstrate a range of skills such as the ability to interpret, infer, deduce, and think critically. The College awards scholarships on the basis of performance in the test. Applications for Academic Scholarships for students who will enter Year 7, Year 10 or Year 11 in 2028 will open in late September 2026. The financial value is not publicly specified on the scholarship page.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement or significant potential in studies; Applicants must register online to sit the ACER Scholarship Test
special_conditions: The College will award scholarships on the basis of performance in the test. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic and conduct standards.

school_name: Saint Ignatius' College - Junior School Campus
overview: Saint Ignatius College offers Music Scholarships to students who are able to display established excellence or significant promise in music. Music Scholarships are open to instrumentalists and vocalists and will be awarded through audition to those who display outstanding musical ability and a sound academic record.
description: Saint Ignatius College offers scholarships to instrumentalists and vocalists who display outstanding musical ability and a sound academic record. A minimum of two years' experience on their instrument or an AMEB equivalent of Grade 4 is required. The ability to play more than one instrument is desirable. Applicants may have a classical, contemporary, or jazz background in music. Recipients are expected to study music as a timetabled curriculum subject up to and including Year 11 and participate in at least two co-curricular music ensembles. An annual review of the student's progress and contribution will be made. Applications for Music Scholarships for students entering Year 7 in 2028 will open in late September 2026. The application fee is $50.00. Music Scholarship students will be students with a high level of accomplishment on their chosen instrument.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must display outstanding musical ability and sound academic record; Minimum two years' experience on instrument or AMEB equivalent of Grade 4 required; Classical, contemporary, or jazz background acceptable
special_conditions: Recipients are expected to study music as a timetabled curriculum subject up to and including Year 11. Recipients are strongly encouraged to continue music through Year 12. Recipients must participate in at least two co-curricular music ensembles including rehearsal, workshop and performance up to Year 12. An annual review of the student's progress and contribution will be made. If progress is not satisfactory, the terms of the scholarship may be reviewed. Scholarship recipients are expected to remain at the College for the duration of their scholarship. Departure from the College during the term of the scholarship, unless at the request of the College or mutually agreed, may require reimbursement to the College of the value of the scholarship.

school_name: Saint Ignatius' College - Junior School Campus
overview: Saint Ignatius College offers Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate a high level of achievement in and contribution to co-curricular sports or activities, faith and service, sustainability, or other areas of excellence. Excellence Scholarships are open to new and current students for Years 7 to 12.
description: Saint Ignatius College offers Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate a high level of achievement in and contribution to one or more of the following areas: co-curricular sports or activities, faith and service, sustainability, and other designated areas of excellence. Students may be eligible if they demonstrate the ability to perform in an outstanding and consistent manner in the field of a particular passion or area of talent within the specified categories. Successful applicants will be required to participate in and provide leadership in at least one co-curricular activity that relates to their area of excellence. Students will also be expected to study a related curriculum subject at the College until at least the end of SACE Stage 1, maintaining an exceptional academic record. Scholarships are reviewed annually based on students' attainment of the criteria. The application deadline was 3 February 2025 and the application fee applies. Excellence Scholarships are not available to overseas students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current students for Years 7 to 12; Must demonstrate high level of achievement in co-curricular sports or activities, faith and service, sustainability, or other designated areas; Must demonstrate ability to perform in outstanding and consistent manner in field of particular passion or talent; Not available to overseas students
special_conditions: Successful applicants must participate in and provide leadership in at least one co-curricular activity that relates to the area of excellence. Students must study a related curriculum subject at the College until at least the end of SACE Stage 1, maintaining an exceptional academic record. Scholarships are reviewed annually based on students' attainment of the criteria. Recipients are expected to complete their scholarship for the duration of their enrolment.

school_name: Saint Ignatius' College - Junior School Campus
overview: Saint Ignatius College offers means-tested bursaries to families seeking a Catholic education in the Ignatian tradition but whose current financial circumstances prevent them from accessing education through the standard fee structure. Bursaries are intended to provide an opportunity to Catholic families whose commitment to their faith is evident to all in the community.
description: In keeping with its Jesuit heritage and the belief that Saint Ignatius wanted his schools to be open to all classes of people, rich and poor alike, Saint Ignatius College offers bursaries to families whose financial circumstances make fee requirements unsustainable. The principal criterion for the provision of financial assistance from the Bursary Fund is a demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford an Ignatian education. In addition to financial need, consideration is also given to people of a refugee and/or First Peoples background. The College has substantial responsibilities to ensure that Bursary Fund monies are distributed with equity, confidentiality and compassion at all times. Each case is assessed on its merits and, where appropriate, the Rector and Principal exercise their discretionary power to apply financial assistance. Families must submit all appropriate documentation, including financial records, in support of the application within a stipulated period. Bursaries are reviewed on an annual basis.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated means-tested inability to afford an Ignatian education; Catholic families whose commitment to their faith is evident; Consideration given to people of refugee and/or First Peoples background; Preference given to families with good fit in terms of academic aptitude, co-curricular interests and social skills
special_conditions: Bursaries are not related to excellence, academic or music scholarship programs. The Bursary Fund is limited and generally offers partial tuition assistance only. Families applying for bursaries must submit financial records with their enrolment application. Bursary Fund monies are distributed with equity, confidentiality and compassion. The Rector and Principal exercise discretionary power to apply financial assistance. Bursaries are reviewed annually to ensure students are meeting the criteria.

overview: Samaritan College offers financial hardship remission to families experiencing genuine financial difficulties. The program is designed to support families who cannot reasonably meet their fee obligations due to illness, unemployment, or other extraordinary circumstances, ensuring education remains accessible.
description: Families not eligible for the School Card who are experiencing genuine financial hardship are encouraged to submit a Financial Scholarship Application found on the School's website or collect a hard copy at the School. All applications will be assessed on an individual basis. This program is sensitive and highly confidential in its application process. The amount of remission varies depending on individual circumstances and family financial situation. Applications may be submitted at any time during the school year as hardship can occur at different points.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing genuine financial hardship; Unable to reasonably meet fee obligations due to circumstances beyond control; All year levels eligible; Application assessed on individual basis
special_conditions: Applications are treated with sensitivity and confidentiality. Assessment is on an individual basis. Families should contact the Senior Finance Officer to discuss their situation.

overview: Samaritan College participates in the South Australian School Card Scheme, a government-funded program that provides fee assistance to families meeting income eligibility thresholds. This program ensures families on low incomes can access quality education at the college.
description: Families eligible under the School Card Scheme are requested to submit a School Card application as early as possible. It is important that parents and caregivers work with the school to complete the necessary documentation. Families eligible and approved for School Card will receive a 75% reduction on their tuition fees. Income threshold details can be obtained from the Senior Finance Officer or at the South Australian government's education and learning website. For 2025, income thresholds for single child families are $74,189, with thresholds increasing depending on the number of children attending school. The application process is coordinated through the school.
eligibility_criteria: Meet South Australian School Card Scheme income thresholds; Single child family threshold $74,189 (2025); Additional children increase threshold; Must apply through School Card Scheme process; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Administered through South Australian Government School Card Scheme. Income thresholds reviewed annually. Families encouraged to apply early in the school year. Work with school to complete necessary documentation.

school_name: Samaritan College - Our Lady Help of Christians Campus
overview: Samaritan College offers Financial Scholarships to families experiencing genuine hardship who are unable to pay tuition fees. The scholarship provides fee remissions to assist eligible families while maintaining the confidentiality and dignity of recipients.
description: Financial Scholarships are provided to families not eligible for School Card who are genuinely unable to pay tuition fees. Applications must be completed annually and a discount will be provided in accordance with the family's individual circumstances. The application process is sensitive and highly confidential to ensure recipients are not identified publicly through school practices such as book collections and excursions. Families seeking financial assistance should contact the Senior Finance Officer at the college. Support is provided in accordance with Samaritan College's Vision and Mission statement and the SACCS Policy. Remissions are available to families in the event of financial hardship, with the exact level of support determined on a case-by-case basis following individual family assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Families not eligible for School Card scheme; Demonstrable financial hardship; Annual application required; Family circumstances assessed individually
special_conditions: Applications must be completed annually. Details of families' financial circumstances remain confidential to those authorised to access the information. Students in receipt of financial scholarships should not be identified publicly through any school practices. The college works closely with families to ensure a parent/caregiver's inability to pay fees does not impact on their child's participation in school activities.

school_name: Samaritan College - Our Lady Help of Christians Campus
overview: Samaritan College supports families eligible under South Australia's School Card Scheme, which provides subsidised school fees for eligible low-income families. Eligible families receive a 75% reduction on tuition fees.
description: Families eligible and approved for School Card receive a 75% reduction on their tuition fees. The School Card Scheme is a South Australian government program designed to support low-income families with school fee costs. Income thresholds for single child families in 2025 are $74,189, with thresholds increasing depending on the number of children attending school. Families eligible under the School Card Scheme are requested to submit a School Card application as early as possible to the college. Information about income thresholds and the application process can be obtained from the Senior Finance Officer at the college or at the official SA government website for the School Card Scheme. It is important that parents and caregivers work with the school to complete the necessary documentation to access this 75% fee reduction benefit.
eligibility_criteria: Family income below specified threshold (2025: $74,189 for single child, increases with number of school children); Approval by SA government School Card Scheme; Application submitted to college; Income assessment by government
special_conditions: Application must be submitted to the college as early as possible. Income thresholds vary based on number of school-aged children in the family. Families must meet SA government School Card Scheme eligibility criteria. Parents and caregivers must work with the school to complete necessary documentation.

overview: Samaritan College offers Financial Scholarships to families experiencing genuine financial hardship who are not eligible for the government School Card scheme. The scholarship provides tuition fee reductions determined on an individual basis according to the family's circumstances.
description: Financial Scholarships are available to families who are not eligible for the School Card scheme but are experiencing genuine financial hardship and unable to pay full tuition fees. Applications must be submitted annually using the Financial Scholarship Application form found on the school's website or available as a hard copy from the school. All applications are assessed on an individual basis, with fee reduction amounts varying according to each family's individual circumstances. The application process is sensitive and highly confidential. Families experiencing hardship are assisted by the school in completing necessary documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Not eligible for School Card scheme; experiencing genuine financial hardship; unable to pay full tuition fees; annual reapplication required
special_conditions: Applications must be completed annually. Verifiable financial information is required. Families granted a Financial Scholarship are required to arrange direct debit payments. Students in receipt of Financial Scholarship should not be identified publicly through school practices such as book collections and excursions.

overview: Samaritan College participates in the South Australian School Card scheme, a government-funded program providing tuition fee assistance to eligible low-income families. Approved families receive a 75% reduction on their tuition fees.
description: The School Card scheme is a government-supported financial assistance program administered through the South Australian government. Families meeting the government income thresholds are eligible to apply for School Card assistance, which provides a 75% reduction on tuition fees. For 2025, income thresholds for single child families are $74,189, with thresholds increasing based on the number of children attending school. Families must apply annually and work with the school to complete the necessary documentation. Income threshold details can be obtained from the school's Senior Finance Officer or through the South Australian government website.
eligibility_criteria: Family income below government-set thresholds; for 2025 single child families threshold is $74,189; thresholds increase with number of children; annual reapplication required; must be approved by government
special_conditions: Families must apply annually. Applications processed through government scheme. School Card bursar available to discuss process with eligible families. Students in receipt of School Card should not be identified publicly through school practices such as book collections and excursions.

school_name: Saint Ignatius' College - Senior School Campus
overview: Saint Ignatius' College offers Academic Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement or show significant potential in their studies. Applicants are assessed through an ACER-administered test and scholarships are awarded based on performance. The program recognises excellence in core academic areas.
description: The Academic Scholarship program is open to students entering Year 7, Year 10, and Year 11. Applicants must register online and sit an Australian Cooperative Entry Program (ACEP) Scholarship Test, which is administered and marked by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). The test assesses the ability to interpret, infer, deduce, and think critically. Tests are not curriculum-based and do not test retrieval of learned knowledge. The College awards scholarships on the basis of performance in the test. Scholarships provide fee assistance for the duration of enrolment, though specific percentage or amount is determined by College and not publicly disclosed. Application deadlines have passed for 2027 entry; applications for 2028 entry will open in late September 2026.
