eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement or significant potential; Must sit the ACEP Scholarship Test; Not available to overseas students
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on test performance. The College will award scholarships on the basis of performance in the test. Departure from the College during the term of the scholarship, unless at the request of the College or mutually agreed, may require reimbursement to the College of the value of the scholarship.

school_name: Saint Ignatius' College - Senior School Campus
overview: Saint Ignatius' College offers Music Scholarships to instrumentalists and vocalists who display outstanding musical ability and a sound academic record. Scholarships are awarded through audition to students who demonstrate exceptional accomplishment on their chosen instrument or as a vocalist. The program recognises excellence in music performance and commitment to the broader music life of the College.
description: Music Scholarships are open to instrumentalists and vocalists entering Year 7 and are awarded through audition to those who display outstanding musical ability and a sound academic record. Applicants must have a minimum of two years' experience on their instrument or an AMEB equivalent of Grade 4. Ability to play more than one instrument is desirable. Applicants may have a classical, contemporary, or jazz background in music. Recipients must study music as a timetabled curriculum subject up to and including Year 11. Auditions include up to ten minutes of performed music plus brief sight-reading and aural test. Application fee is $50. Applications for 2027 entry have closed; applications for 2028 entry will open in late September 2026. Scholarships provide fee assistance for the duration of enrolment, though specific amounts are not publicly disclosed.
eligibility_criteria: Open to instrumentalists and vocalists; Entering Year 7; Minimum two years' instrument experience or AMEB Grade 4 equivalent; Sound academic record; Not available to overseas students; Must study music curriculum subject to end of Year 11
special_conditions: Recipients will be students with a high level of accomplishment on their chosen instrument. They must endeavour to excel in all areas of music within the College; maintain a high level of application and performance through continued study of instrument(s); involve themselves in the broader life of music at the College including participation in music at liturgies, assisting with performance running and assuming leadership role within the department. An annual review of the student's progress and contribution will be made. If progress is not satisfactory, the terms of the scholarship may be reviewed. Recipients must remain at the College for the duration of their scholarship. Departure during the scholarship term, unless at the request of the College or mutually agreed, may require reimbursement of the scholarship value.

school_name: Saint Ignatius' College - Senior School Campus
overview: Saint Ignatius' College offers Excellence Scholarships to students who demonstrate a high level of achievement in and contribution to co-curricular sports or activities, faith and service, and sustainability. The scholarships recognise students who embody Ignatian values and excel across multiple disciplines beyond the classroom.
description: Excellence Scholarships are open to new and current students in Years 7 to 12 who demonstrate a high level of achievement in and contribution to areas including co-curricular sports or activities, faith and service, and sustainability. Recipients must participate in and provide leadership in at least one co-curricular activity offered by the College relating to their area of excellence. Students must also study a related curriculum subject at the College until at least the end of SACE Stage 1, maintaining an exceptional academic record. The scholarship value and duration are determined by the College and not publicly disclosed. Application deadline for 2026 entry was 3 February 2025. Scholarships are reviewed annually based on students' attainment of the criteria. Recipients must demonstrate continued excellence and commitment to their designated area.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7 to 12; New or current students eligible; Must demonstrate high level of achievement in co-curricular sports, activities, faith and service, or sustainability; Must provide leadership in chosen co-curricular activity; Must study related curriculum subject until end of SACE Stage 1; Must maintain exceptional academic record; Not available to overseas students
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Successful applicants must participate in and provide leadership in at least one co-curricular activity offered by the College relating to their area of excellence (activity to be identified upon acceptance). Students must study a related curriculum subject at the College until at least the end of SACE Stage 1, maintaining an exceptional academic record. Scholarships are reviewed annually, based on students' attainment of criteria. Recipients must embody Ignatian values. Departure from the College during the scholarship term, unless at the request of the College or mutually agreed, may require reimbursement of the scholarship value.

school_name: Saint Ignatius' College - Senior School Campus
overview: Saint Ignatius' College offers bursaries to families who may be seeking a Catholic education in the Ignatian tradition but whose current financial circumstances prevent them from accessing the standard fee structure. Bursaries are needs-based and not merit-related, reflecting the Jesuit commitment to education accessible to all classes of people.
description: The Bursary Fund provides means-tested financial assistance to Catholic families seeking an Ignatian education whose financial circumstances make fee requirements unsustainable. The principal criterion for financial assistance is a demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford an Ignatian education. Consideration is also given to people of a refugee and/or First Peoples background. The Bursary Fund is limited and generally offers partial tuition assistance only. Applicants must submit all appropriate documentation, including financial records, in support of the application within a stipulated period. Each case is assessed on its merits, and the Rector and Principal exercise discretionary power to apply financial assistance. Bursaries are reviewed on an annual basis to ensure students continue to meet criteria. The College maintains confidentiality and distributes Bursary Fund monies with equity and compassion.
eligibility_criteria: Seeking Catholic education in Ignatian tradition; Demonstrable, means-tested inability to afford standard fees; All school year levels eligible; Catholic families with commitment to faith preferred; May include consideration of refugee and/or First Peoples background; Must submit financial records and documentation
special_conditions: Bursary Fund monies are limited and generally offer partial tuition assistance only. Not related to merit-based academic or music scholarship programs. Preference given to Catholic families committed to faith with congruence to College values and beliefs. Families applying must have good fit in terms of student's academic aptitude, co-curricular interests and social skills. It is essential that families submit all appropriate documentation including financial records within a stipulated period. Each case is assessed on merits. Bursaries are reviewed annually to ensure ongoing compliance with criteria. Rector and Principal exercise discretionary power in financial assistance decisions. Confidentiality and equity are maintained in all distributions.

overview: Salisbury High School partners with Youth Opportunities to provide scholarship awards to graduates of the Personal Leadership Program. Recipients receive scholarships including financial aid, career guidance, or academic tutoring support. This program recognises student leadership development and personal growth.
description: Youth Opportunities scholarships are available to graduates of the Personal Leadership Program who may be experiencing financial difficulty. Recipients receive a variety of awards including laptops, tablets, driving lessons, school supplies, and tutoring. The program is delivered off-campus by Youth Opportunities trainers over 10 weeks per school term, with participating students receiving 20 SACE credits upon completion. Students must first participate in the core Personal Leadership Program in Year 10 to be eligible. Selection is based on participation in the program and demonstration of financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a graduate of the Personal Leadership Program; Must be experiencing financial difficulty; Open to all genders; Available to students who complete Year 10 program
special_conditions: Recipients must be participating in the core Personal Leadership Program in Year 10. Programs are delivered off-campus one day a week over 10 weeks. Participating students receive 20 SACE credits upon completion.

overview: Salisbury High School offers financial assistance to eligible families to help with education-related expenses. Families meeting eligibility criteria are encouraged to apply for scholarship payments to support students with uniforms, backpacks, excursions and camps.
description: Families that meet the eligibility criteria are encouraged to apply for a scholarship to receive payments from $478 per year per student, to help with education expenses like uniforms, backpacks, excursions and camps. This financial assistance program is designed to support students whose families may face financial hardship. The annual value of $478 per student demonstrates the school's commitment to ensuring all students can participate in school activities regardless of financial circumstances. Application requires meeting specific eligibility criteria related to family income or financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Families must meet eligibility criteria; Financial need assessment required; Available to all year levels; Open to all genders
special_conditions: Families must meet specific eligibility criteria to apply. Assessment of financial need is required. Payments are intended for education-related expenses including uniforms, backpacks, excursions and camps.

overview: Salisbury High School presents the Jenni Hill Memorial Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement among graduating Year 12 students. This award honours students who have demonstrated exceptional academic excellence throughout their secondary education.
description: The Jenni Hill Memorial Scholarship is awarded annually to Year 12 students who have demonstrated exceptional academic achievement. Based on historical records from the school, multiple recipients have been recognised with this award, indicating it is presented to students of outstanding academic merit. The scholarship recognises academic excellence and is a prestigious award within the school community. Recipients are selected based on their academic performance throughout their secondary schooling. The award is presented at the school's annual awards ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must demonstrate exceptional academic achievement; Selected based on academic merit; Co-educational award
special_conditions: Award is based on academic performance demonstrated throughout secondary schooling. Recipients are selected by school staff based on merit criteria.

overview: Salisbury High School presents the Phillip Warner Memorial Award to recognise outstanding achievement among graduating Year 12 students. This award honours students who have demonstrated excellence in their secondary education.
description: The Phillip Warner Memorial Award is presented annually to Year 12 students who have achieved outstanding results in their studies. Based on historical award records from the school, multiple students have received this honour, indicating it is a significant academic recognition. The award is named after Phillip Warner and serves to recognise academic excellence and achievement. Students are selected based on their demonstrated academic merit and performance throughout their secondary schooling. The award is presented at the school's annual awards ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Selected based on academic merit; Co-educational award
special_conditions: Award is based on academic performance demonstrated throughout secondary schooling. Recipients are selected by school staff based on merit criteria.

overview: Salisbury High School presents the Natalie Louise Streather Memorial Award to Year 12 students who have demonstrated outstanding achievement. This award honours the memory of Natalie Louise Streather and recognises academic excellence.
description: The Natalie Louise Streather Memorial Award is presented annually to Year 12 students who have achieved outstanding academic results. Named in memory of Natalie Louise Streather, this award recognises excellence in secondary education. Historical records from the school show multiple recipients of this award over the years, confirming its status as a significant academic honour. Students are selected based on their demonstrated academic merit and overall achievement throughout their secondary schooling. The award is presented at the school's annual awards ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Selected based on academic merit; Co-educational award
special_conditions: Award is based on academic performance demonstrated throughout secondary schooling. Recipients are selected by school staff based on merit criteria.

overview: Salisbury High School presents the Rotary Development Award in partnership with the Rotary Club. This award recognises Year 12 students who have demonstrated outstanding leadership potential and contribution to the school community.
description: The Rotary Development Award is presented annually to Year 12 students who have shown exceptional leadership qualities and commitment to community service. In partnership with the Rotary Club, this award recognises students who have made significant contributions to their school and wider community. Historical records from the school show regular recipients of this award, indicating it is a consistent and valued recognition within the school. Students are selected based on demonstrated leadership ability, community engagement, and positive contribution to the school community. The award is presented at the school's annual awards ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must demonstrate outstanding leadership potential; Must show commitment to community and school; Co-educational award
special_conditions: Award is presented in partnership with the Rotary Club. Selection is based on demonstrated leadership potential and community contribution.

overview: Salisbury High School awards the Dimitroff Shield to recognise all-rounder achievement. This award honours Year 12 students who have demonstrated excellence across multiple areas of school life including academics, leadership, and co-curricular involvement.
description: The Dimitroff Shield is an all-rounder award presented annually to Year 12 students who have demonstrated well-rounded excellence across academic, sporting, cultural, and leadership dimensions of school life. Historical records show multiple recipients of this prestigious award over several years, confirming its importance in recognising balanced achievement. The award recognises students who have made significant contributions to the school community in multiple ways. Students are selected based on demonstrated excellence across several key areas of school involvement and achievement. The award is presented at the school's annual awards ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must demonstrate all-rounder achievement across multiple areas; Excellence in academics and/or co-curricular activities; Co-educational award
special_conditions: Award is based on demonstrated all-rounder achievement across academics and co-curricular involvement. Selection is made by school staff based on overall contribution to school community.

overview: Salisbury High School presents the Caltex All Rounder Award to Year 12 students who have demonstrated well-rounded excellence. This award recognises students who have excelled across academic and co-curricular areas throughout their secondary education.
description: The Caltex All Rounder Award is presented annually to Year 12 students who have shown exceptional all-round achievement combining academic excellence with participation in sports, arts, leadership, and community service. Named in partnership with Caltex, this award recognises students who have made meaningful contributions across multiple dimensions of school life. Historical records from the school show regular recipients of this award, confirming its status as a valued recognition. Students are selected based on demonstrated excellence across academic studies and involvement in co-curricular activities. The award is presented at the school's annual awards ceremony.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must demonstrate excellence across academic and co-curricular areas; Strong participation in multiple school activities; Co-educational award
special_conditions: Award is presented in partnership with Caltex. Selection is based on demonstrated all-round excellence combining academic achievement with co-curricular involvement.

overview: Seaford Secondary College students from eligible low-income families can access the South Australian School Card Scheme. This scheme provides financial relief to support school education costs for families meeting income thresholds.
description: The South Australian School Card Scheme provides financial relief for eligible low-income families in relation to school education costs. Families are eligible based on income testing. Applications should be completed prior to the end of Term 1 each year to ensure eligibility for the current school year. The financial benefit varies depending on family income level and assessed need. The scheme is administered by the Government of South Australia Department for Education. Students are encouraged to contact the school Finance Office or School Card directly for assessment of eligibility and application details.
eligibility_criteria: Families with income below specified thresholds; Must apply each year per school attended; Applications open from start of school year
special_conditions: Applications must be completed and submitted each year. Applications should be completed prior to end of Term 1 to ensure eligibility for the current school year. Contact school Finance Office on 8327 5200 for eligibility assessment.

overview: The Karkanthi Scholarship is offered through Adelaide University to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students at Seaford Secondary College. The scholarship provides comprehensive support including free tutoring, university awareness events, and monetary contribution to improve student outcomes at school.
description: The Karkanthi Scholarship is administered through Adelaide University and is available to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in Years 10, 11, and 12 at Seaford Secondary College. The scholarship provides free tutoring services to support student learning and academic achievement. Recipients receive invitations to university engagement events that encourage interest in attending university and provide awareness of available programs, courses and scholarships. The scholarship includes a monetary contribution allocated to improve student outcomes at school. Selection is based on student status as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander with eligibility in specified year levels. Additional university preparation excursions such as Life in the Uni Lane and Marni Wingku programs provide practical experience of university life.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students; Year 10, 11, or 12 enrolment; Must apply through Adelaide University
special_conditions: Administered through Adelaide University. Scholarship includes free tutoring, events to encourage interest in attending university, and monetary contribution to improve outcomes at school. Students also have access to additional university preparation excursions including Life in the Uni Lane at Flinders University and Marni Wingku at Adelaide University.

school_name: Southern Montessori School - Middle School Campus
overview: Southern Montessori School acknowledges that family financial circumstances may change significantly. Families experiencing temporary financial hardship may apply to the Business Manager for a Fee Remission to assist with tuition costs.
description: The Centre recognises that at times a family's financial circumstances may significantly change. Families experiencing temporary financial hardship may apply to the Business Manager for a Fee Remission. This program provides relief for families facing unexpected financial difficulties during their enrolment at the school. The application is managed on a case-by-case basis by the Business Manager, who assesses each family's circumstances individually. Approval and the level of remission are at the discretion of the Business Manager and Board of Governors. The program aims to support families who are otherwise committed to the Montessori education offered at Southern Montessori School but face temporary hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Families currently enrolled or seeking enrolment; Experiencing temporary financial hardship; Application required to Business Manager
special_conditions: Assessment of financial hardship is performed individually. Applications are reviewed by the Business Manager on a case-by-case basis. Approval is at the discretion of management.

school_name: Southern Montessori School - Middle School Campus
overview: Southern Montessori School offers Sibling Discounts to families with multiple children enrolled. The discount applies when two or more students from the same family attend the school simultaneously, reducing the tuition burden for larger families.
description: Sibling Discounts are available where two or more students from the same family attend Southern Montessori School at the same time. The discount applies only to the second and each subsequent child in the family. This ongoing benefit recognises the commitment of families with multiple children in the school and provides financial relief for parents managing tuition for more than one student. The discount amount and structure are determined annually by the Board of Governors as part of the overall fee schedule. Families should contact the Enrolments Officer for specific discount rates applicable to their situation.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children from the same family enrolled simultaneously; Discount applies to second and each subsequent child; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Discount applies only to the second child and subsequent children; families must have multiple children enrolled simultaneously at the school; discount is determined and may vary annually by Board of Governors decision.

overview: Southern Montessori School offers Sibling Discounts to families with multiple children enrolled simultaneously. The discount applies to the second and each subsequent child from the same family attending the school at the same time.
description: Southern Montessori School provides fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled concurrently. This program recognises the financial burden of educating multiple children and provides cost relief for sibling families. The discount applies specifically to the second and each subsequent child from the same family, helping to reduce the overall cost of education for larger families. Families can benefit from this discount as long as two or more students from the same family are enrolled at the school simultaneously. The exact percentage of the discount is available by contacting the school directly or reviewing the fee schedule.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more students from the same family must be enrolled at Southern Montessori School at the same time; Discount applies only to the second and each subsequent child; Open to all students in Baby Steps, Toddlers, Preschool, Reception through Year 9
special_conditions: The discount applies only to the second and each subsequent child from the same family. Both or all children must be enrolled at the school simultaneously for the discount to apply.

overview: Southern Montessori School acknowledges that families may experience temporary financial hardship and offers a Fee Remission program to assist eligible families. This program provides relief for families whose financial circumstances have significantly changed.
description: The Centre acknowledges that at times a family's financial circumstances may significantly change. Families experiencing temporary financial hardship may apply to the Business Manager for a Fee Remission. This program is designed to provide flexible support to families facing unexpected financial difficulties while their children are enrolled at Southern Montessori School. The application process requires families to contact the Business Manager directly to discuss their circumstances and explore available options. The remission amount and duration are determined on a case-by-case basis, depending on the family's specific financial situation and the school's assessment of need. This demonstrates the school's commitment to ensuring that all families can access quality Montessori education regardless of temporary economic challenges.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing temporary financial hardship; Application must be made to the Business Manager; Financial circumstances must have significantly changed; Applies to all year levels and programs at Southern Montessori School
special_conditions: Application must be made directly to the Business Manager; Assessment is case-by-case; Remission is intended for temporary financial hardship situations; Amount and duration determined individually based on family circumstances and school discretion.

overview: Seymour College offers Academic Scholarships to current and prospective students entering Years 5 to 10. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and a commitment to learning.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to current and prospective students entering Years 5 to 10 in 2027. The scholarships aim to support students with demonstrated academic excellence and provide financial assistance to enable access to a quality education at Seymour College. The exact financial benefit, selection process details, and renewal conditions are available through the College's formal application process. Interested families should contact the College directly for comprehensive programme details and specific terms.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students; Entering Years 5 to 10 in 2027; Demonstrated academic ability required
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10

overview: Seymour College awards General Excellence Scholarships to students entering Years 7 to 10 who are not currently attending the College. These scholarships recognise students demonstrating demonstrated ability and commitment to the College's values of excellence, respect and innovation.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to new students entering Years 7 to 10 in 2027 who are not currently attending Seymour College and are Australian residents. The College seeks aspirational young women with a diversity of interests, abilities and backgrounds who demonstrate demonstrated ability and embody the College values. The scholarship programme aims to support diversity and ensure that education at Seymour is accessible to a wide range of students. Specific financial benefits and selection criteria are available through the formal application process.
eligibility_criteria: Not currently attending Seymour College; Entering Years 7 to 10 in 2027; Must be Australian resident; Demonstrated ability required; Must embody Seymour College values of excellence, respect and innovation

overview: Seymour College offers Music Scholarships for instrumental and voice students. The scholarships recognise and support talented young musicians who wish to enter or continue their education at the College at Year 7 through Year 10 in 2027.
description: Music Scholarships are available to instrumental and vocal students who currently attend or seek to enter Seymour College at Year 7 through to Year 10 in 2027. The scholarships recognise exceptional musical ability and provide support to enable talented musicians to access a quality education with strong music programmes. Students may audition in instrumental or vocal disciplines. The exact financial benefit, audition requirements and terms are available through the College's scholarship application process.
eligibility_criteria: Instrumental or vocal music ability required; Currently attending or seeking to enter Year 7 to Year 10 in 2027; Demonstrated musical performance capability

overview: Seymour College offers Boarding Scholarships to students entering Years 7 to 10 in 2027 who do not currently attend the College. These scholarships support students who demonstrate solid all-round capabilities and a willingness to contribute to boarding life and the College community.
description: Boarding Scholarships are awarded to students entering Years 7 to 10 in 2027 who do not currently attend Seymour College. The College welcomes boarding applicants who can demonstrate solid all-round capabilities and a willingness to contribute to boarding life and the broader College community. The scholarship programme aims to make boarding at Seymour accessible to a diverse range of students. Specific financial benefits and selection processes are available through the College's formal application process. Boarders at Seymour benefit from the inclusive boarding house culture and pastoral support.
eligibility_criteria: Not currently attending Seymour College; Entering Years 7 to 10 in 2027; Solid all-round capabilities required; Willingness to contribute to boarding life and College community; Demonstrated ability and commitment
special_conditions: Boarding enrolment required.

overview: Seymour College offers a Years 11 and 12 Financial Assistance Scholarship to students who would not ordinarily be able to attend the College without financial support. This scholarship enables access to education in Years 11 and 12.
description: This scholarship allows a student who would not ordinarily be able to attend Seymour College without financial assistance to complete Years 11 and 12 at the College. The scholarship programme is designed to remove financial barriers and support students from disadvantaged backgrounds in accessing a quality education through their final years of secondary school. The exact level of financial assistance, application process and eligibility criteria are available through the College's formal scholarship application. This programme demonstrates the College's commitment to equity and providing educational opportunities to all students regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrable financial hardship; Seeking to complete Years 11 and 12; Unable to afford fees without financial assistance; Currently enrolled or seeking entry to Years 11 or 12
special_conditions: Demonstrated financial need required. Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

overview: Seymour College offers an Australian Indigenous Education Foundation Scholarship to Indigenous students in financial need. This scholarship removes financial barriers and provides opportunities for Indigenous students to pursue their education at the College.
description: An Australian Indigenous Education Foundation (AIEF) Scholarship provides an opportunity for an Indigenous student in financial need to attend Seymour College. The scholarship facilitates choice for parents and families by removing the financial barriers that may prevent Indigenous students who want to pursue their education from doing so. The AIEF Scholarship is awarded on the basis of student enthusiasm and family support for the opportunity, among other eligibility criteria. The scholarship programme recognises the importance of supporting Indigenous Australian students and enabling them to access quality education within the College's inclusive community.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage required; Financial need demonstrated; Student enthusiasm and family support required; Seeks to attend Seymour College; Meets general AIEF eligibility criteria
special_conditions: Demonstrated financial need required.

overview: The Roy and Marjory Edwards Scholarship is open to residents of the Northern Territory who wish to board at Seymour College. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate solid all-round capabilities and a willingness to contribute to boarding life and the College community.
description: The Roy and Marjory Edwards Scholarship is open to residents of the Northern Territory who can demonstrate solid all-round capabilities and a willingness to contribute to boarding life and the College. This scholarship supports students from remote regions to access education at Seymour College through boarding. The scholarship aims to provide opportunity for geographically disadvantaged students to benefit from the College's boarding facilities and educational programmes. Specific financial benefits and selection criteria are available through the College's formal application process.
eligibility_criteria: Northern Territory resident required; Solid all-round capabilities demonstrated; Willingness to contribute to boarding life and College community; Seeking to board at Seymour College; General demonstrated ability required
special_conditions: Boarding enrolment required.

overview: The Sarah Bowen General Excellence Scholarship is awarded to a student entering Year 10 as a boarder with an interest in pursuing Medicine. This scholarship supports talented students from disadvantaged backgrounds who aspire to careers in medicine.
description: The Sarah Bowen General Excellence Scholarship provides support for a student entering Year 10 as a boarder with an interest in pursuing a career in Medicine, who would not ordinarily be able to afford a private school education. Established by Old Collegian Dr Sarah Dolder (Bowen, '93), a medical, surgical and cosmetic dermatologist in New York City, this scholarship recognises the importance of supporting talented young women pursuing medical careers. The scholarship removes financial barriers and enables access to Seymour's educational programmes and boarding facilities for deserving students with medical aspirations.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 10 in boarding capacity; Interest in pursuing career in Medicine; Demonstrable financial hardship; Would not ordinarily afford private school education; Must demonstrate academic and personal capability
special_conditions: Boarding enrolment required. Demonstrated financial need required.

school_name: Southern Vales Christian College - Morphett Vale Campus
program_name: Academic Scholarship - Primary Level ACER Test
overview: Southern Vales Christian College offers Academic Scholarships for students entering Year 5 or Year 6 in 2026. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability through performance in the ACER Scholarship Test and provides tuition fee discounts to support outstanding scholars.
description: Southern Vales Christian College is pleased to offer Academic Scholarships for 2026 entry using the ACER Scholarship Test. Candidates applying for the Primary ACER Scholarship Test must be in Year 5 or Year 6 in 2026. The scholarship provides tuition discounts determined during the scholarship application process. The selection process involves sitting the ACER Scholarship Test, which assesses reading comprehension, verbal reasoning, abstract reasoning, and written expression. Each scholarship is offered for a maximum of two years, after which students may reapply. The College welcomes both internal and external applicants at both Morphett Vale and Aldinga campuses.
eligibility_criteria: Must be in Year 5 or Year 6 in 2026; Must sit the ACER Scholarship Test; Open to internal and external applicants; Available at both Morphett Vale and Aldinga campuses
special_conditions: Each scholarship length is for a maximum of two years. Students can reapply after this period. The student may not accept additional scholarships that conflict with their obligations under this agreement unless approved by the College. In the event of injury or illness that prevents the student from fulfilling their obligations, the College will review the circumstances and determine appropriate adjustments or continued eligibility for the scholarship. Details of the scholarship (including its value and terms) should not be disclosed to other students or families without the College's written consent.

school_name: Southern Vales Christian College - Aldinga Campus
overview: Southern Vales Christian College offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement and exceptional ability in incoming students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and reasoning skills through rigorous assessment.
description: The Academic Scholarship is open to students in Years 5 to 12 entering Southern Vales Christian College beginning in 2026. Applicants sit the ACER Scholarship Test to assess their reasoning, comprehension, mathematics, and verbal abilities. The scholarship selection process determines a tuition discount during the application process, with exact financial benefits varying based on performance and College assessment. Scholarship length is for a maximum of two years, after which students can reapply. Both internal and external applicants are welcome. Selection is based purely on academic merit as assessed through the ACER Scholarship Testing program.

school_name: Southern Vales Christian College - Aldinga Campus
overview: Southern Vales Christian College offers the Arts Scholarship to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional talent in performing and visual arts. The scholarship celebrates excellence in drama, music, dance, and visual arts disciplines.
description: The Arts Scholarship is available for students in Years 5 to 12 beginning in 2026 at both Morphett Vale and Aldinga campuses. Applicants are assessed based on demonstrated talent and excellence in performing arts disciplines including drama, music, dance, or visual arts. The selection process involves portfolio review, audition, and demonstration of artistic ability and commitment. Tuition discounts offered will be determined during the scholarship application process based on the level of artistic excellence demonstrated. The scholarship is for a maximum of two years, with students able to reapply after this period. Both internal and external applicants demonstrating arts excellence are welcome to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5 to 12; Must demonstrate exceptional talent in performing or visual arts; Open to both internal and external applicants; Students entering either Morphett Vale or Aldinga campus beginning in 2026; Must be willing to represent the College in arts programs and performances
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Each scholarship is for a maximum of two years. Students can reapply after this period. The student may not accept additional scholarships that conflict with their obligations under this agreement unless approved by the College. Recipients are expected to participate actively in College arts programs and performances. In the event of injury or illness affecting artistic performance, the College will review circumstances and determine adjustments.

school_name: Southern Vales Christian College - Aldinga Campus
overview: Southern Vales Christian College offers the Sports Scholarship to recognise and support students demonstrating exceptional sporting ability and commitment. The scholarship celebrates athletic excellence and dedication to representation of the College in competitive sports.
description: The Sports Scholarship is available for students in Years 5 to 12 beginning in 2026 at both Morphett Vale and Aldinga campuses. Applicants are assessed based on demonstrated sporting excellence, athletic ability, and commitment to competitive representation. Selection involves coach evaluation, athletic trials, and assessment of sporting merit and potential. Tuition discounts offered will be determined during the scholarship application process, varying based on level of sporting achievement and competitive potential demonstrated. The scholarship is for a maximum of two years, with students able to reapply after this period. Both internal and external applicants demonstrating sporting excellence are welcome to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5 to 12; Must demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment; Open to both internal and external applicants; Students entering either Morphett Vale or Aldinga campus beginning in 2026; Must be willing to represent the College in competitive sports programs
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Each scholarship is for a maximum of two years. Students can reapply after this period. The student may not accept additional scholarships that conflict with their obligations under this agreement unless approved by the College. Recipients are expected to participate actively in College sports teams and represent the College at competitive events. In the event of injury or illness preventing sporting participation, the College will review circumstances and determine appropriate adjustments or continued eligibility.

school_name: Southern Vales Christian College - Aldinga Campus
overview: Southern Vales Christian College offers the Hospitality Scholarship for students in Years 10 and 11 to support exceptional talent in hospitality and trade training. The scholarship recognises commitment to developing hospitality skills and pursuing vocational pathways.
description: The Hospitality Scholarship is available exclusively for students in Years 10 and 11 beginning in 2026, offered at both Morphett Vale and Aldinga campuses where the Hospitality Trade Training Centre is located. Applicants are assessed based on demonstrated ability in hospitality, culinary skills, customer service, and commitment to vocational training. Selection involves assessment of practical skills, interview, and evaluation of dedication to hospitality and trade education. Tuition discounts offered will be determined during the scholarship application process, based on the applicant's demonstrated potential in hospitality training and vocational achievement. The scholarship is for a maximum of two years, with students able to reapply after this period. Both internal and external applicants are welcome. The scholarship provides support for students pursuing the Hospitality Trade Training program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10 or Year 11; Must demonstrate exceptional ability or potential in hospitality or trade skills; Open to both internal and external applicants; Students must be enrolled at either Morphett Vale or Aldinga campus beginning in 2026; Must be willing to actively participate in Hospitality Trade Training Centre programs
special_conditions: Each scholarship is for a maximum of two years. Students can reapply after this period. The student may not accept additional scholarships that conflict with their obligations under this agreement unless approved by the College. Recipients are expected to maintain active participation in Hospitality Trade Training programs and demonstrate commitment to developing hospitality and vocational skills. In the event of illness or circumstances preventing participation, the College will review and determine appropriate adjustments.

school_name: Sports College South Australia - Adelaide Comets

overview: Seaview High School provides access to the School Card Scheme for low-income families. This scheme offers financial help with school fees, materials and services charges, and additional expenses such as uniforms, camps and excursions.
description: The School Card Scheme is a South Australian government financial assistance program available to low-income families with children attending government schools including Seaview High School. Eligible families receive financial help with school fees, materials and services charges, and where school fees are less than the School Card subsidy, can also access assistance with expenses such as uniforms, camps and excursion costs. The level of assistance varies based on family income and circumstances. Students remain at school and continue their education without disruption while receiving support. Families must apply through the appropriate government channels and demonstrate financial need to qualify.
eligibility_criteria: Low-income family status; Children must be enrolled at Seaview High School; Family income must meet government-set thresholds
special_conditions: School Card is a state-based program administered by the South Australian government, not by the school directly. Families must apply through official government channels.

school_name: Specialised Assistance School For Youth (SASY) - Port Road Campus
overview: SASY offers the Stepping Out Micro-Grants Program to graduates and school leavers to support their transition beyond secondary school. The program provides financial assistance for post-school pathways including tertiary education, vocational training, employment, and life skill development.
description: The Stepping Out program is SASY's community for graduates and school leavers that includes access to networking, community activities, training, micro-grants, and other events. Members of the program have access to micro-grants which can contribute to a SASY leaver's next steps, whatever this may look like. Financial support can be directed towards university books, laptops for TAFE, work boots, short courses, or assistance with obtaining a Driver's licence. The micro-grants are available to all SASY graduates and provide flexible, needs-based financial support for diverse post-school pursuits. Grants are administered individually based on the student's specific pathway goals and circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: SASY graduates and school leavers; Must have completed enrolment at SASY; Eligible for any post-school pathway including university, TAFE, vocational training, apprenticeships, or employment
special_conditions: Program is specifically designed for students who have graduated or left SASY. The support system aims to ensure that vulnerable young people are supported as vulnerable adults. Grants are flexible and tailored to individual circumstances and post-school goals.

school_name: Sports College South Australia - Edwardstown Oval
overview: Sports College SA offers the Excellence Scholarship to Year 11 and Year 12 students who can demonstrate authentic excellence in one or more specific categories. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional achievement in academic, sports, leadership and wellbeing domains.
description: The SCSA Excellence Scholarship is designed for Year 11 and Year 12 students who can demonstrate authentic excellence in one or more specific categories including academic achievement, sporting excellence, leadership, and the ability to support and promote wellbeing within a school and/or sports community. Applicants must complete a two-stage selection process: Stage 1 involves a written application, and successful applicants progress to Stage 2, which consists of a scholarship interview with a presentation (5-10 minutes). The application process requires applicants to prepare a presentation using any form of media (written, video, slideshow) and may include items of relevance such as photos, trophies, and certificates. No specific financial value is publicly stated for this scholarship on the official website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12; Must demonstrate authentic excellence in one or more specific categories; Must demonstrate academic achievement; Must demonstrate sporting excellence; Must demonstrate leadership qualities; Must demonstrate ability to support and promote wellbeing within school and/or sports community
special_conditions: Applications close on 31 August annually for the following school year. Scholarship students will have the opportunity to undertake high-quality personal development at the College in a supportive learning environment, with access to hands-on learning linked to the sport and recreation industry. Significant opportunities exist to develop networks, unique experiences, and create a planned, personalised pathway to employment and/or higher education.

school_name: Sports College South Australia - Edwardstown Oval
overview: Sports College SA offers the Equity Scholarship to Year 11 and Year 12 students who may otherwise not be able to attend the College due to one or more factors of disadvantage. The scholarship is designed to support students facing financial or social barriers to education.
description: The Equity Scholarship is designed to enable Year 11 and Year 12 students to attend the College who may otherwise not be able to due to one or more factors of disadvantage. This may include a combination of factors such as financial hardship, social circumstances, or other barriers to education. The scholarship aims to remove financial obstacles and provide equitable access to sports education and career pathways. Applicants must complete the application process as detailed on the scholarships page. No specific financial value is publicly stated for this scholarship on the official website, though it is described as providing financial assistance to enable attendance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12; Students experiencing one or more factors of disadvantage including financial hardship; Must meet the criteria demonstrating genuine need to attend the college

special_conditions: Administered through SA Government; Income-tested by government body; College assists families with applications and information

overview: St Andrew's School offers the ACER Academic Scholarship to recognise and enhance students' academic abilities at the primary level. The scholarship is available to students in Years 4, 5 and 6, and successful applicants receive a remission of up to 50% of base tuition fees. The objective of the Academic Scholarship is to acknowledge and enhance students' academic abilities, ultimately supporting them in reaching their full academic potential.
description: The ACER Academic Scholarship programme recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic capability through standardised testing. Successful candidates may receive a remission of up to 50% of the base tuition fees each year, continuing until the conclusion of Year 6 at St Andrew's School. As part of the application process, students are required to take part in a standardised ACER Scholarship Test that is externally moderated and used nationwide to identify academically capable students. The test assesses the ability to interpret, infer, deduce and think critically; tests are not curriculum-based and do not test the ability to retrieve learned knowledge. All external applicants must submit additional information to St Andrew's School before the application deadline. The scholarship is awarded at the Principal's discretion, subject to availability. For successful external candidates, acceptance requires completion of the St Andrew's application form, payment of the Confirmation Fee, and a Tuition Bond.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 4, 5 or 6 in 2025; Standardised academic test required; External and internal students eligible; Satisfactory level of academic performance expected; Positive contribution to all facets of School life required
special_conditions: Registration fee of $140 payable at time of registration via ACER website. Scholarship examination takes place at St Andrew's School. St Andrew's operates independently from ACER; test results used exclusively for determining scholarship recipients at St Andrew's School. Test results are not shared with other schools and parents are not provided with their child's ACER results report. Scholarships are offered on a continuing basis until the conclusion of Year 6. Successful applicants expected to maintain satisfactory academic performance.

overview: St Andrew's School offers the Music Scholarship to recognise and extend the musical ability of talented student musicians. Music Scholarships are open to students in Years 4, 5 and 6, with successful applicants receiving a remission of up to 50% of base tuition fees. The aim of the Music Scholarship is to recognise and extend the musical ability of the successful applicant while fostering excellence in performance.
description: The Music Scholarship programme is designed to recognise musical excellence and extend the ability of talented musicians within the school community. Successful applicants may receive a remission of up to 50% of the base tuition fees each year, continuing until the conclusion of Year 6 at St Andrew's School. Students must demonstrate an excellent attitude towards their music studies and set a high standard of performance both as a soloist and in ensembles. As part of the application process, students are required to complete an aural exam demonstrating high music ability on two instruments. Two contrasting pieces of music for the main instrument and one piece for each additional instrument, including voice, are required for the exam. The scholarship is awarded at the Principal's discretion, subject to availability. Successful applicants are expected to exemplify school values and contribute significantly to the musical life of the School.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 4, 5 or 6 in 2025; Aural examination and two-instrument audition required; Excellent attitude towards music studies required; High standard of performance as soloist and in ensembles required; External and internal students eligible; Satisfactory level of academic performance expected
special_conditions: Audition involves aural examination and performance on two instruments minimum. Students required to prepare two contrasting pieces for main instrument and one piece for each additional instrument including voice. Recipients expected to exemplify school values, demonstrate consistent academic excellence, positive attitude toward learning, actively participate in co-curricular activities, and contribute significantly to the life of the School. Scholarships continue until conclusion of Year 6 at School.

overview: St Andrew's School offers the Bright Futures Scholarship exclusively to external students demonstrating leadership potential and commitment to community service. The scholarship is open to students starting from Mid-Year Reception to Year 6 and recognises students who want to become young leaders within the school community and the broader community. This scholarship celebrates students' personal attributes and potential for leadership development.
description: The Bright Futures Scholarship programme recognises and supports students with strong leadership potential and a commitment to making a positive difference in the school and broader community. The scholarship is available to external students only, starting from Mid-Year Reception to Year 6 in 2024 and 2025. To apply, families and prospective students are invited to write a letter to the Principal, sharing their reasons for wanting to join the school community, how they intend to make a positive difference, and their interest in participating in additional activities. Applications are assessed as they are received, encouraging early submission. Successful applicants are expected to exhibit a positive contribution to all facets of School life while maintaining a satisfactory level of academic performance. The scholarship awards recognition for demonstrating leadership qualities and commitment to the broader school community.
eligibility_criteria: Exclusively open to external applicants; Open to students starting Mid-Year Reception to Year 6 in 2024 and 2025; Demonstrated leadership potential required; Commitment to making positive difference required; Interest in participating in additional activities required; Satisfactory level of academic performance expected; Positive contribution to all facets of School life required
year_levels: Reception, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Exclusively open to external applicants; not available to current students. Application requires letter to Principal detailing reasons for joining school community, how student intends to make positive difference, and interest in participating in additional activities. Applications assessed as received with early submission encouraged. Scholarships awarded at Principal's discretion, subject to availability. Recipients expected to exemplify school values of Belonging, Creativity, Humility, Integrity, Compassion and Excellence. Recipients must demonstrate consistent academic excellence, positive attitude toward learning, actively participate in co-curricular activities, and contribute significantly to life of School.

overview: St Andrew's School offers the Nurturing Greatness Scholarship to prospective students entering Reception through Year 4 who exemplify the school's values and demonstrate personal excellence. Successful candidates may receive a remission of up to 50% of the base tuition fees through to the completion of Year 6. The scholarship recognises students' personal attributes, academic achievements and their potential across a range of areas within the school community.
description: The Nurturing Greatness Scholarship programme recognises prospective students who embody the school's core values of Belonging, Creativity, Humility, Integrity, Compassion and Excellence, and demonstrate the potential to thrive academically and personally at St Andrew's. Successful candidates may receive a remission of up to 50% of the base tuition fees each year, continuing through to the completion of Year 6 at St Andrew's School. Offers may be made for Term 1 or Term 3 commencement. The application process requires submission of a letter detailing how the family and child exemplify each of the school values, an external personal reference, and school references if available. Applications are assessed as received with early submission encouraged. Scholarships are awarded at the Principal's discretion, subject to availability. Recipients are eligible for one form of discount only and cannot receive additional discounts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students entering Reception, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3 or Year 4 in 2025 and 2026; Current students not eligible to apply; External personal reference required; School references if available required; Exemplify school values of Belonging, Creativity, Humility, Integrity, Compassion and Excellence; Demonstrate consistent academic excellence and positive attitude toward learning; Active participation in co-curricular activities required; Significant contribution to life of School expected
special_conditions: Current students not eligible to apply. Registration deadline is 5.00pm Friday 28 February 2025; late applications not accepted. Scholarship recipients eligible for one form of discount only and unable to receive additional discounts. Offers may be made for Term 1 or Term 3 commencement. Recipients expected to exemplify school values, demonstrate consistent academic excellence, positive attitude toward learning, actively participate in co-curricular activities, and contribute significantly to life of School. Acceptance of scholarship offer requires completion of St Andrew's online application form, payment of Confirmation Fee, and Tuition Bond. Upon offer, families invited to attend school tour and meet with member of Executive Leadership Team.

overview: St Catherine's School Stirling offers financial difficulty remissions to families experiencing financial hardship. These fee concessions are assessed on a case-by-case basis and provide support to ensure families' financial circumstances are not a barrier to accessing a Catholic education.
description: Financial difficulty remissions are available to families experiencing financial hardship who are unable to pay school fees under the standard payment options provided by the school. Families can apply using the Reduced Fee Concession form available on the school website. The level of support varies and is assessed individually based on each family's financial circumstances and provided supporting evidence. Applications are reviewed on a case-by-case basis. The school encourages families to contact either the Finance Officer or Principal to discuss fee support in a confidential manner. All fee arrangements are treated in the strictest confidence.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Unable to pay fees under standard payment options; Must provide supporting evidence of financial difficulty; Case-by-case assessment basis
special_conditions: Financial difficulty remissions are assessed on a case-by-case basis. Families must provide supporting evidence to substantiate their claim of financial hardship. All fee arrangements are treated in the strictest confidence. The school believes a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to a student accessing a Catholic education.

overview: St Catherine's School offers a Family Discount when more than one child from the same family attends the school. This discount applies to tuition fees for families with multiple enrolled students.
description: St Catherine's School Stirling provides a Family Discount program when more than one child from the same family is enrolled at the school. This discount is applied to school fees to provide financial relief to families with multiple children attending. The discount is part of the school's commitment to making Catholic education accessible to all families within the community. The discount is automatically applied to fee accounts when multiple siblings are enrolled. Details of the discount structure and percentage reductions are included in the school's fees information.
eligibility_criteria: More than one child from the same family enrolled at the school; All enrolled children must be currently attending St Catherine's School
special_conditions: Discount applies when multiple children from the same family are enrolled. Due to legal advice from the Catholic Education Office, the option of splitting accounts is no longer available to families.

overview: St Columba College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding intellectual achievement. The scholarship recognises students with academic gift and ability who demonstrate exceptional academic promise and commitment to learning. Successful applicants join the College's Academic Extension Program.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to new students entering the College and existing St Columba College students in Years 7-11. Applications from students of academic gift and ability will be invited to interview. If successful, they will be asked to join the Academic Extension Program upon commencement. The Academic Extension Program is designed to meet the needs of students with high academic ability or potential. To determine a student's suitability for the Program, standardised tests such as NAPLAN, school reports, other accreditation and teacher recommendation are taken into consideration. If applicants have not done so previously, they will be asked to undertake an AGAT (ACER General Ability Test) – a nationally recognised tool for measuring academic gift and talent. The Program provides curricular and co-curricular opportunities, and students in the Program experience different learning opportunities. While specific financial values are not detailed on the scholarship page, this represents a merit-based academic award. The financial benefit, duration, and renewal conditions should be confirmed by contacting the school directly.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students to the College and existing St Columba College students in Years 7-11; Students of academic gift and ability; Demonstrated outstanding academic achievement or potential; Invitation to interview required; Suitable AGAT or NAPLAN performance
special_conditions: Successful applicants will be asked to join the Academic Extension Program upon commencement. The College will consider standardised tests such as NAPLAN, school reports, other accreditation and teacher recommendation in assessing suitability.

overview: St Columba College offers Music Scholarships to support students who demonstrate potential to excel as musicians. The scholarship recognises talented student performers on voice and instruments including violin, piano, guitar, and drums. The scholarship provides full remission of voice and instrumental tuition fees.
description: A limited number of Music Scholarships are awarded each year to Year 5-9 students who have the potential to excel in Voice (singing) and instruments. The scholarship will cover full remission of Voice and Instrumental Tuition Fee while enrolled at the College. These are competitive scholarships designed to support students with genuine musical talent and commitment to their musical development. The College currently supports 36 students with music scholarships annually. Applicants should be established performers with demonstrated instrumental or vocal ability. Selection is based on audition performance and musical potential. The scholarships are subject to annual review to ensure students continue to meet the required standard of musical achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5-9 students; Potential to excel as singers or instrumental players (violin, piano, guitar, drums); Established performers; Currently enrolled or applying for enrolment at St Columba College
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed annually. Students must maintain appropriate standards of musical performance to retain the scholarship. Full remission covers Voice and Instrumental Tuition Fee only, not general tuition fees.

overview: St Columba College offers Music Scholarships to senior students who demonstrate excellence in instrumental performance. The scholarship recognises accomplished musicians in piano, guitar, bass guitar, and drums. The scholarship provides full remission of instrumental tuition fees for one nominated instrument.
description: A limited number of Music Scholarships are awarded each year to Year 10-12 students who have the potential to excel as players of piano, guitar, bass guitar, drums and voice. The scholarship will cover full remission of Instrumental Tuition Fee for one of the instruments listed above. These competitive scholarships support senior students who have achieved a high level of musical proficiency and wish to continue developing their musical skills. Selection is based on demonstrated instrumental ability and musical potential. The scholarships are subject to annual review to maintain standards of musical achievement. This is a valuable opportunity for senior students to receive recognition and financial support for their musical pursuits.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10-12 students; Demonstrated excellence or potential to excel as instrumental players (piano, guitar, bass guitar, drums, voice); Currently enrolled or applying for enrolment at St Columba College
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed annually. Full remission covers Instrumental Tuition Fee only for one nominated instrument, not general tuition fees. Students must maintain appropriate standards of musical performance.

program_name: Alan Hickinbotham Dux of Year 10 Scholarship
overview: This scholarship honours Alan Hickinbotham, an industry leader and significant supporter of St Columba College. It is awarded annually to the Dux of Year 10 in recognition of outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship covers full tuition fees for Years 11 and 12.
description: Awarded annually to the Dux of Year 10, the scholarship covers tuition fees for Years 11 and 12 at St Columba College. This prestigious dux award recognises the student who achieves the highest overall academic performance in Year 10. The scholarship is provided by the College in memory of Alan Hickinbotham, who was an industry leader and significant supporter of St Columba College. The award directly supports the top-performing Year 10 student to continue their education at the College with full financial support for their final two years of secondary education. The selection is automatic based on dux status and requires no separate application process.
eligibility_criteria: Dux of Year 10 (highest overall academic performer in Year 10); Currently enrolled at St Columba College in Year 10
special_conditions: Awarded annually to the Dux of Year 10. The scholarship covers tuition fees for Years 11 and 12. Selection is based on Year 10 academic achievement.

overview: This scholarship is awarded annually to the Dux of Year 11 in recognition of outstanding academic achievement in Year 11. The scholarship covers full tuition fees for Year 12, enabling the top academic performer to complete their final year with full financial support.
description: This Scholarship, provided by the College and named in honour of its first Business Manager, is awarded every year to the Dux of Year 11 for study in Year 12 at St Columba College. The scholarship covers all tuition fees for Year 12. This prestigious award recognises the student who achieves the highest overall academic performance in Year 11. The scholarship provides complete financial support for the dux to pursue their final year of secondary education without financial burden. The selection is automatic based on dux status achieved in Year 11. The award is one of the College's most prestigious honours for academic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Dux of Year 11 (highest overall academic performer in Year 11); Currently enrolled at St Columba College in Year 11
special_conditions: Awarded annually to the Dux of Year 11. The scholarship covers all tuition fees for Year 12. Selection is based on Year 11 academic achievement.

overview: St Brigid's School offers fee exemption for all Reception students in their first year of enrolment, eliminating school fees to support families during their child's transition to primary school.
description: All Reception students who join St Brigid's School will not be charged school fees for their first year of school. This program covers tuition, levies and charges for each child's education. The exemption applies to full-time enrolled Reception students entering from either Term 1 or Term 3 intake. Families receive full fee remission for the first 12 months of enrolment. No application process is required beyond standard school enrolment. The program aims to reduce financial barriers for families and help them focus on their child's adjustment to primary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all Reception students; Must be newly enrolled in Reception year; Available for full-time enrolment; Applies regardless of family income or background
special_conditions: The exemption applies only to the first year of enrolment at Reception level. Standard school fees apply from Year 1 onwards. Families must complete regular school enrolment procedures. Exemption may be subject to agreement with the school Finance Officer and is conditional upon full attendance.

overview: St Brigid's School provides reduced tuition fees for families who qualify as low income through the State Government's School Card scheme. Eligible families pay a lower income fee instead of the standard tuition fee.
description: To be eligible for the lower income fee, families must qualify as a low-income family based on gross annual income thresholds set by the State Government. The specific dollar threshold varies based on family size and number of dependent children. Proof of income is required and applicants must apply through the State Government's School Card scheme. The school's finance officer provides assistance with the application process. The lower income fee provides a reduced tuition payment while still covering all tuition, levies and standard charges for each child's education. The program operates on an annual basis with renewal required each year. Flexible payment options are available for families managing financial constraints.
eligibility_criteria: Family gross income must be below State Government threshold (varies by number of dependents); Proof of income documentation required; Application via State Government School Card scheme; Annual renewal required; Available to all year levels
special_conditions: Income threshold is determined by State Government and changes annually. Families must provide proof of income documentation. The finance officer can assist with application completion. Families may contact the school for confidential discussions about financial hardship. Exemption status is reviewed annually with School Card scheme renewal.

overview: St Brigid's School is committed to ensuring that families experiencing genuine financial hardship can access Catholic education. The school provides support and flexible arrangements to assist families unable to pay full school fees.
description: The school strongly encourages families concerned about their ability to pay school fees to contact the finance officer or principal for a confidential discussion. Families can request a meeting to discuss their specific circumstances and explore available support options. The school recognises that financial circumstances can change and impact families' ability to maintain regular fee payments. Rather than denying enrolment due to financial inability, the school works with families to develop individualised arrangements. Support may include alternative payment plans, fee concessions, or other assistance tailored to the family's situation. All discussions and arrangements are handled confidentially and respectfully. The school operates on the principle that educational access should not be determined by financial circumstances alone.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing genuine financial hardship or difficulty paying school fees; Australian residency not specified but implied Catholic community member; Willingness to discuss circumstances with school finance officer or principal; No formal application required; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: No child will be denied enrolment due to inability to pay fees. All matters are treated confidentially. Families must proactively contact the school to discuss circumstances. Arrangements are determined on a case-by-case basis at the principal's and finance officer's discretion. Support is available for both short-term and long-term financial difficulty.

overview: St Brigid's School offers fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled at the school simultaneously, reducing the financial burden on families with siblings attending.
description: Families with two or more children enrolled at St Brigid's School at the same time are eligible for sibling discounts on school fees. The sibling discount is applied to tuition fees and reduces the overall annual fee burden. Specific discount percentages and structures are outlined in the school's fee schedule and payment information documents. Discounts apply automatically once families have multiple children enrolled. The discount provides meaningful financial relief particularly for larger families. Families should confirm specific discount rates with the finance office as rates may vary by year level and overall fee structure. The discount operates alongside other fee assistance programs such as the School Card scheme for lower income families. Discount status is reviewed annually with fee renewal.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children enrolled at St Brigid's School; Children must be enrolled at same time; All year levels eligible for discount consideration; Open to all families regardless of other circumstances
special_conditions: Discount applies only when multiple siblings are enrolled simultaneously at the school. Specific discount amounts should be confirmed with the finance office. Discount may not be combined with certain other fee relief programs depending on school policy. Families should contact the school to confirm current discount structure.

overview: St Francis de Sales College provides fee remission support to families experiencing financial hardship. The College recognises the Church's obligation to support families who are less fortunate and ensures that financial circumstances are not a barrier to enrolment.
description: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship and changes in financial circumstances may apply for fee remission from St Francis de Sales College. The application process is sensitive and highly confidential, with all applications assessed on an individual basis. Families must meet with the Finance Manager to discuss their circumstances. The value of remission varies depending on individual family circumstances and assessed need. All families supported with a remission are expected to have in place a monthly direct debit or Centrelink payment arrangement to cover minimum resource fees, capital levy, camp costs and year book expenses. Remission applications must be lodged annually, and support is provided from February through November.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; families with change of financial circumstances; demonstrated ongoing support for the school's ethos; willingness to enter payment arrangement with the College
special_conditions: All families supported with remission must have in place a monthly direct debit or Centrelink payment arrangement to cover minimum costs. Applications are confidential and assessed on an individual basis. Families must meet with Finance Manager. Remission is subject to annual review and families must maintain ongoing support for the school's Catholic ethos.

overview: St Francis de Sales College supports eligible families through the Government's School Card Scheme. Families who qualify for School Card assistance receive a 40% discount on tuition fees, reducing the financial burden of Catholic education.
