description: Families eligible under the South Australian Government's School Card Scheme receive a 40% discount on tuition fees at St Francis de Sales College. Eligibility is determined by family gross income levels set by the State Government. Eligible families must complete a School Card application form each year they wish to claim the benefit. All School Card families are charged full fees initially, with the discount adjustment made to their account once the College receives Department for Education confirmation of their eligibility. Families can contact the School Card scheme via telephone 1800 672 758 or download application forms from the State Government website. The discount applies to tuition fees only and does not include resource fees, capital levy, camps or other charges.
eligibility_criteria: Gross family income below threshold set by South Australian Government; application completed annually; State Government approval required
special_conditions: Annual application required. Families charged full fees initially with adjustment made upon Department for Education confirmation of eligibility. Discount applies to tuition fees only. Families must maintain ongoing support for school ethos.

overview: St Francis de Sales College's Basketball Academy provides elite-level development for talented student athletes. The program assists talented athletes in Years 8-9 and Years 10-12 to achieve at the highest levels of performance in basketball while supporting their educational and personal development.
description: The Basketball Academy is an elite program established to assist talented student athletes in Years 8 and 9, and Years 10, 11 and 12 to achieve at the highest levels of performance in basketball. The program combines on-court and off-court success, helping develop students' educational, emotional and social wellbeing. Students participate through a selection process that identifies elite athletes. The program provides structures and processes to support students accessing a range of post-College options. The Academy integrates basketball development with academic success and personal growth. The specific financial value of the program is not publicly detailed, but participation involves access to elite coaching and training facilities within the college's Community Sports Centre.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstration of elite basketball ability; Years 8-9 or Years 10-12 only; selection through formal application and assessment process; commitment to both basketball and academic excellence
special_conditions: Selection through formal application process. Students must meet required behavioral and academic criteria. Participation is subject to ongoing review of academic progress and conduct. Families must meet full financial commitment to the College within the current calendar year.

overview: St Francis de Sales College participates in the SACCS initiative offering free tuition for Reception students who enrol in a mid-year (Term 3) intake. This initiative aims to reduce financial burden on families entering their child into early education.
description: All Diocesan Catholic schools, including St Francis de Sales College, offer a free Reception intake in Term 3 of each calendar year as part of the South Australian Commission for Catholic Schools (SACCS) initiative. Reception students who enrol in the mid-year intake receive free tuition in Terms 3 and 4. The free intake includes tuition fees and compulsory charges but excludes uniforms and extra-curricular costs. This initiative aims to reduce the financial burden on families wishing to enrol their child in Catholic education. The college also offers a Term 1 Reception intake for children turning five on or before 30 April, and the second mid-year intake (Term 3) includes children who turn five on or before 31 October.
eligibility_criteria: Child turning five on or before 31 October; Term 3 enrolment only; no other special requirements; families must support the school's Catholic ethos
special_conditions: Free tuition for Terms 3 and 4 only. Covers tuition fees and compulsory charges. Does not include uniforms or extra-curricular costs. Child must be eligible age (turning 5 by 31 October) for Term 3 intake. Standard enrolment application process applies.

school_name: St Francis Xavier's Regional Catholic School

program_name: Australian Co-operative Entry Program (ACEP) Academic Scholarship
overview: St George College offers ACEP Academic Scholarships administered by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). The scholarship provides a subsidy on tuition fees to students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance on the Entry Program Scholarship Test. Scholarships are awarded based on assessment results and are tenable up to Year 12.
description: The Australian Co-operative Entry Program (ACEP) gives students the opportunity to apply for an Academic Scholarship provided by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). Students undertake the Entry Program Scholarship Test, and a subsidy on fees may be awarded subject to performance assessment. Academic Scholarships offered for the 2025 academic year provide a discount to the tuition fee component of the school fees up to Year 12 (note that it does not extend to the curriculum fee or the cost of uniform or textbooks). Strong academic results must be maintained throughout the duration of the scholarship. Annual reviews are conducted to determine whether the scholarship is being utilised effectively. Scholarships are applicable to all independent schools in South Australia.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students who will be in Years 5-8 in 2025; Must undertake ACER Entry Program Scholarship Test; Scholarship open to students from all independent schools in South Australia; Strong academic results must be maintained throughout duration of scholarship
special_conditions: Annual reviews are conducted to determine whether the scholarship is being utilised effectively. Strong academic results must be maintained throughout the duration of the scholarship. Scholarship does not extend to curriculum fees, uniform costs, or textbooks.

program_name: School Card Scheme - State Government Financial Assistance
overview: St Francis School recognises eligible families through South Australia's government School Card Scheme. Families who qualify for support under this scheme must not be required to pay full fees. The school encourages families experiencing financial difficulty to engage with the school to explore available support options.
description: St Francis School supports families eligible for the South Australian Government's School Card Scheme, which provides fee assistance for qualifying low-income families. Families determined to be low-income based on gross income thresholds set by the State Government are eligible to apply. Proof of income through the latest tax return is required, and applicants must apply via the State Government's School Card scheme. The school's Business Manager provides assistance with the application process. No child is denied a Catholic education because of a parent's demonstrated inability to pay fees, though those able to contribute are required to do so. The school has multiple payment options including direct debit, credit card, cash, EFTPOS and BPay to assist families in meeting fee commitments.
eligibility_criteria: Family must qualify as low-income based on State Government gross income threshold; Must provide proof of income via latest Australian Taxation Office tax return; Must apply through the State Government School Card scheme; Open to all students currently enrolled or seeking enrolment at the school
special_conditions: Families who qualify for School Card support must not be required to pay full fees. In most instances, such families are expected to make some fee contribution. Proof of income is required. Administrative cut-off dates are determined by Government authorities, and parents are encouraged to complete necessary documentation promptly. All family details remain confidential at all times.

program_name: Financial Assistance Application - COVID-19 Related Support
overview: St Francis School offers a formal Application for Financial Assistance form specifically designed to support families experiencing financial hardship. This program demonstrates the school's commitment to ensuring Catholic education remains accessible and affordable to all families in the community, regardless of financial circumstances.
description: St Francis School has established a formal Application for Financial Assistance process to support families experiencing financial difficulties. The school emphasises that a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to a student accessing a Catholic education. Families concerned about their ability to pay school fees are strongly encouraged to have a discussion with the school's finance office. The school operates on an income approach for fee setting which focuses on family financial capacity. An application form is available and families can contact the School Finance Office for assistance with the application process. Decisions about fee remission and assistance are made by the school based on individual family circumstances. All family details and financial information remain strictly confidential.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship or difficulty meeting school fee payments; Must be concerned about ability to pay school fees; Must complete the Application for Financial Assistance form; Open to all enrolled students or prospective students seeking enrolment
special_conditions: Financial circumstances should not be a barrier to Catholic education. All family details remain confidential. School encourages early discussion with finance office. Application form is available from the school. The school is committed to keeping fees as low as possible and ensuring education is affordable and accessible to all families.

program_name: School Card Scheme — Lower Income Fee
overview: St Francis of Assisi School offers reduced school fees to families eligible for the Australian Government's School Card Scheme. This program ensures that family financial circumstances are not a barrier to accessing Catholic education at the school.
description: The Lower Income Fee is available to families who qualify as a low income family as determined by gross family income thresholds set by the State Government. Eligible families receive a fee reduction applied to their school fees invoice. To apply, families must submit proof of income and complete an application through the State Government's School Card scheme. The school's Finance Officer, Jo Sayer, is available to answer questions about the program. The school is committed to making Catholic education affordable and inclusive for all families.
eligibility_criteria: Must qualify as a low income family as determined by State Government gross income thresholds; Proof of income required; Annual application required through State Government's School Card scheme
special_conditions: School Card must be applied for each year. Proof of income is required. Applicants will need to apply via the State Government's School Card scheme.

program_name: ACEP Academic Scholarship (Australian Co-operative Entry Program)
overview: St George College offers ACEP Academic Scholarships through the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). The scholarship program provides a fee subsidy to students who demonstrate exceptional academic potential. Students undertake the standardised Entry Program Scholarship Test and are assessed for eligibility.
description: The ACEP (Australian Co-operative Entry Program) Academic Scholarship is available through St George College for independent school students in South Australia. Students undertake the Entry Program Scholarship Test provided by ACER. A subsidy on tuition fees may be awarded subject to performance assessment. The scholarship provides a discount to the tuition fee component of school fees up to Year 12 (note that it does not extend to the curriculum fee or the cost of uniform or textbooks). Strong academic results must be maintained throughout the duration of the scholarship. Annual reviews are conducted to determine whether the scholarship is being utilised effectively.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students who will be in Years 5 to 8 in 2025; Must undertake the Entry Program Scholarship Test administered by ACER; Applicable to all independent schools in South Australia; Strong academic results must be maintained throughout duration; Annual review required
special_conditions: Scholarship provides a discount to the tuition fee component only - does not extend to curriculum fee or cost of uniform or textbooks. Annual reviews are conducted to determine whether the scholarship is being utilised effectively. Scholarships are offered for 2025 entry. Examinations took place on Saturday, 3 February 2024.

overview: St George College offers a Primary Scholarship for students entering the primary years. This scholarship recognises academic excellence and achievement in students transitioning to primary education at the college.
description: The Primary Scholarship is available for students who will be in Year 5 or Year 6 in 2025. This scholarship program is part of St George College's commitment to supporting academically gifted students. The scholarship provides a discount to the tuition fee component of school fees and is available for the duration of the student's enrolment up to Year 12. Recipients are expected to maintain strong academic results throughout their time at the college. Annual reviews are conducted to ensure the scholarship continues to be utilised effectively.
eligibility_criteria: Child will be in Year 5 or Year 6 in 2025; Must participate in scholarship selection process; Must maintain strong academic results; Subject to annual review
special_conditions: Limited scholarship places available. Scholarship provides a discount to tuition fee component only. Does not extend to curriculum fee, uniform costs or textbooks. Valid for duration of enrolment through Year 12. Annual reviews are conducted to determine whether scholarship is being utilised effectively.

overview: St George College offers a Secondary Scholarship for students entering secondary education. This scholarship recognises and rewards students demonstrating strong academic achievement at the secondary level.
description: The Secondary Scholarship is available for students who will be in Year 7 or Year 8 in 2025. This scholarship program supports academically talented students transitioning to secondary education. The scholarship provides a discount to the tuition fee component of school fees and continues for the duration of the student's enrolment through Year 12. Applicants must demonstrate academic excellence and maintain strong results throughout the scholarship period. Annual reviews are conducted to ensure effective utilisation of the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Child will be in Year 7 or Year 8 in 2025; Must participate in selection process; Must maintain strong academic results; Subject to annual review; Open to students demonstrating academic potential
special_conditions: Limited scholarship places available. Scholarship provides a discount to tuition fee component only. Does not extend to curriculum fee, uniform costs or textbooks. Valid for duration of enrolment through Year 12. Annual reviews are conducted to determine whether scholarship is being utilised effectively. Strong academic results must be maintained.

overview: St Francis of Assisi College offers the School Card Scheme to support families on low incomes with their children's education. The scheme provides eligible families with a 50% reduction on tuition fees. This government-administered program is designed to ensure that a family's financial circumstances are not a barrier to enrolment.
description: The School Card Scheme is a South Australian government program that provides fee assistance to families who qualify as low-income households. Eligible families receive a 50% Fee Reduction on their tuition fees at St Francis of Assisi College. To be eligible for the lower income fee, families must meet the gross income threshold set by the State Government each year. Families eligible and approved for School Card must submit a School Card application as early as possible in the year they wish to apply. Proof of income is required and applicants must apply via the State Government's School Card scheme. Applications are assessed on an individual basis and the process is handled with appropriate confidentiality in accordance with the school's Privacy Policy.
eligibility_criteria: Family must qualify as low-income household; Gross family income must be below threshold set by State Government; Families must apply via State Government's School Card scheme; Proof of income required
special_conditions: Applications should be submitted as early as possible in the year. Proof of income is required. Details of family's financial circumstances are handled with confidentiality in accordance with Privacy Policy.

overview: St Francis of Assisi College provides Financial Hardship Scholarships for families experiencing genuine financial difficulties who do not qualify for the School Card Scheme. These scholarships offer fee remissions to support families in crisis. The application process is sensitive and confidential, with each case assessed individually.
description: Families not eligible for the School Card who are experiencing genuine financial hardship are encouraged to submit a Financial Scholarship Application form or collect a hard copy at the College. All applications will be assessed on an individual basis by the College's Finance Manager. Fee remissions are available to eligible families, with the exact amount determined based on individual circumstances. The application process is sensitive and highly confidential. Details of a family's financial circumstances in determining fee remission arrangements are handled respectfully and with appropriate confidentiality in accordance with the school's Privacy Policy. Families experiencing hardship are encouraged to meet with the Finance Manager to discuss their situation.
eligibility_criteria: Not eligible for School Card scheme; Experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must complete Financial Scholarship Application form; Applications assessed on individual basis
special_conditions: Application process is sensitive and highly confidential. All applications assessed on individual basis. Families encouraged to meet with Finance Manager. Financial circumstances handled respectfully and confidentially in accordance with school's Privacy Policy.

overview: St Francis of Assisi College promotes Saver Plus, a government-funded financial inclusion program that supports eligible families with matched savings. Participants can receive up to $500 in matched savings annually to help cover education-related expenses, alongside valuable financial literacy support.
description: Saver Plus is a government-funded financial inclusion program administered by The Smith Family that supports eligible participants with up to $500 in matched savings to help cover their child's education costs. If parents commit to saving up to $50 per month for 10 months, the government will match their savings up to $500. The program assists with a wide range of education-related expenses including school uniforms, stationery, camps, laptops, sports fees and music lessons. Alongside the financial support, participants gain valuable budgeting skills and financial literacy education that contribute to long-term financial wellbeing. Income from Carer Payment or Child Support is considered as regular income. Participants can complete a referral form or enquire directly by calling 1300 610 355 or visiting The Smith Family website.
eligibility_criteria: Must be on lower income; Eligible families can commit to saving up to $50 per month for 10 months; Income from Carer Payment, Child Support and Cash in hand accepted as regular income; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Participants must commit to saving funds which are then matched. Financial literacy workshops are included. Program administered by The Smith Family, not the school directly. Families can join in February to claim matched savings in December for upcoming year costs.

overview: St Francis of Assisi College provides a school bus transport subsidy for families in regional areas who reside more than 5km from the nearest regional Catholic school. Catholic Education South Australia subsidises 60% of the annual bus cost, with additional government support providing a further discount.
description: Families accessing the school bus service managed by St Francis of Assisi College and who reside more than 5km from their nearest regional Catholic school are eligible for transport cost assistance. Catholic Education South Australia allocates funding to subsidise 60% of the full annual cost for families. Additionally, families receive a discount of 60% of the annual cost of accessing the school bus as part of the State Government's commitment to regional Catholic school accessibility. This funding is applied directly to families' school bus fees. The bus schedule and fees are available through the college website. To register for the bus, families should contact the College or email info@assisi.catholic.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Reside more than 5km from nearest regional Catholic school; Access school bus service managed by the college; Families must register for bus service
special_conditions: Must reside more than 5km from nearest regional Catholic school. Requires registration with the College. Subsidy is applied directly to school bus fees. Distance eligibility is determined based on location of nearest regional Catholic school.

overview: St Francis of Assisi College recognises outstanding students who demonstrate exceptional contributions to community service and personal development through the Access and Equity Scholarship. This award recognises leadership, generosity and responsibility in students from diverse backgrounds.
description: The Access and Equity Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional contributions to their school and broader community. Recipients are recognised for their leadership, generosity, strong sense of responsibility and service to others. The scholarship acknowledges students who overcome barriers and demonstrate resilience in their educational journey. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to community service, leadership initiatives, and personal achievement despite challenging circumstances. Recipients are recognised as inspiring role models within the college community. The scholarship recognises the importance of providing educational access to students from all backgrounds and circumstances, ensuring that excellence and potential are acknowledged regardless of socioeconomic status.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrate exceptional community service contributions; Show leadership and responsibility; Display resilience in overcoming barriers; Commitment to school and broader community; Students from diverse backgrounds and circumstances eligible
special_conditions: Award recognises non-academic achievement and community contribution. Recipients serve as role models. Award may include recognition and opportunities rather than direct financial benefit.

overview: St Francis of Assisi College students may access a Defence Science and Technology Group (DSTG) scholarship that supports rural and remote students in attending the Space School Camp. This scholarship covers costs associated with accessing educational STEM opportunities from regional areas.
description: Defence Science and Technology Group (DSTG) sponsors a scholarship to enable rural and remote students to attend the Space School, a three-night STEM camp held in Adelaide. The scholarship can be used to help cover the costs of getting to the space school from a regional area, recognising the additional travel expenses faced by students from the Riverland. The camp has a cost of $80 for attendance. This scholarship is specifically designed to provide access and equity for geographically disadvantaged students who wish to explore careers within the space industry and participate in hands-on STEM learning experiences. The application requires a statement from the school supporting the student's participation.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; Rural and remote location residence; Interest in space industry careers or STEM; School support required through statement
special_conditions: Application requires statement from school. Scholarship covers costs for accessing camp from regional area. Students attend three-night camp during school holidays in Adelaide at Warradale Urban Camp.

school_name: St Joseph's Memorial School - Junior Primary Campus

overview: St John Bosco School offers fee reduction to qualifying low income families. Families assessed as eligible for School Card by the Department for Education become eligible to receive the Lower Income Fees. This program supports families facing financial hardship in accessing a Catholic education.
description: St John Bosco School provides fee reduction to qualifying low income families through the School Card program administered by the Department for Education. Families who are assessed as eligible for School Card by the Department for Education become eligible to receive the Lower Income Fees as outlined by the school. The financial benefit provides reduced tuition fees for eligible families. Applications for 2025 are open and can be submitted electronically or via hardcopy form which must be completed and submitted to the Front Office. The school's Finance Manager can assist families with any questions about applying for the Lower Income Fee. The school believes that a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to a student accessing a Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Family must be assessed as eligible for School Card by the Department for Education; Eligible families receive reduced tuition fees
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted to the Front Office electronically or via hardcopy form. The school's Finance Manager is available to assist families with any questions about the application process. If a family is concerned about their ability to pay school fees, they are encouraged to have a discussion with the school.

school_name: St John The Apostle Parish School
overview: St John the Apostle Parish School offers the Lower Income Family Fee Scheme to families who qualify as low income. This program provides reduced school fees for families whose gross income meets State Government thresholds for non-government school assistance.
description: The Lower Income Family Fee Scheme provides families who qualify as low income with access to reduced school tuition fees. Eligibility is determined by your family's gross income against a value set by the State Government. In 2025, eligible families must meet Non-Government Schools limits for the 2023/2024 financial year (limits vary depending on the number of dependent children). Families must apply through the State Government's School Card scheme and provide proof of income. This program is available to all enrolled students and remains in place for the duration of their enrolment, provided income eligibility is maintained annually.
eligibility_criteria: Family must qualify as low income; Gross income must meet State Government Non-Government Schools limits for 2023/2024 financial year; Limits vary based on number of dependent children; Proof of income required; Application through State Government School Card scheme
special_conditions: Eligibility must be re-assessed annually. Limits are based on family income and number of dependent children as determined by State Government. The school encourages all families concerned about their ability to pay fees to discuss options with the school directly.

school_name: St John The Apostle Parish School
overview: St John the Apostle Parish School offers discounts for families with more than one student attending the school. This program recognizes the financial commitment families make in educating multiple children.
description: The Multi-Child Family Discount is available to families with more than one student enrolled at St John the Apostle Parish School. The school believes that a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing a Catholic education and provides this discount to support families with multiple children in the school. The discount applies to the tuition fees and covers all tuition, levies and charges for each child's education (camps are excluded and charged separately). This assistance remains available for the duration of enrolment for each student.
eligibility_criteria: Family must have more than one student enrolled at St John the Apostle Parish School; All students must be currently enrolled
special_conditions: Discount applies automatically to families with multiple students enrolled. The school encourages all families with financial concerns to have a discussion with school administration regarding available options.

school_name: St John The Apostle Parish School
overview: St John the Apostle Parish School provides financial hardship support to families who are concerned about their ability to pay school fees. The school believes that financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing a Catholic education.
description: The school's Financial Hardship Support program is designed to assist families who are experiencing difficulty paying school fees. The school strongly encourages families concerned about their ability to meet fees to have a confidential discussion with school administration to explore available options and support. The school is committed to making a quality Catholic education accessible and affordable to all families in the community. Support may include flexible payment arrangements, temporary assistance, or referral to government support schemes. The nature and amount of support is determined on a case-by-case basis.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial difficulty or hardship; Demonstrated inability to pay full school fees; Willingness to discuss situation confidentially with school administration
special_conditions: Support is provided on a case-by-case basis and is confidential. Families should contact the school office to arrange a confidential discussion with school administration. The school reserves the right to review support arrangements annually.

school_name: St John the Baptist Catholic School
overview: St John the Baptist Catholic School participates in the South Australian Government's School Card Scheme to provide financial assistance to low-income families. The scheme supports families attending non-government schools by assisting with school fees and materials and services charges.
description: The School Card Scheme is a government-funded financial assistance program designed to help low-income families access non-government school education. The scheme provides financial support for school fees and materials and services charges. Families must meet specific income thresholds set by the South Australian Government to qualify for assistance. The financial benefit is determined based on family circumstances and income level. Once approved, the discount is applied to the family's school account. This program is available to all students at St John the Baptist Catholic School who meet the eligibility criteria. Families experiencing changes in financial circumstances or facing hardship are encouraged to apply for support through this scheme.
eligibility_criteria: Family gross income below state government threshold (approximately $74,189 for 2025 for a family with one school-age child); Income threshold varies depending on number of dependent children; Families must provide proof of income; Families attending St John the Baptist Catholic School
special_conditions: Applicants must apply via the State Government's School Card scheme. Proof of income is required. Families whose circumstances change are encouraged to contact the school immediately. The discount is applied to the family's account once approval is received.

school_name: St John the Baptist Catholic School
overview: St John the Baptist Catholic School offers a 5% discount on annual school fees for families who pay their fees in full by the designated early payment deadline. This incentive program supports families in managing their educational expenses.
description: The Early Fee Payment Discount is a fee reduction program that rewards families for timely full payment of annual school fees. Families who pay their complete annual school fees by the specified deadline receive a 5% discount applied to their fees. This program is available to all families regardless of financial circumstance, providing a financial incentive for early payment. The discount applies only when the full annual fees are paid in advance as a single payment, not through installment payment plans. The discount amount equals 5% of the total annual school fees for each child enrolled at St John the Baptist Catholic School. This program assists families in reducing overall educational costs through advance payment.
eligibility_criteria: Families enrolling students at St John the Baptist Catholic School; Annual school fees must be paid in full by the designated deadline; Applicable to all year levels
special_conditions: Discount only applies if the full annual school fees are paid in full by the designated deadline. The discount does not apply to families using installment or payment plans. Families must contact the school office for the specific deadline date each year.

school_name: St John the Baptist Catholic School
overview: St John the Baptist Catholic School provides fee discounts for families with more than one student attending the school. This program recognizes the increased financial burden on families with multiple children in education.
description: The Sibling Fee Discount program is designed to reduce the financial burden on families with two or more children enrolled at St John the Baptist Catholic School. Families are eligible to receive fee discounts when multiple siblings attend the school simultaneously. The exact discount percentage or amount is determined on a case-by-case basis and families should contact the school office for specific details regarding their situation. This program acknowledges that educating multiple children increases family costs and seeks to make Catholic education more accessible to larger families. The discount applies to school fees and is applied to the family's account. Families can discuss their individual circumstances with the school's finance team to understand the specific discount available to them.
eligibility_criteria: Family must have two or more students enrolled at St John the Baptist Catholic School simultaneously; Students must be active enrollees during the same school term; Contact with school office required to determine eligibility and discount amount
special_conditions: Discount availability and amount must be confirmed directly with the school office. Families should contact the finance team to discuss their specific circumstances and determine eligible discounts. Multiple discounts may not be combined (e.g., early payment discount and sibling discount).

overview: St Joseph's Memorial School supports families experiencing financial difficulty through the South Australian School Card Scheme. This means-tested financial assistance program provides reduced school fees to eligible families based on gross income thresholds, ensuring no student's education is impacted by financial hardship.
description: St Joseph's Memorial School participates in the South Australian School Card Scheme, a government-funded program providing financial assistance to families who qualify based on gross family income limits. Eligible families receive a lower income fee applied to their school account once School Card is approved. The school works directly with the School Card Office to determine eligibility based on the number of dependent children in the family. A credit is applied to the account to reflect the reduced fee. Families with dependent children must have gross family income below specified thresholds. The school's Bursar and Principal are available to meet with families to discuss fee arrangements and ensure financial circumstances do not impact educational access.
eligibility_criteria: Gross family income below specified limits (limits vary by number of dependent children); Current enrolment at St Joseph's Memorial School; Application to School Card Office through the SA government scheme
special_conditions: Applications must be made through the SA School Card Office (1800 672 758). All details provided are kept strictly confidential. The school meets with families experiencing financial difficulties to discuss fee remission options.

overview: St Joseph's Memorial School offers a direct fee remission program for families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The Principal and Bursar work individually with families to ensure financial circumstances do not prevent their children from continuing their education.
description: St Joseph's Memorial School offers a dedicated fee remission application process for families facing financial difficulties. Parents and caregivers can apply directly to the Bursar for fee remission consideration. The school maintains strict confidentiality of all financial information provided. The Bursar and Principal are available to discuss fee arrangements and explore solutions to ensure children's educational continuation. This program operates alongside the government School Card Scheme and provides individualized consideration of each family's circumstances. Applications can be submitted to the school's accounts department year-round.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing genuine financial hardship or difficulty meeting fee obligations; Current enrolment at the school; Willingness to meet with Principal or Bursar to discuss circumstances
special_conditions: Applications should be made by email to accounts@sjms.catholic.edu.au or by contacting the Bursar directly. All information is kept strictly confidential. The school works on individual case-by-case basis.

overview: St Joseph's Parish School supports families accessing the State Government's School Card Assistance Scheme. Eligible families on low incomes receive automatic tuition fee reduction through this government-funded program.
description: The School Card Assistance Scheme is a State Government program that provides automatic financial support to eligible families. Families who are approved for School Card assistance automatically receive a 40% reduction in their Tuition Fees. The school actively encourages families on low incomes to apply for this scheme, as it provides meaningful financial relief for families struggling with fees. Families eligible and approved for School Card will automatically receive a 40% reduction in their Tuition Fees and can pay fees through flexible arrangements including cash, eftpos, cheque, direct bank deposit, or Centrelink deductions.
eligibility_criteria: Families on low incomes; Must apply for and be approved by the State Government; Automatic 40% tuition fee reduction upon approval
special_conditions: Families must be approved by the State Government School Card Assistance Scheme. Administrative cut-off dates apply each term and must be complied with to receive funds for eligible children.

overview: St Joseph's Parish School recognises that families may experience genuine financial hardship and offers discretionary tuition fee reductions. The school prioritises putting the education of children first and encourages families facing financial difficulty to seek support.
description: St Joseph's Parish School is committed to ensuring that financial hardship does not prevent access to Catholic education. Families suffering genuine financial hardship are encouraged to make an appointment with the Principal to discuss assistance for a warranted reduction in tuition fees. The school offers flexible tuition fee reductions on a case-by-case basis assessed according to family financial need and the school's capacity to support such reductions. All enquiries are treated confidentially to protect family privacy. The school supports the concept of equitable financial responsibility and encourages regular communication between families and the school regarding fee payments.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must make appointment with Principal; Income and financial circumstances considered; Confidential assessment process
special_conditions: Families must make an appointment with the Principal to discuss their circumstances. All discussions are treated as confidential. Non-payment of agreed reduced tuition fees is treated as an overdue account. Reductions are considered in terms of the financial needs of the family and the school board's responsibility to families making effort to pay regular fees.

school_name: St John's Grammar School - Junior Campus
St John's Grammar School - Secondary Campus

program_name: School Card Scheme — Lower Income Fee Reduction
overview: St Joseph's School offers eligible families access to the State Government's School Card Scheme, which provides fee reduction for families determined to be low income. The school believes that a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing a Catholic education.
description: The Lower Income Fee Reduction through the School Card Scheme is available to families who qualify as low income based on their gross family income, as determined by a dollar threshold set by the State Government. The amount of reduction depends on family income and the number of dependent children. Proof of income is required, and families must apply through the State Government's School Card Scheme. School Card approved lower income fee reduction is not applicable for Preschool fees. Families must complete the application form available from the Front Office and provide supporting documentation. The school's Bursar is available to assist families with questions about the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Family gross income must be below the State Government threshold; Amount of reduction depends on number of dependent children; Proof of income required; Not applicable for Preschool fees; Open to all families
special_conditions: Families who are concerned about their ability to pay school fees are strongly encouraged to have a discussion with the school. School fees can be tailored to individual family circumstances. Additional charges for extracurricular activities such as after school sport, choir, camps, senior tops and instrument hire are excluded from the tuition fee and will be charged separately.

overview: St Joseph's School provides financial assistance to families with more than one student attending the school through generous sibling discounts. This helps make Catholic education more accessible to larger families.
description: The Sibling Discount is available to families with more than one student enrolled at St Joseph's School, Hindmarsh. The school recognises that multiple family members attending school creates additional financial burden and offers this discount to support families. The specific discount amount is not specified on the public website. Families wishing to know the exact discount amount should contact the school directly. Discounts can be applied to annual fees or incorporated into customised payment plans, and payment arrangements can be made flexibly through weekly, fortnightly, monthly or termly deductions.
eligibility_criteria: Must have two or more children enrolled at St Joseph's School; Open to all families; No income assessment required
special_conditions: Only applies if multiple children from same family are enrolled at the school. Payment can be made in full or by approved payment plan, with options for weekly, fortnightly, monthly or termly direct debit payments.

overview: St Joseph's School offers families flexible payment plans to manage school fees. The school recognises that different families have varying financial circumstances and provides multiple payment options to ensure access to education.
description: St Joseph's School provides flexible payment arrangements to families to help manage school fee payments. Families can choose to make payments weekly, fortnightly, monthly or termly through direct debit arrangements. The school encourages all families to set up direct debit payments to simplify fee payment. Families may pay in full or utilise the approved payment plan structure. An indicative schedule of deductions is provided based on school fee charges, though additional charges for extracurricular activities must be paid separately or deduction amounts adjusted. From 1 February each year, deduction amounts are automatically adjusted to reflect updated fee rates or ensure payment of total outstanding fees by year end. Families can request to vary their deduction amount by contacting the Accounts Officer.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Direct debit must be established; Payment options include weekly, fortnightly, monthly or termly payments; Payment must be completed by end of calendar year
special_conditions: School fees must be paid in full by end of year. Families concerned about ability to pay are encouraged to have discussions with school. Deduction amounts are adjusted automatically from 1 February each year to reflect rate changes or ensure full payment of outstanding fees by year end. Families may vary deduction amounts by contacting Accounts Officer at accounts@sjsh.catholic.edu.au.

overview: St Joseph's School offers Middle School Scholarships to students entering Years 7-9 who demonstrate academic excellence and commitment to the school's Catholic values. The scholarship recognises students who show strong academic achievement, contribution to community and commitment to MacKillop values.
description: Up to two scholarships valued at $2,500 per annum will be awarded annually to students entering St Joseph's in the Middle School, Years 7-9, for a period of up to three years depending on enrolment starting point. Scholarships will be awarded based on a portfolio of evidence of growth mindset, academic achievement, contribution to community and commitment to MacKillop values. The selection process includes assessment of academic achievement from the student's current school, evidence of extracurricular and community involvement, and a satisfactory interview with the Principal demonstrating commitment to upholding the values of St Joseph's School. Students must complete their schooling at St Joseph's School to qualify for a scholarship, and early departure or transition to another school may require the scholarship to be repaid.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 8, or Year 9; Not currently attending St Joseph's School; Must demonstrate academic achievement and application to study; Must show evidence of extracurricular and community involvement; Must successfully complete interview with Principal
special_conditions: Scholarships will be awarded on the basis of a range of criteria which take into account the merit and need of eligible students. Students must complete their schooling at St Joseph's School to qualify for a scholarship. Early departure/transition to another school may require the Scholarship to be repaid to the school. Scholarship applications close at the end of Term 2. Short-listed applicants will be notified at the beginning of Term 3, with interviews conducted with the Principal by Week 5 in Term 3. Applicants will be notified of the outcome no later than 3 weeks after their interview.

overview: St Joseph's School offers Senior School Scholarships to students entering Years 10-12 who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and leadership qualities. The scholarship recognises commitment to academic excellence and contribution to the school community in the tradition of St Joseph and St Mary of the Cross MacKillop.
description: Up to two scholarships valued at $2,500 per annum will be awarded annually to students entering St Joseph's in the Senior School, Years 10-12, for a period of up to three years depending on enrolment starting point. Scholarships will be awarded based on a portfolio of evidence of growth mindset, academic achievement, contribution to community and commitment to MacKillop values. The selection process includes assessment of academic achievement and application to study from the student's current school, evidence of extracurricular and community leadership or involvement, and a satisfactory interview with the Principal. Students must demonstrate commitment to upholding the core values of faith, hope, love and respect. Early departure or transition to another school may require repayment of the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10, Year 11, or Year 12; Not currently attending St Joseph's School; Must demonstrate strong academic achievement and application to study; Must show evidence of extracurricular and community involvement; Must successfully complete interview with Principal
special_conditions: Scholarships will be awarded on the basis of a range of criteria which take into account the merit and need of eligible students. Students must complete their schooling at St Joseph's School to qualify for a scholarship. Early departure/transition to another school may require the Scholarship to be repaid to the school. Applicants must submit a personal statement of 400-500 words outlining their strengths, achievements and how they live the values of faith, hope, love and respect. Scholarship applications close at the end of Term 2. Short-listed applicants will be notified at the beginning of Term 3, with interviews conducted with the Principal by Week 5 in Term 3.

overview: St Joseph's School offers the St Joseph the Worker Scholarship to support students entering Years 11-12 who are pursuing a Vocational Education and Training (VET) pathway. The scholarship recognises commitment to a clearly defined VET pathway and supports students in construction or other committed vocational education pursuits.
description: Two St Joseph the Worker scholarships valued at $2,500 per annum will be awarded annually to students entering the school in Year 11 or Year 12 for a maximum of two years to pursue a Vocational Education and Training (VET) Pathway in Construction or another clearly defined and committed VET pathway. The selection process includes assessment of the student's commitment to their chosen VET pathway, evidence of academic achievement and application from their current school, evidence of extracurricular and community involvement, and a satisfactory interview with the Principal. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to upholding the values of faith, hope, love and respect. Financial need and circumstances may also be considered in the assessment process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12; Not currently attending St Joseph's School; Must be pursuing a clearly defined and committed Vocational Education and Training (VET) pathway; Preference for Construction pathway but other VET pathways considered; Must show commitment to academic achievement and community involvement; Must successfully complete interview with Principal
special_conditions: Available for a maximum of 2 years. Students must complete their schooling at St Joseph's School to qualify for a scholarship. Early departure/transition to another school may require the Scholarship to be repaid to the school. Scholarship applications close at the end of Term 2. Short-listed applicants will be notified at the beginning of Term 3, with interviews conducted with the Principal by Week 5 in Term 3. Applicants must submit a personal statement of 400-500 words outlining their strengths and commitment to their VET pathway.

overview: St Joseph's School offers a Creative and Performing Arts Scholarship to support students entering Years 11-12 who demonstrate excellence in creative or performing arts. The scholarship recognises students committed to pursuing a creative or performing arts pathway at the school.
description: A Creative and Performing Arts scholarship valued at $2,500 per annum will be awarded annually to a student entering the school in Year 11 or Year 12 for a maximum of two years to pursue a creative or performing arts pathway. The selection process includes assessment of the student's demonstrated excellence and commitment to creative or performing arts, evidence of academic achievement from their current school, evidence of extracurricular involvement particularly in arts-related activities, and a satisfactory interview with the Principal. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to upholding the values of St Joseph's School and show how they live the values of faith, hope, love and respect. The scholarship may be awarded to students pursuing music, drama, dance, visual arts or related creative disciplines.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11 or Year 12; Not currently attending St Joseph's School; Must demonstrate excellence in creative or performing arts; Must be committed to pursuing a creative or performing arts pathway; Must show evidence of academic achievement and community involvement; Must successfully complete interview with Principal
special_conditions: Available for a maximum of 2 years. Students must complete their schooling at St Joseph's School to qualify for a scholarship. Early departure/transition to another school may require the Scholarship to be repaid to the school. Scholarship applications close at the end of Term 2. Short-listed applicants will be notified at the beginning of Term 3, with interviews conducted with the Principal by Week 5 in Term 3. Applicants must submit a personal statement of 400-500 words highlighting their artistic achievements and how they live the values of faith, hope, love and respect.

overview: St Joseph's School offers Boarding House Scholarships to support newly enrolled students attending St Joseph's Boarding House. The scholarship recognises outstanding qualities and assists students in reaching their career aspirations while supporting boarding accommodation costs.
description: Up to three scholarships valued at $5,000 per year may be presented annually to newly enrolled students attending St Joseph's Boarding House for a period of up to two years. Alternatively, funds can be put towards school fees according to what works best for families as students may be eligible for an allowance for study away from home. The selection process includes assessment of academic achievement, evidence of extracurricular and community involvement, and an interview with a selection panel or the Principal demonstrating commitment to upholding the values of St Joseph's School. Financial circumstances and limitations may also be considered in the assessment process. Recipients must demonstrate a commitment to academic achievement, community engagement and leadership qualities in the tradition of St Joseph and St Mary of the Cross MacKillop.
eligibility_criteria: Open to newly enrolled students attending St Joseph's Boarding House; Not currently enrolled at St Joseph's School; Must demonstrate commitment to academic achievement; Must show evidence of extracurricular and community engagement; Must demonstrate leadership qualities; Must successfully complete interview with selection panel or Principal; Financial limitations may be considered
special_conditions: Available for a period of up to two years. Funds may be applied towards boarding house costs or school fees as determined to work best for families. Recipients may be eligible for an allowance for study away from home. All scholarship applications close at the end of Term 1 in the year prior to commencement at the school. Shortlisted applicants will be notified at the commencement of Term 2 with interviews scheduled by week 5 in Term 2. Applicants will receive the outcome no later than three weeks after their interview. A scholarship can be revoked at the Principal's discretion. All information submitted to the school will be held in the strictest confidence. There will be no formal recognition of successful applicants.

program_name: Sisters of St Joseph Memorial Scholarship
overview: St Joseph's School Tranmere offers the Sisters of St Joseph Memorial Scholarship to honour the legacy of the Sisters of St Joseph who founded the school. The scholarship recognises academic excellence and is awarded annually to an outstanding Year 6 student. Through the generosity of the parish, this scholarship celebrates the commitment to Catholic education that has shaped the school since its establishment in 1927.
description: The Sisters of St Joseph Memorial Scholarship is a prestigious award presented annually to a Year 6 student in recognition of their academic excellence and engagement with school life. The scholarship is valued at $1,500 and is used directly towards school fee payment for the recipient. The Principal, in consultation with the Year 5 teaching team, makes a recommendation to the parish each year for consideration and selection. This scholarship honours the pioneering work of the Sisters of St Joseph in Catholic education and celebrates their enduring legacy at St Joseph's School Tranmere. The award recognises students who embody the values of the school and demonstrate outstanding commitment to their learning journey.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 student; Outstanding academic achievement and engagement in learning; Recommendation from Principal and Year 5 teachers; Parish approval required
special_conditions: Scholarship recipient must be entering Year 6. Selection is made through principal recommendation and consultation with Year 5 teachers, with final approval by the parish. The scholarship value of $1,500 is applied directly to school fee payment.

overview: St Mary Magdalene's School offers fee-free, Catholic education to all students, providing every child with an opportunity to achieve success without financial barriers. The school believes that family income should not restrict access to high-quality Catholic education and therefore charges no tuition fees to any enrolling families.
description: St Mary Magdalene's School operates on a unique fee-free model where families enrol their children and pay $0 tuition fee. This commitment ensures that family income does not restrict access to high quality Catholic education. The financial model covers tuition costs completely with no charges to families, removing the primary financial barrier to school enrolment. This approach aligns with the school's founding values of Respect, Dignity, Spirit of Mercy, Social Justice and Service established by the Sisters of Mercy in 1957. The fee-free structure applies to all students regardless of their year level (Reception through Year 7) and remains in effect for the duration of the child's enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students entering Reception through Year 7; Must be enrolled at St Mary Magdalene's School; Applies to all families regardless of income level or financial circumstances
year_levels: Reception, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7
special_conditions: The fee-free model is part of the school's commitment to Catholic education and aligns with the founding values of the Sisters of Mercy. This is a structural feature of the school rather than a competitive scholarship program. No tuition fees are charged under any circumstances for regular enrollment.

overview: St Martin's Catholic Primary School offers Fee Remission Assistance to families experiencing extreme financial difficulty. The program provides eligible families with support toward their tuition obligations through a formal assessment process managed by the school's Finance Committee.
description: Fee Remission is available to families who are experiencing extreme financial difficulty. Applications for Fee Remission are available from the Business Manager's Office and are assessed by the Finance Committee. The remission form must be fully completed with all required paperwork attached. The amount of remission varies depending on individual family circumstances and the school's available budget. All discussions and ensuing arrangements regarding fee remission are treated in the strictest confidence. Families who are experiencing difficulty in meeting their financial liability are asked to contact the school immediately to discuss the situation.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing extreme financial difficulty; Complete remission form with all required documentation; Assessment by Finance Committee
special_conditions: Applications available from Business Manager's Office. All discussions and arrangements treated in strict confidence. Assessed by Finance Committee on case-by-case basis.

overview: St Martin's Catholic Primary School supports eligible families through the School Card scheme, an income means-tested assistance program administered by the Department of Education South Australia. This program provides financial support toward education expenses for qualifying low-income families.
description: The School Card scheme is an income means-tested scheme providing assistance to low-income families towards the cost of education expenses. The scheme is administered by the Department of Education South Australia and must be applied for each year. For eligible families, St Martin's applies the Lower Income Fee Assistance as determined by the School Card approval. Families should apply directly through the Department of Education South Australia online portal, with the 2026 School Card application available from Monday 12 January 2026. The school will liaise with families regarding the application process and any documentation required.
eligibility_criteria: Low-income families as defined by Department of Education South Australia; Income means-tested; Application required each year; Open to all students enrolled at St Martin's
special_conditions: Administered by Department of Education South Australia. Application must be completed and submitted each year. School will support with information and liaising with the department regarding eligible families.

overview: St Martin's Catholic Primary School offers sibling discounts on tuition fees for families with multiple children enrolled at the school. Discounts apply to the tuition fees as outlined in the school's fee schedule.
description: Sibling discounts apply to tuition fees for families with multiple children enrolled at St Martin's Catholic Primary School. The specific discount percentage and calculation method are detailed in the school's Fee Schedule which is available upon request from the Business Manager's Office or through the school website enrolments section. Families with siblings at the school should contact the school directly to clarify how sibling discounts are calculated and applied to their fee account. The discount is applied at the time of invoicing and reduces the overall family tuition obligation.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more siblings enrolled at St Martin's simultaneously; Application to families with multiple children; Discount reflected on fee schedule
special_conditions: Applicable for families with multiple siblings enrolled simultaneously at the school. Specific discount amounts detailed in school's Fee Schedule. Contact school for current discount rates.

overview: St Martins Lutheran College offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding academic achievement in Year 6 (Middle School entry) and Year 9 (Senior School entry) students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to excellence in their studies.
description: The Academic Scholarship provides reduced tuition fees for the duration of three years. The scholarship is open to both new and existing students currently studying in Year 6 (for Middle School entry) and Year 9 (for Senior School entry) in the year of application. Recipients are selected through a competitive assessment process. Applicants must complete the relevant application form and submit it to the College's Front Office. Scholarship results are advised approximately four to six weeks after the scholarship test date.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently in Year 6 (for Middle School entry); Open to students currently in Year 9 (for Senior School entry); Open to both new and existing students; Competitive academic merit assessment required
special_conditions: The scholarship will provide reduced tuition fees for 3 years per Scholarship. Applicants are required to complete the relevant application form and email it to the College's Front Office at enrolments@stmartins.sa.edu.au.

overview: St Martins Lutheran College offers Sports Scholarships to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional sporting achievement and excellence. These scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 (Middle School) and Year 10 (Senior School).
description: The Sports Scholarship provides reduced tuition fees for the duration of three years. The scholarship is open to both new and existing students currently studying in Year 6 (for Middle School entry) and Year 9 (for Senior School entry) in the year of application. Recipients are selected through a competitive assessment process that evaluates sporting ability, commitment, and potential. Applicants must complete the relevant application form and submit it to the College's Front Office. Scholarship results are advised approximately four to six weeks after the assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently in Year 6 (for Middle School entry); Open to students currently in Year 9 (for Senior School entry); Open to both new and existing students; Competitive sporting merit assessment required
special_conditions: The scholarship will provide reduced tuition fees for 3 years per Scholarship. Applicants are required to complete the relevant application form and email it to the College's Front Office at enrolments@stmartins.sa.edu.au.

overview: St Martins Lutheran College offers Performing Arts Scholarships to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional talent in drama, music, and related performance disciplines. These scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 (Middle School) and Year 10 (Senior School).
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship provides reduced tuition fees for the duration of three years. The scholarship is open to both new and existing students currently studying in Year 6 (for Middle School entry) and Year 9 (for Senior School entry) in the year of application. Recipients are selected through a competitive assessment process that evaluates performance ability, technical skill, and artistic potential. Applicants must complete the relevant application form and submit it to the College's Front Office. Scholarship results are advised approximately four to six weeks after the assessment or audition.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently in Year 6 (for Middle School entry); Open to students currently in Year 9 (for Senior School entry); Open to both new and existing students; Demonstrated performing arts ability and talent required
special_conditions: The scholarship will provide reduced tuition fees for 3 years per Scholarship. Applicants are required to complete the relevant application form and email it to the College's Front Office at enrolments@stmartins.sa.edu.au.

overview: St Martins Lutheran College offers Creative Arts Scholarships to recognise and support students who demonstrate exceptional talent in visual arts and creative disciplines. These scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 (Middle School) and Year 10 (Senior School).
description: The Creative Arts Scholarship provides reduced tuition fees for the duration of three years. The scholarship is open to both new and existing students currently studying in Year 6 (for Middle School entry) and Year 9 (for Senior School entry) in the year of application. Recipients are selected through a competitive assessment process that evaluates artistic ability, creative vision, and technical skill. Applicants must complete the relevant application form and submit it to the College's Front Office. Scholarship results are advised approximately four to six weeks after the portfolio review or assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently in Year 6 (for Middle School entry); Open to students currently in Year 9 (for Senior School entry); Open to both new and existing students; Demonstrated creative arts ability and talent required
special_conditions: The scholarship will provide reduced tuition fees for 3 years per Scholarship. Applicants are required to complete the relevant application form and email it to the College's Front Office at enrolments@stmartins.sa.edu.au.

overview: St Michael's College awards the Dr Jennifer Bourne Science Scholarship annually to students who have shown keen interest in their scientific studies. The scholarship provides financial support for students to attend the National Youth Science Forum. The scholarship is generously donated and supported by the Bourne family in memory of Dr Jennifer Bourne, a celebrated Scientist and former Board Member of St Michael's College.
description: The Dr Jennifer Bourne Science Scholarship is awarded annually to Year 12 students at St Michael's College who have demonstrated a keen interest in scientific studies. Recipients receive financial support that enables them to attend the National Youth Science Forum (NYSF), a premier national program for gifted science students. The scholarship recognises students with outstanding commitment to STEM disciplines and provides funding to cover the costs of the NYSF experience. Students who attend the NYSF are exposed to a range of science disciplines including microbiology, extra-terrestrial research, and other advanced scientific fields. The scholarship is renewable annually based on student engagement and continued academic performance in science subjects. Recipients are recognised at school academic assemblies, and the scholarship honours the legacy of Dr Jennifer Bourne, whose contributions to scientific education and school leadership remain central to the College's STEM initiatives.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; demonstrated keen interest in scientific studies; strong academic performance in science subjects; eligible to attend the National Youth Science Forum
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded annually. Recipients must demonstrate ongoing interest in science and maintain good academic standing. Funding is specifically allocated to enable attendance at the National Youth Science Forum.

overview: St Patrick's Special School offers a Lower Income Fee Scheme to support families experiencing financial difficulty. This scheme provides reduced tuition fees to eligible families who qualify as low income under State Government criteria.
description: St Patrick's Special School recognises that families may experience financial hardship and offers a Lower Income Fee Scheme to assist with school fee payments. To be eligible for the lower income fee, families must qualify as a low income family as determined by their family's gross income against a dollar value set by the State Government. Proof of income is required and applicants must apply via the State Government's School Card scheme. The financial benefit is determined based on individual family circumstances and income levels. The school's finance manager is available to assist families with questions about applying for this scheme. This is an ongoing annual program available to families in financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Families must qualify as low income under State Government criteria; Proof of income required; Application via State Government's School Card scheme; No restriction by year level
special_conditions: Proof of income is required. Application must be made through the State Government's School Card scheme. The school's finance manager can provide assistance with the application process.

overview: St Patrick's Special School offers tuition fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled in Catholic schools. The discount applies whether children are enrolled in the same school or across different Catholic schools.
description: St Patrick's Special School recognises that families with multiple children in Catholic education may face increased financial burden. The school offers discounts on tuition fees for families with children enrolled in other Catholic schools. These discounts apply for multiple children in the one school or across all Catholic schools in the diocese. Families with children enrolled in another Catholic school should contact the Business Manager to provide details for possible reassessment of school fees and determination of the applicable discount. The discount amount is determined on an individual basis according to family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Child or children enrolled in another Catholic school; Multiple siblings in Catholic education; Applies across all Catholic schools; Available to current and new families
special_conditions: Parents must contact Stella Li, Business Manager (sli@stpats.catholic.edu.au) with details of other Catholic school enrollments for fee reassessment. Discount is determined on a case-by-case basis.

overview: St Patrick's Special School provides financial hardship support to families experiencing unexpected difficulty in meeting school fee payments. The school believes that financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing Catholic education.
description: St Patrick's Special School acknowledges that family financial circumstances can change and may impact their ability to pay school fees. The school explicitly states that 'a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to a student accessing a Catholic education.' Families who are concerned about their ability to pay school fees are encouraged to have a confidential discussion with school leadership. Appropriate payment arrangements can be made on an individual basis to accommodate families experiencing financial hardship. The school office can be contacted to discuss flexible payment options or fee adjustments. Support is provided on a case-by-case basis with individual assessment of family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing financial difficulty or hardship; Willing to discuss circumstances confidentially with school leadership; Any year level; Unexpected change in financial circumstances
special_conditions: Families must discuss circumstances confidentially with the school principal or office. Arrangements are made on a case-by-case basis. Families are encouraged to initiate contact with the school office rather than waiting. All discussions are confidential.

school_name: St Michael's Lutheran School, Hahndorf Inc
overview: St Michael's Lutheran School offers bursaries to families experiencing genuine difficulty in meeting fee obligations. The bursary provides a discount on regular tuition fees and applications are considered on a merit-based system to ensure funds are distributed to those most in need.
description: Bursaries are made available where there is a genuine difficulty in meeting fee obligations. The bursary provides a discount on regular fees, and applications are considered on a merit-based system to ensure the funds are distributed to those that are in most need. The specific value and percentage of the discount are determined on a case-by-case basis through the application and assessment process. Families should contact the school's Business Manager, Mrs Tracy Downing, on 8388 7228 or TDowning@stmichaels.sa.edu.au for further inquiries regarding bursary applications and eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Families must demonstrate genuine financial difficulty in meeting fee obligations; Merit-based assessment process applied; Applications reviewed individually
special_conditions: Merit-based system used to ensure funds are distributed to those most in need. Applications are considered on an individual basis.

school_name: St Michael's Lutheran School, Hahndorf Inc
overview: St Michael's Lutheran School offers a discount on tuition fees for second children in the same family. This automatic discount recognises the commitment families make to Lutheran education and reduces fees for families with multiple children enrolled.
description: Families with more than one child enrolled at St Michael's Lutheran School receive automatic fee reductions. The second child receives a percentage discount on tuition fees. This discount is applied automatically to the fee account and recognises the financial commitment families make to Lutheran education. The school also recognises multi-child families through additional discounts for third and subsequent children, with the fourth and subsequent children receiving a 100% discount on tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Must have two or more children enrolled at St Michael's Lutheran School simultaneously; Automatic discount applied; Standard fee policies apply
special_conditions: Discount is automatically applied and does not require application. Second child receives a reduction; third child receives a greater reduction; fourth and subsequent children attract 100% discount on tuition fees.

school_name: St Michael's Lutheran School, Hahndorf Inc
overview: St Michael's Lutheran School and Cornerstone College jointly offer a rebate for families with students simultaneously enrolled at both institutions. This recognises families' commitment to Lutheran education across the primary and secondary years and provides financial support for the 13-year Lutheran education pathway.
description: To acknowledge the commitment that families make to Lutheran education for the thirteen years of each child's schooling, St Michael's Lutheran School and Cornerstone College offer a rebate for families who have students simultaneously enrolled at both institutions. This rebate is applied to the school fees and recognises the long-term commitment to Lutheran education from Foundation through Year 12. The specific rebate amount should be confirmed with the school's Business Manager. This program is part of Lutheran Education Adelaide Hills, a partnership between Cornerstone College and Lutheran primary schools in the Adelaide Hills region.
eligibility_criteria: Must have students enrolled simultaneously at both St Michael's Lutheran School and Cornerstone College; Enrolment at both institutions required for rebate eligibility; Rebate automatically applied to fees
special_conditions: Rebate applies only when students are simultaneously enrolled at both St Michael's Lutheran School and Cornerstone College. Enrolment at St Michael's guarantees a place at Cornerstone College in Year 7.
