program_name: School Card Scheme - Low Income Family Fee Assistance
overview: St Mary's Memorial School offers fee assistance to families who qualify as low-income families under the State Government's School Card scheme. The school recognises that financial circumstances should not be a barrier to accessing a Catholic education and encourages eligible families to apply for support.
description: St Mary's Memorial School provides financial assistance to families experiencing financial hardship through the State Government's School Card scheme. Families whose gross annual income falls below the State Government threshold (approximately $75,524 for a family with one school-age child in 2026) may qualify for a reduced tuition fee. Proof of income is required and applications are processed through the State Government's School Card scheme. The school's finance manager is available to assist families with questions about eligibility and the application process. The school strongly encourages any family concerned about their ability to pay school fees to have a confidential discussion with the school administration.
eligibility_criteria: Family gross annual income below approximately $75,524 for a family with one school-age child (varies based on number of dependent children); Proof of income required; Application through State Government's School Card scheme; Families experiencing financial hardship
special_conditions: The school believes that a family's financial circumstances should not be a barrier to a student accessing a Catholic education. Families are strongly encouraged to have a discussion with the school if they are concerned about their ability to pay school fees.

program_name: Catholic Parish Primary School Scholarship Year 7
overview: St Mary's College offers entrance scholarships to students entering Year 7 from Catholic Parish Primary Schools. These merit-based scholarships recognize exceptional academic achievement and commitment to Catholic education.
description: Two entrance scholarships are awarded annually to students entering Year 7 from Catholic Parish Primary Schools. The scholarship is awarded on the basis of academic merit demonstrated through school performance and entrance testing. Recipients must maintain consistent academic progress and demonstrate a commitment to the College's values. The financial value and duration of the scholarship are subject to annual review and renewal based on continued academic achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6 at a Catholic Parish Primary School in South Australia; Demonstrated academic excellence; Entering Year 7 at St Mary's College Adelaide
special_conditions: Awarded on the basis of academic merit. Recipients must maintain consistent academic progress and commitment to College values throughout their enrolment.

overview: St Mary's College participates in the Catholic Schools Scholarship Program (CESA) which provides assistance to children and young people in care. This program ensures access to Catholic education for vulnerable students from the community.
description: The Catholic Schools Scholarship Program (CESA) is a support initiative for children and young people in care who wish to attend Catholic schools, including St Mary's College Adelaide. The scholarship provides financial assistance to remove barriers to education for vulnerable populations. Students in out-of-home care or with support from child protection services may be eligible for this program. The scholarship aims to provide equitable access to Catholic education regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Children and young people in care or receiving child protection services; Open to multiple year levels; Demonstrated need for financial assistance; Commitment to attending St Mary's College
special_conditions: Administered through CESA in conjunction with Catholic Education South Australia. Eligibility determined by child protection status and enrolment at St Mary's College.

overview: St Mary's College supports families accessing the South Australian government's School Card scheme, which provides financial assistance with school fees for eligible families. The College works with families to help them apply for this government support.
description: School Card is a South Australian government financial assistance program that helps eligible families with school fees. St Mary's College actively facilitates access to this program by providing information and supporting families through the application process. Eligible families can receive a concession card that provides fee relief. The College's Finance Team can assist families with obtaining the School Card application form and understanding their eligibility. This is a means-tested program administered by the South Australian government through the Education Department.
eligibility_criteria: Family income below set government thresholds; Resident in South Australia; Enrolled or enrolling at St Mary's College; Meet government School Card eligibility criteria
special_conditions: Administered through South Australian government. Families must apply through sa.gov.au or contact College Finance Team. Financial assessment required to determine eligibility. Annual review required.

overview: St Mary's College offers a 5% discount on tuition fees for families with siblings enrolled at another Catholic school. This recognizes the commitment of families to Catholic education and supports families with multiple children.
description: Families with siblings currently enrolled at another Catholic school are eligible to apply for a 5% discount on the tuition fee component at St Mary's College. The discount applies only to the tuition fee and does not extend to resource fees or other costs. Families must submit a Sibling Discount Form to activate this benefit. The discount requires verification of enrollment at another Catholic school and applies to new and continuing students at St Mary's College.
eligibility_criteria: One or more siblings enrolled at another Catholic school; Student enrolling or enrolled at St Mary's College; Parent application and verification required; Maintains enrollment at Catholic school
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fee component only. Resource fees are excluded. Application via Sibling Discount Form required. Verification of sibling enrollment at Catholic school necessary.

overview: St Mary's College rewards families who pay their tuition fees promptly by offering a 5% discount on the annual tuition fee. This encourages timely payment and provides financial relief to families planning ahead.
description: Families who have no outstanding fees from the previous year and pay their full tuition fee by 28 February each year receive a 5% discount on the tuition fee component. This prompt payment incentive applies each year the conditions are met. The discount encourages families to plan financially and supports the College's cash flow management. Resource fees are not eligible for the discount and must be paid separately.
eligibility_criteria: No outstanding fees from previous year; Enrolled at St Mary's College; Pay tuition fee in full by 28 February; Payment via approved method
special_conditions: Applies to tuition fee component only. Resource fees excluded. Must have no outstanding fees from previous year. Discount applies annually if conditions met. Full fee payment required by 28 February each year.

program_name: The Smith Family – Learning for Life Scholarships
overview: St Patrick's Technical College is proud to be associated with The Smith Family through its Learning for Life program. The Smith Family's Learning for Life program supports disadvantaged children and young people all the way through their education, from pre-school and primary school, to senior school and on to tertiary studies. This holistic, long term support gives young Australians the assistance they need to develop vital life skills, stay engaged in their education and have the best chance to realise their potential.
description: The Smith Family Learning for Life program provides comprehensive support to disadvantaged children and young people through their education. Learning for Life support is provided in three main ways: through Learning for Life Workers, who connect students to learning opportunities in their local community and encourage them to fully participate in their education; by enabling access to Smith Family literacy programs and mentoring support; and by supporting families who provide the crucial nurturing relationships and supportive learning environments that children need. Parents can access education opportunities themselves to help them develop and build their own skills and abilities. The financial benefit is a partial reduction in tuition fees for eligible students. This scholarship is open to all year levels at the College and students can remain supported throughout their secondary education at St Patrick's Technical College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10, 11 or 12; Must demonstrate financial disadvantage; Students must be referred or identified as eligible by The Smith Family; Families experiencing genuine financial hardship are eligible; Open to all backgrounds
special_conditions: This is a need-based program administered by The Smith Family. Eligibility criteria and application details should be confirmed directly with The Smith Family. Contact The Smith Family on 8224 1400 for further details including specific eligibility criteria.

overview: The Central District Football Club Scholarship is provided by the Central District Football Club for the purpose of rewarding students attending (or wishing to attend) St Patrick's Technical College who demonstrate outstanding commitment to representing the Club and to the College's education and training program. The scholarship recognises students who excel in football and contribute to both their club and the College community.
description: The Central District Football Club Scholarship is awarded annually to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding commitment to representing the Central District Football Club and to St Patrick's Technical College's education and training program. As part of the scholarship, the Club assists St Patrick's Technical College in sourcing School-based Apprenticeship employment opportunities for scholarship recipients through publicising the scholarship to Club sponsors and supporters. This scholarship covers two-thirds of the full Tuition Fee, providing substantial financial support for eligible students. The scholarship is open to Year 10, 11 and 12 students who are active members of the Central District Football Club and demonstrate exceptional commitment both to football and their studies at the College. Scholarship recipients are supported in securing apprenticeship placements in their chosen trade.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10, 11 or 12; Must be an active member of the Central District Football Club; Must demonstrate outstanding commitment to representing the Club; Must be committed to the College's education and training program; Athletic excellence in Australian Rules Football required
special_conditions: Awarded annually. Recipients must maintain active involvement with the Central District Football Club and satisfy the College's education and training program requirements. Scholarship holders must be willing to participate in discussions and case studies about their scholarship experience. Full application details and selection criteria are available directly from the Central District Football Club.

overview: St Patrick's Technical College is proud to be associated with YourTown, a not-for-profit organisation with over 50 years of experience helping disadvantaged young people find their place in life through training, counselling and other support services. Through its various programs, YourTown has supported numbers of young people to attend St Patrick's Technical College.
description: YourTown provides comprehensive support services to disadvantaged young people, with a focus on training, counselling and other community-based assistance. Through its various programs, YourTown has supported a number of young people to attend St Patrick's Technical College by providing financial and non-financial assistance to help them access quality vocational education and training. The program supports students from disadvantaged backgrounds who demonstrate potential to succeed in a trade career. YourTown assistance is tailored to individual circumstances and may include partial fee reductions, counselling support, and referrals to additional community services. Support is provided for the duration of the student's enrolment at the College as they progress through their SACE and apprenticeship pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10, 11 or 12; Must be identified as disadvantaged or experiencing genuine hardship; Must be referred or supported by YourTown; Demonstrated commitment to completing SACE and pursuing a trade apprenticeship required
special_conditions: This is a needs-based program administered by YourTown, a not-for-profit organisation. Students are typically referred by YourTown case managers or identified through their community networks. The level and type of support is tailored to individual circumstances. For further details about YourTown support and eligibility, contact YourTown directly or speak to the College's General Manager of Finance & Operations.

overview: St Paul's College offers fee remission assistance to families experiencing difficulty in meeting the college's fee requirements. The program provides confidential and considerate assessment of financial circumstances to eligible families seeking support.
description: St Paul's College provides an Application for Fee Remission program for families experiencing difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College. Families can request assistance by completing an 'Application for Fee Remission' form, available upon request from the College Office. An assessment will be made as to eligibility and supporting documentation will be required to enable a confidential and considerate assessment of the family's circumstances. The college recognizes that financial circumstances can change and works with families to arrange appropriate assistance. The amount of fee remission varies based on individual family circumstances as determined through the formal assessment process.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing difficulty meeting fee requirements; Must complete Application for Fee Remission form; Must provide supporting documentation for assessment; Open to all students enrolled or seeking enrolment at St Paul's College
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted confidentially to the College Office. Supporting documentation required for eligibility assessment. Assessment considers individual family financial circumstances. Families are encouraged to discuss fee structure and arrange individual payment plans with the Business Manager.

overview: St Paul's College offers sibling discounts to families with more than one child enrolled at the college. The eldest child pays full fees while younger siblings receive a discount that is automatically applied.
description: St Paul's College offers sibling discounts to any family who has more than one child enrolled at St Paul's College. The basis of the discount is that the eldest child pays full fees and the discount applies to younger siblings. The discount will automatically be applied to St Paul's students from the same family. However, an application form must be completed online when the Payment Options form is submitted. This program provides ongoing fee relief for families with multiple children attending the college.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children from the same family enrolled at St Paul's College; Eldest child pays full fees; Younger siblings eligible for discount
special_conditions: Application form must be completed online when Payment Options form is submitted. Discount applies automatically to younger siblings once application is processed. Early payment discount of 3% is also available if full annual tuition fee is paid by 28 February.

overview: St Paul's College provides a 3% discount on annual tuition fees for families who pay their fees in full by 28 February each year. This discount incentivises early payment and provides fee relief to families.
description: Families who pay the annual tuition fee in full by 28 February will receive a 3% discount on their fees. This early payment discount applies to all families regardless of whether they use other financial arrangements or assistance programs. The discount is automatically calculated when fees are paid in full by the deadline. Payment can be made through multiple methods including direct debit, BPAY, QkR, credit card, or electronic fund transfers.
eligibility_criteria: Annual tuition fee must be paid in full; Payment must be received by 28 February; Applies to all enrolled students
special_conditions: Discount applies only to annual tuition fee paid in full by 28 February. Payment arrangement must be agreed with Finance Team by 30 November of the previous year for flexibility. Late payment administration charge of $100 applies to families not making payment or arrangement by 28 February.

overview: St Paul's College offers flexible payment arrangements for school fees to accommodate families' financial situations. Families can discuss their fee structure and arrange individual payment plans with the Business Manager based on their circumstances.
description: St Paul's College provides flexible payment options for families managing school fees. Families are welcome to discuss the fee structure and arrange individual payment plans with the Business Manager, who will assess the appropriate level of fees based on the family's financial circumstances. Payment arrangements must be agreed with the Finance Team by 30 November of the preceding year. Families can choose from multiple payment methods including direct debit, BPAY, QkR, credit card, Centrelink, or electronic fund transfers. Payments can be made on weekly, fortnightly, or monthly bases depending on arrangement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families enrolling students at St Paul's College; Payment arrangement must be negotiated with Finance Team; Arrangement must be agreed by 30 November of the preceding year
special_conditions: Arrangement must be agreed with Finance Team by 30 November. Families failing to make payment or arrange payment by 28 February will incur late payment administration charge of $100. One full term's notice in writing to Principal required if withdrawing child from school.

overview: St Peter's Woodlands offers one Gifted and Talented Scholarship per year level to new students entering into Years 3 to 6. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and intellectual potential, providing significant financial support to enable them to access the school's educational programs.
description: St Peter's Woodlands Grammar School offers one Gifted and Talented Scholarship per year level exclusively to new students entering Years 3, 4, 5, or 6. The successful candidate will receive a remission of 40% of the tuition fees each year to the conclusion of Year 6 at St Peter's Woodlands. This scholarship is designed to attract and support exceptionally gifted students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and potential. The selection process involves identification of students meeting specific gifted criteria, and recipients are expected to maintain their academic standing throughout their enrolment at the school. The scholarship continues for the duration of enrolment through to the end of Year 6.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Years 3 to 6; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability and gifted potential; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Scholarship continues for the duration of enrolment to the conclusion of Year 6. Recipients are expected to maintain their academic standing and commitment to learning.

overview: The Elaine Balfour Ogilvy Memorial Scholarship is available to Year 5 students who attend St Peter's Woodlands Grammar School. This scholarship provides financial subsidy towards their Year 6 school fees and honours the legacy of an old scholar who demonstrated exceptional character, leadership, and service.
description: The Elaine Balfour Ogilvy Memorial Scholarship is available to current Year 5 students attending St Peter's Woodlands Grammar School as a financial subsidy towards their Year 6 school fees. The scholarship was established in 1944 by the St Peter's Woodlands Old Scholars' Association in memory of Lieutenant Elaine Balfour Ogilvy AANS, an old scholar who exemplified courage, service, and leadership. The scholarship value is $2,500 to be paid directly to the school as a subsidy to Year 6 school fees. Candidates are selected through a competitive process involving submission of a letter of introduction and resume, followed by a short interview during which candidates may present examples of their achievements, enthusiasm, and commitment to school and learning. The interview panel comprises the President of the Old Scholars' committee, members of the Old Scholars' Association, and the Community Engagement Manager.
eligibility_criteria: Must be currently enrolled as a Year 5 student at St Peter's Woodlands Grammar School; Must demonstrate excellence, character, and commitment to learning and school community
special_conditions: Recipients are selected through competitive interview process conducted in Term 3. Recipients are invited to attend the annual Bangka Day Memorial Service to honour Elaine's legacy and service. Scholarship is awarded annually and paid directly to the school as fee subsidy.

overview: St Peter's Girls' School offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 5 to 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability based on ACER examination results.
description: St Peter's Girls' School offers Academic Scholarships to internal and external students based on the results of the Australian Council for Education Research (ACER) examination. In most cases, scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment, with the exact amount determined at the discretion of the Principal. Selection is based entirely on performance in the ACER examination held annually. Recipients must exemplify the school values of courage, creativity and compassion and maintain satisfactory academic performance throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to internal and external students; Entering Year 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12; Must sit ACER examination; Scholarship value and continuation subject to Principal's discretion
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment. The exact amount is determined at the discretion of the Principal. All Academic Scholarships are open to internal and external students based on the results of the Australian Council for Education Research (ACER) examination, which is held on Saturday 1 February 2025.

overview: St Peter's Girls' School offers Performing Arts Scholarships to recognise students with outstanding dance talent and potential. Each year a select number of these scholarships may be awarded to internal and external students with elite-level dance abilities.
description: The Performing Arts Scholarship at St Peter's Girls' School recognises excellence in dance performance and training. Recipients must have a minimum of 4 years of dance training, maintain a strong academic record, and demonstrate a proven ability to perform at an elite level. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment, with the exact amount determined at the discretion of the Principal. Selection involves submission of application materials and evaluation by the school's performing arts staff.
eligibility_criteria: Open to internal and external students; Entering Years 5 to 11; Minimum 4 years of dance training required; Strong academic record required; Proven ability to perform at elite level required
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Applicants must have minimum 4 years of dance training. Selection is based on demonstrated ability to perform at elite level combined with strong academic record. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment, with exact amount determined at the discretion of the Principal.

overview: St Peter's Girls' School offers Music Scholarships to recognise students with exceptional musical ability and talent. The scholarship rewards students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in music at an entry level appropriate to their year level.
description: Music Scholarships at St Peter's Girls' School may be awarded to internal and external students who demonstrate exceptional ability in music. Applicants must achieve minimum AMEB (Australian Music Examinations Board) grades appropriate for their entry year level and must participate in an audition. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment, with the exact amount determined at the discretion of the Principal. Specific eligibility criteria and AMEB grade requirements are detailed in the official Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to internal and external students; Entering Years 7 to 11; Minimum AMEB grades required for entry year level; Must successfully complete audition process; Strong academic record expected
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate exceptional ability in music and meet minimum AMEB grades for their entry year level. Audition is required and specific criteria are listed in the Application Form. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment.

overview: St Peter's Girls' School offers Sport Scholarships to recognise students with exceptional ability in sporting disciplines. The scholarship is awarded to external students who can demonstrate elite-level performance in their chosen sport.
description: Sport Scholarships at St Peter's Girls' School are offered to recognise excellence and elite-level performance in sporting disciplines. Applicants must be external students who can demonstrate an ability to perform at an elite level in their sporting disciplines and possess a strong academic record. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment, with the exact amount determined at the discretion of the Principal. Eligibility requirements and selection criteria are detailed in the official Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to external students only; Entering Years 7 to 11; Demonstrated ability to perform at elite level in sporting disciplines; Strong academic record required; Must meet specific sport qualification criteria
special_conditions: Applicants must be external students who demonstrate ability to perform at elite level in their sporting disciplines and possess strong academic record. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment, with exact amount determined at discretion of the Principal.

overview: The Principal's Scholarship at St Peter's Girls' School recognises exceptional all-round achievement and potential in external students. The scholarship is awarded to students who embody the school's core values of Courage, Creativity, and Compassion.
description: The Principal's Scholarship at St Peter's Girls' School is a testament to the school's commitment to nurturing exceptional individuals who embody the core values of Courage, Creativity, and Compassion. The scholarship is awarded to external students who demonstrate proven all-round abilities based on school reports, references, evidence of high-level participation in extra-curricular activities and leadership potential. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment, with the exact amount determined at the discretion of the Principal. Selection is based on demonstrated all-round excellence and potential rather than single-discipline achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to external students only; Entering Years 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 or 12; Must demonstrate proven all-round abilities; School reports and references required; Evidence of high-level extra-curricular participation required; Leadership potential required
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Principal's Scholarship is open to external students only and recognises exceptional individuals who embody school values of Courage, Creativity, and Compassion. Selection is based on proven all-round abilities including school reports, references, high-level participation in extra-curricular activities and leadership potential. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only.

overview: St Peter's Girls' School offers IBDP Scholarships for external students who wish to undertake the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme for Years 11 and 12. The scholarship recognises academic excellence and commitment to the rigorous IB curriculum.
description: St Peter's Girls' School offers IBDP (International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme) Scholarships for external students who wish to undertake the distinguished IB Diploma for Years 11 and 12. The scholarship provides entry to the academically challenging pre-university matriculation course that emphasises both breadth and depth of knowledge. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment in the IBDP (Years 11 and 12), with the exact amount determined at the discretion of the Principal. The IB Diploma is highly regarded by tertiary institutions both locally and overseas.
eligibility_criteria: Open to external students only; Entering Years 11 or 12; Must commit to undertaking the full IB Diploma Programme; Strong academic record demonstrated in previous schooling; Subject to Principal's assessment and discretion
special_conditions: IBDP Scholarships are for external students who wish to undertake the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme for Years 11 and 12. Scholarships are offered from 25% - 100% remission of tuition fees only, for the duration of enrolment in the IBDP, with the exact amount determined at the discretion of the Principal.

overview: St Raphael's School offers sibling discounts on tuition fees when more than one child from a family attends a Catholic school. This program recognises family commitment to Catholic education and helps reduce the overall cost for families with multiple children enrolled across the Catholic school system.
description: St Raphael's School is committed to supporting families with multiple children in education by offering sibling discounts on tuition fees. The discount applies when more than one child from a family attends any Catholic school, not limited to St Raphael's alone. This initiative acknowledges the financial commitment families make to Catholic education. Families are encouraged to contact the school office to discuss specific discount amounts available, as these may vary by year level and family circumstances. The school's Finance Officer is available to provide detailed information about how sibling discounts apply to individual family situations. Discounts are applied to annual tuition fees and represent a meaningful reduction in overall educational costs.
eligibility_criteria: More than one child from the same family attending a Catholic school; applicable to all year levels Reception to Year 6; enrolment at any Catholic school qualifies for the discount
special_conditions: Discount applies to families with multiple children enrolled in any Catholic school, not exclusively St Raphael's. Specific discount percentage or amount should be discussed with the school's Finance Officer. Families are encouraged to contact the school confidentially to discuss available support.

program_name: School Card Scheme - Reduced Fees for Low-Income Families
overview: St Raphael's School accepts the South Australian Government School Card scheme to provide reduced tuition fees for families who qualify for low-income support. This program ensures that genuine financial hardship is never a barrier to enrolment at the school.
description: St Raphael's School is committed to working with families experiencing genuine financial hardship to ensure access to Catholic education. The school participates in the South Australian Government School Card scheme, which provides reduced fees for families who qualify for low-income support. Families meeting income thresholds set by the State Government are eligible for fee reductions. To apply for assistance under this scheme, families must first establish eligibility through the State Government's School Card application process. Once approved, the school will adjust fees accordingly. The school office strongly encourages families in difficult financial circumstances to contact them confidentially to discuss their specific situations and available support options. No family seeking a Catholic education will be denied enrolment due to genuine financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Family qualifies for low-income support under South Australian Government School Card scheme; meets income threshold criteria set by State Government; open to all year levels Reception to Year 6
special_conditions: Families must apply through the South Australian Government School Card scheme first. Eligibility is based on family income and assets. The school handles applications with confidentiality and dignity. Assistance is separate from any sibling discounts. Families are encouraged to contact the school office and Finance Officer to discuss their circumstances and available support options.

program_name: Financial Hardship Support - Principal's Discretionary Assistance
overview: St Raphael's School offers discretionary financial hardship support through the Principal's office for families experiencing genuine financial difficulty. The school is committed to ensuring that no family will be denied a Catholic education because of financial circumstances.
description: St Raphael's School maintains a commitment that genuine financial hardship is never a barrier to enrolment. Families experiencing unexpected financial difficulty or changed circumstances are encouraged to contact the Principal or Finance Officer confidentially to discuss available support options. The school recognises that family circumstances can change and aims to work with families to find appropriate arrangements. Support may include payment plan adjustments, extended payment terms, or other assistance tailored to individual family needs. The school handles all discussions about financial difficulty with complete confidentiality, dignity and compassion. Families should not hesitate to reach out to discuss their circumstances, as the school is committed to finding solutions that allow children to continue their education. All arrangements are made on a case-by-case basis considering individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship or change in circumstances; open to all year levels Reception to Year 6; families must contact school to discuss eligibility
special_conditions: Assessment is conducted on a case-by-case basis. All discussions are confidential. Support is discretionary and may include modified payment plans, fee reduction or other arrangements. Families must approach the school Principal or Finance Officer directly. No predetermined amount or percentage is specified; assistance is tailored to individual circumstances. School reserves the right to determine appropriate support level based on family needs and school resources.

overview: St Raphael's School offers free tuition for Reception students enrolling in the mid-year intake during Terms 3 and 4. This CESA No Fee Policy initiative makes Catholic education more accessible for families joining the school later in the year.
description: St Raphael's School, in partnership with Catholic Education South Australia (CESA), offers a mid-year Reception intake with free tuition for students commencing in Terms 3 and 4. This initiative aims to ease financial burdens on families while providing high-quality early learning experiences backed by research. Students beginning in Term 3 typically spend 18 months in Reception before moving to Year 1, enabling a smooth educational transition. The free tuition covers essential charges and tuition fees, excluding uniforms and extracurricular activities. This No Fee Policy is part of CESA's Making Catholic Education More Affordable initiative. Children eligible for this program must turn five during the period 1 May to 31 October. The school's mid-year intake decision reflects research demonstrating the benefits of high-quality early learning experiences. Enrolment requires submission of an enrolment form and $50 application fee per family.
eligibility_criteria: Child turns five between 1 May and 31 October; enrolment in Terms 3 or 4 for Reception intake; open to co-ed students; $50 non-refundable application fee per family required
special_conditions: Free tuition applies to Terms 3 and 4 only. Excludes uniforms, extracurricular activities and optional charges. Students engage in 18 months of Reception learning before moving to Year 1. Families must use the school's enrolment calculator to verify appropriate enrolment age. Application is required through completion of enrolment form. Principal interview follows application submission.

overview: Stella Maris Parish School offers the Low-Income Family Support Remission to families experiencing financial hardship. This program aligns with the SA Government School Card Scheme to ensure equitable access to Catholic education.
description: Stella Maris Parish School has always supported families who require assistance with their school fees. The school offers families experiencing hardship a Low-Income Family Support Remission by application. The income thresholds for the Low-Income Family Support Remission align with the SA Government School Card Scheme. Families will automatically qualify for the Low-Income Family Support Remission by applying and being eligible to receive SA Government School Card. Proof of income is required, and applicants will need to apply via the State Government's School Card scheme.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship; Income thresholds align with SA Government School Card Scheme; Proof of income required; Must apply via State Government's School Card scheme
special_conditions: Proof of income is required. Applicants will need to apply via the State Government's School Card scheme. The Finance Officer can assist families with questions about applying for the lower income fee.

overview: Stella Maris Parish School provides full fee exemption for children of refugee families during their first 12 months of residence in Australia. This program recognises the unique challenges faced by refugee families settling in the country.
description: Stella Maris Parish School community and the Board are sensitive to the needs of refugee families and the hardship that fees may impose. As a result, the school provides a full exemption from payment of fees for children of refugee families in the first 12 months of their residence in Australia. This exemption is applicable even if the child has come from another school or college during the 12 months. Fee remission after the 12 months will be considered on an individual family basis.
eligibility_criteria: Child of refugee family; Within first 12 months of residence in Australia; Full fee exemption applies regardless of school transfers during 12-month period
special_conditions: Exemption applies for first 12 months of residence in Australia only. After 12 months, further fee remission will be considered on an individual family basis by the school.

overview: Star of the Sea School recognises that financial hardship can occur through unforeseen circumstances. The school provides confidential fee remission assistance to families experiencing financial difficulties. This support is available to eligible families regardless of their background.
description: Star of the Sea School offers financial hardship support through fee remission for families experiencing payment difficulties. According to the school's Fee Policy, families are encouraged to work with the Finance Manager and Principals to complete an 'Application for Remission' form if fee variation is required. The school follows South Australian Commission for Catholic Schools (SACCS) policy which states that financial circumstances should not prevent a student from accessing a Catholic education. Support is provided on a case-by-case basis. All applications are treated with complete confidentiality, and only the number of applications and dollar amounts are reported to the Board, with no identifying information disclosed. Remission amounts vary depending on individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Demonstrated payment difficulties; Open to all enrolled and prospective students
special_conditions: Families must discuss financial difficulties with the school prior to or during enrolment. Confidentiality is strictly maintained. Annual review of financial circumstances occurs. Proof of income may be required.

overview: The School Card Scheme is a South Australian Government income-tested assistance program that provides fee support to eligible families. Star of the Sea School encourages families on low incomes to apply for this government-funded assistance.
description: The School Card Scheme is a state government program that provides financial assistance based on family gross income. Eligible families receive a dollar value of support determined by the State Government according to their income threshold. Families must apply directly through the State Government School Card scheme. Proof of income is required as part of the application process. Applications are normally available before the start of each school year. This is a means-tested program designed to support families with demonstrated financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Family gross income below State Government threshold; Proof of income required; Australian residents; Open to all students
special_conditions: Income threshold determined by State Government; Proof of income documentation required; Applications processed before school year begins; Support amount varies by family income

overview: St Thomas School and Preschool offers fee remissions to families experiencing financial difficulties. The school is committed to ensuring financial circumstances do not prevent families from accessing Catholic education at St Thomas.
description: St Thomas School and Preschool offers fee remissions or payment arrangements to support families who are experiencing financial difficulties. The school is grounded in the shared belief that everyone matters and operates as a school of inclusion and welcome. As a Catholic school, St Thomas recognises that financial hardship can affect families and is committed to working with parents to find appropriate arrangements. Families experiencing financial difficulty or hardship are encouraged to discuss options confidentially with the school principal. The school works with families to establish flexible payment arrangements and fee remissions on a case-by-case basis to ensure that no child is disadvantaged due to their family's financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship or difficulty; Open to all students at St Thomas School and Preschool
special_conditions: Applications are made directly to the school principal. All discussions and arrangements are treated confidentially. Fee remission is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on individual family circumstances.

overview: Sunrise Christian School offers fee assistance to families experiencing financial hardship. The school has a tiered fee structure and formal fee assistance process to ensure Christian education is accessible to families with limited income.
description: Sunrise Christian School recognises that families may experience genuine financial need and provides a formal Fee Assistance assessment process. Families wishing to access Fee Assistance must provide Proof of Income and supporting documentation. The school has assessed that circumstances can change unexpectedly and encourages parents who cannot meet full fee payments to discuss this with the Principal. Parents may apply for a fee reduction and the school will negotiate an agreed amount to ensure the child continues enrolled. The program is designed to provide flexible support for families in low-income brackets, operating in addition to the school's tiered fee schedule.
eligibility_criteria: Families must be enrolled at Sunrise Christian School Morphett Vale; Must demonstrate genuine financial need through Proof of Income; Must provide supporting documentation as requested by the school; Open to families of all backgrounds and sectors
special_conditions: Fee Assistance is available for families experiencing changed circumstances and is assessed on an individual basis. The school requires one full term's written notice when a student intends to leave. Parents must notify the Principal in writing and meet the notice period. The tiered fee structure assists families in low-income brackets without requiring formal application.

program_name: The Robson Academic Scholarships (Temple Christian College Marion Middle School)
overview: Temple Christian College offers The Robson Academic Scholarships to exceptional students entering Year 7 at the Marion Middle School campus. The scholarship is designed to enable students to be involved in Christian education throughout their high school years by providing financial assistance based on academic merit demonstrated through competitive ACER testing.
description: The Robson Academic Scholarships at Temple Christian College Marion Middle School are merit-based awards offered to Year 7 students entering in 2027. The scholarship provides recipients with either full or part remission of tuition fees across all high school years, subject to annual review. The scholarship is awarded to the top candidates who reach a sufficiently high standard on the ACER assessment, with the equivalent of two full scholarships offered annually. Selection is determined primarily on ACER Scholarship Test results, and recipients are expected to be supportive of the Christian ethos of the school. The scholarship extends across Year 7 to Year 12 studies, with annual renewal conditions based on maintaining acceptable academic and behavioural standards.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 5; Sitting the ACER Scholarship Test at the beginning of Year 6; Score sufficiently high on ACER test; Must be supportive of the Christian ethos of the school; Scholarship subject to annual review
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarships provide for the recipient to receive either full or part remission of tuition fees across all high school years, subject to an annual review. It is expected that the recipient will be supportive of the Christian ethos of the school. The 2026 ACER Scholarship Test for 2027 entry will be held on Saturday 7 February 2026. For Marion Middle School students, enrolment is subject to the Sunrise Christian School Fee Structure, which includes a tiered fee schedule with the possibility of fee assistance for families where combined annual household income is under $67,000.

overview: Sunrise Christian School Marion offers a tiered fee schedule and fee reduction assistance for families experiencing financial hardship. The school provides fee assistance based on demonstrated financial need to ensure families can access Christian education regardless of income.
description: Sunrise Christian School Marion implements a compassionate fee assistance program for families unable to meet full payment of tuition fees due to changing financial circumstances. Parents who cannot afford the full fee can apply for fee reduction by providing proof of income documentation, including recent payslips and ATO Notice of Tax Assessment. For families with combined annual household income under $67,000, fees are charged at a reduced rate outlined in the tiered fee schedule once proof of income is provided. The school negotiates an agreed amount between the family and school to ensure children can continue to remain part of the Sunrise system. Applications for fee reduction can be made by contacting the Finance Office, and the school takes a compassionate approach to understanding changes in family circumstances throughout the school year.
eligibility_criteria: Parents unable to meet full payment of fees; Genuine financial hardship demonstrated; Combined household income under $67,000 preferred; Must provide proof of income documentation; Negotiation with Principal required; Any family circumstances can trigger application consideration
special_conditions: Applications require documentation of combined before-tax family income prior to salary sacrificing, salary packaging, or business investments. Required evidence includes four recent payslips for each working parent/caregiver and ATO Notice of Tax Assessment for the previous financial year. If families are unable or choose not to provide required evidence of income, tuition fees will be charged at the full fee rate. Fee reduction applications can be submitted at any time if family circumstances change during the school year. A new application is required each year. The school encourages parents to discuss matters with their Principal when they consider they cannot meet full payment of fees.

overview: Sunrise Christian School Marion offers sibling discounts for families with multiple children enrolled at metro Adelaide Christian Schools locations. The discount applies to tuition fees and provides families with financial relief.
description: Sunrise Christian School Marion offers tiered sibling discounts to families with multiple children enrolled at any of the metro Adelaide Christian Schools (Fullarton, Paradise, Morphett Vale, or Marion). The discount structure provides percentage reductions on tuition fees for the second, third, and subsequent children, with larger discounts applying as family enrolment increases. These discounts apply to tuition fees only and are applied at enrolment based on the number of children from the same family already enrolled or enrolling simultaneously. Families enrolling multiple children receive automatic application of the discount according to the published fee schedule. The discounts extend to Temple Christian College fees for students transitioning to middle school, which receive the same percentage discount that applied at the primary level.
eligibility_criteria: Multiple children enrolled at metro ACS schools (Fullarton, Paradise, Morphett Vale, Marion); Tuition fees only; Discount applies based on chronological order of enrolment
special_conditions: Discounts apply to tuition fees only, not to other levies or building fund contributions. When a student transitions to Temple Christian College for middle school years, they will receive the same percentage discount that applied at the primary school level. Discount structure increases with number of siblings enrolled.

overview: Sunrise Christian School offers Fee Assistance to families experiencing financial hardship. The school has introduced a process for assessing genuine need for fee assistance to ensure families on low incomes are not disadvantaged.
description: The Fee Assistance Program is available to families who cannot meet the full payment of school fees due to genuine financial hardship or changed circumstances. Parents who consider they cannot meet the full payment of fees are encouraged to discuss the matter with their Principal. Parents can apply for a fee reduction and the school will negotiate an agreed amount between families to ensure the child continues to remain part of the Sunrise system. The financial assistance available varies based on individual family circumstances and documented proof of income. The application process requires families to provide Proof of Income and supporting documentation as stated by the school. This is a means-tested program designed to remove financial barriers to Christian education.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must provide Proof of Income documentation; Open to all enrolled families or prospective families unable to meet full fee commitments; Financial circumstances must be assessed and approved by the Principal
special_conditions: Applications must be discussed directly with the Principal. The school will negotiate an agreed fee amount with families on a case-by-case basis. One full term's written notice is required if a student intends to leave the school.

overview: Sunrise Christian School operates a tiered fee structure designed to help families in low-income brackets access Christian education. The school believes in enabling all families to have access to quality Christian education regardless of financial circumstances.
description: The Fee Schedule operates on a tiered structure that provides differentiated fee levels for families based on income brackets. This is a built-in financial assistance mechanism rather than a traditional scholarship. The tiered approach means families in lower income brackets pay reduced fees compared to standard fees. The school publishes an annual fee schedule that outlines the different fee tiers available. This structure is part of the school's commitment to accessibility and ensuring that families on low incomes are not disadvantaged from accessing Christian education. Families can select the appropriate tier based on their financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families on low-income brackets; Income-based assessment required; Applied at point of enrolment or fee adjustment; Open to all prospective and current families
special_conditions: Fee schedule is reviewed and published annually. The tiered structure is an ongoing feature of school fees rather than a one-off scholarship. Families must provide documentation to support their fee tier selection.

overview: Sunrise Christian School provides sibling discounts for families with multiple children enrolled at metro Adelaide Christian Schools (ACS) locations. The discount applies to tuition fees and recognises the financial commitment of families with more than one child in the school system.
description: Families with more than one child enrolled at a metro ACS school are eligible for sibling discounts applied to tuition fees only. The discount percentage increases based on the number of children enrolled across Sunrise and Temple Christian College campuses. For families with multiple children, this represents a meaningful reduction in overall school fees. The sibling discount is calculated on the total annual tuition fees for each student and applies across the metro ACS school system including Sunrise Christian School locations and Temple Christian College. This benefit recognises and supports families committed to Christian education across multiple children.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children enrolled at metro ACS schools (Sunrise and/or Temple Christian College); Applies to tuition fees only; Available for families with children at multiple Sunrise locations or combination of Sunrise and Temple Christian College
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only, not to other levies or costs. Discounts are applied automatically upon verification of sibling enrolment. The discount applies across all metro ACS school locations.

overview: Temple Christian College offers The Robson Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding academic achievement in Year 7 students entering the college. These scholarships are designed to enable students to be involved in Christian Education throughout their high school years and recognise exceptional academic ability demonstrated through competitive testing.
description: The Robson Academic Scholarships are offered through the Australian Cooperative Entry Program (ACER) Scholarship Test, which is the primary selection mechanism for identifying exceptional students. The scholarship provides either full or partial remission of tuition fees across all high school years (Years 7-12), subject to annual review. The equivalent of two full scholarships will be offered providing a sufficiently high standard is reached by the top candidates. The award is based primarily on ACER test results. Recipients are expected to be supportive of the Christian ethos of the school and must maintain satisfactory academic progress to retain the scholarship through annual review.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Assessed through ACER Scholarship Test; Expected to support the Christian ethos of the school
special_conditions: Scholarships provide for the recipient to receive either full or part remission of Tuition Fees across all high school years, subject to an annual review. Recipients must be supportive of the Christian ethos of the school. Scholarship subject to annual review. The college reserves the right to withdraw any scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress.

overview: Temple Christian College offers The Robson Academic Scholarships to award outstanding academic achievement in students entering Year 7. The scholarships are designed to enable students to be involved in Christian Education throughout their high school years and recognize exceptional academic ability demonstrated through the ACER Scholarship Test.
description: The Robson Academic Scholarships are awarded to students entering Year 7 in the following year based on performance in the Australian Cooperative Entry Program (ACER) Scholarship Test. Recipients receive either full or part remission of tuition fees across all high school years (Years 7-12), subject to annual review. The equivalent of two full scholarships will be offered providing a sufficiently high standard is reached by the top candidates. The selection process involves sitting the ACER Scholarship Test, which is held in February. Recipients must be supportive of the Christian ethos of the school and scholarships are renewable annually pending satisfactory academic progress. The application fee is $150.00, which represents the full ACER fee.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Must sit the ACER Scholarship Test; Expected to be supportive of the Christian ethos of the school
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships provide for the recipient to receive either full or part remission of Tuition Fees across all high school years, subject to an annual review. It is expected that the recipient will be supportive of the Christian ethos of the school. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress to renew the scholarship annually.

school_name: The Heights School - The Heights Technical College
overview: The Ignite Program is a selective entry program offered by The Heights School for students with high intellectual potential. The program allows gifted and talented students to work at an extended level through accelerated learning opportunities. Students entering the Ignite Program in Year 7 are guaranteed direct entry to The Heights School, regardless of residential location.
description: The Ignite Program is a Department for Education specialist program designed for gifted and talented students. Students selected for entry into the Ignite Program progress at an accelerated rate commensurate with their ability, studying Years 8 and 9 in one year and then Year 10 in one year, all at an extended level. After Year 10, students continue Years 11 and 12 at a normal pace. Approximately 60 students are accepted into the program each year based on performance in the ACER assessment. Selection is based on performance in the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER) Gifted and Talented Assessment, which comprises four parts completed over 3 to 5 hours including reading comprehension, mathematical reasoning, abstract reasoning, and written expression. This is a non-monetary program with no direct financial benefit but provides accelerated educational pathways and guaranteed school entry. The program has no formal renewal conditions beyond continued enrolment and satisfactory academic progress, as entry occurs only at Year 7.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be in Year 6 at the time of assessment; Open to all students regardless of school of origin; Students must sit the ACER assessment; Assessment held once per year in February; No specific academic threshold stated, selection based on ACER assessment performance
special_conditions: Students selected for entry into the Ignite Program are guaranteed direct entry into The Heights School regardless of whether they live in the zone. Registration must be completed through the ACER portal. Students can only sit the assessment once. Students from country or interstate primary schools may sit the assessment at their primary school if approved by the school principal and a teacher accepts supervision responsibility. Registrations for the 2026 assessment will open in November 2025.

overview: Tatachilla Lutheran College offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding achievement and well-rounded excellence across multiple disciplines. This scholarship supports high-achieving students who excel in both academic and co-curricular pursuits.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship provides recipients with a 25% discount on college tuition fees for one academic year, with the opportunity to reapply in subsequent years. Selection is based on demonstrated excellence across academic and co-curricular areas. All scholarship recipients must embrace the college motto 'believe, become, belong', display high-level executive functioning skills, have potential to develop into leaders in the Tatachilla community, uphold the Student Code of Conduct, and commit to a minimum three-year tenure at the college. Applicants complete the standard Tatachilla Lutheran College scholarship application form with supporting documentation submitted to scholarships@tatachilla.sa.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Demonstrated excellence in academic and co-curricular pursuits; Display of high-level executive functioning skills; Commitment to three-year minimum tenure at college; Embrace of college values and Student Code of Conduct compliance
special_conditions: Recipients must embrace college motto 'believe, become, belong' and college values of faith, ambition, courage, growth, kinship and compassion. Scholarship is offered on a one-year basis with option to reapply. Recipients must maintain a minimum three-year tenure commitment at the college and remain in good standing according to the Student Code of Conduct.

overview: Tatachilla Lutheran College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and commitment to learning. This scholarship recognises outstanding performance across the full range of academic subjects.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship provides recipients with a 25% discount on college tuition fees for one academic year, with the option to reapply in subsequent years. Selection is based on demonstrated exceptional academic performance across all subjects. Applicants must demonstrate consistent high achievement reflected in their latest school reports. Shortlisted candidates are invited to attend interviews as part of the selection process. The scholarship supports the college's commitment to nurturing academic excellence and helping students achieve personal excellence in their learning journey.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Demonstrated exceptional academic performance across all subjects; Consistent high achievement reflected in school reports; Interview participation required for shortlisted applicants; Commitment to college values and Student Code of Conduct
special_conditions: Scholarship offered on a one-year basis with option to reapply. Recipients must maintain commitment to college values and Student Code of Conduct. Minimum three-year tenure commitment required.

overview: Tatachilla Lutheran College offers the Science and Mathematics Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional ability and achievement in science and mathematics. This scholarship supports students with strong aptitude in STEM disciplines.
description: The Science and Mathematics Scholarship provides recipients with a 25% discount on college tuition fees for one academic year, with the option to reapply in subsequent years. Selection recognises demonstrated excellence in science and mathematics subjects. Applicants must show consistent high achievement in these disciplines as reflected in their school reports. The scholarship is available for students at multiple year levels who demonstrate strong potential in STEM fields. All scholarship recipients must uphold the college's Student Code of Conduct and commit to supporting the college's values.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Demonstrated exceptional ability in science and mathematics; Consistent high achievement in STEM subjects; Reflection of strong performance in latest school reports; Commitment to college values and Code of Conduct
special_conditions: Scholarship offered on a one-year basis with option to reapply. Recipients required to maintain three-year minimum tenure at college. All recipients must uphold Student Code of Conduct and college values.

overview: Tatachilla Lutheran College offers the Advanced Athletics Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and achievement. This scholarship supports elite athletes who combine athletic excellence with commitment to their studies.
description: The Advanced Athletics Scholarship provides recipients with a 25% discount on college tuition fees for one academic year, with the option to reapply in subsequent years. Selection recognises demonstrated excellence in athletic performance and participation in competitive sports. The college supports its elite athletes through the Advanced Athlete Development Program, which provides coaching and mentorship from accredited coaches. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to both athletic pursuits and academic responsibilities while upholding college values and the Student Code of Conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Demonstrated exceptional athletic ability and performance; Active participation in competitive sports; Commitment to academic responsibilities alongside athletic pursuits; Upholding of college values and Student Code of Conduct; Three-year minimum tenure commitment
special_conditions: Scholarship offered on a one-year basis with option to reapply. Recipients expected to be active contributors to college life through participation in the Advanced Athlete Development Program. Minimum three-year tenure commitment required. All recipients must uphold college values of faith, ambition, courage, growth, kinship and compassion.

overview: Tatachilla Lutheran College offers the Arts Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional talent and commitment in the performing and visual arts. This scholarship supports artistically gifted students to develop their creative potential.
description: The Arts Scholarship provides recipients with a 25% discount on college tuition fees for one academic year, with the option to reapply in subsequent years. Selection recognises demonstrated excellence in performing arts such as music, drama, and dance, as well as visual arts. The college has won national awards for its performing arts programs and offers a Performing Arts Academy for extension students. Recipients are expected to actively contribute to college cultural life and participate in artistic performances and exhibitions. All recipients must uphold the college's Student Code of Conduct and commit to the college's values.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Demonstrated exceptional talent in performing or visual arts; Evidence of artistic commitment and achievement; Active participation in arts programs or performances; Commitment to college values and Student Code of Conduct; Three-year minimum tenure commitment
special_conditions: Scholarship offered on a one-year basis with option to reapply. Recipients expected to be active contributors to college artistic and cultural life. Eligible recipients may participate in the Performing Arts Academy. Minimum three-year tenure commitment required. All recipients must embody college values of faith, ambition, courage, growth, kinship and compassion.

overview: Tatachilla Lutheran College offers the Ecological Stewardship Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate commitment to environmental conservation and ecological leadership. This scholarship supports students passionate about environmental action and sustainability.
description: The Ecological Stewardship Scholarship provides recipients with a 25% discount on college tuition fees for one academic year, with the option to reapply in subsequent years. Recipients must be active environmentalists already volunteering with a recognised environmental action group. They must demonstrate a willingness to advocate for the Tatachilla EcoSanctuary, participate in the college's EcoRangers program, present ecological updates in school assemblies, and actively contribute to conservation efforts at the college. The college features an EcoClassroom with native plant species and fauna monitoring programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Active environmentalist status with current volunteering in recognised environmental action group; Demonstrated commitment to ecological conservation; Willingness to participate in EcoSanctuary advocacy and EcoRangers program; Commitment to present ecological updates in assemblies; Active contribution to college conservation efforts; Upholding of college values and Student Code of Conduct
special_conditions: Scholarship offered on a one-year basis with option to reapply. Recipients must maintain active participation in environmental action groups and college conservation programs. Expected to present ecological updates to the broader school community. Must commit to supporting the Tatachilla EcoSanctuary and participating in the EcoRangers program. Minimum three-year tenure commitment required.

overview: Tatachilla Lutheran College offers the First Nations Scholarship to expand educational opportunities for First Nations students and recognise their strengths and abilities. This scholarship supports Indigenous Australian students to nurture their individual gifts and passions.
description: The First Nations Scholarship provides recipients with a 25% discount on college tuition fees for one academic year, with the option to reapply in subsequent years. Recipients must be First Nations Australians who can demonstrate consistent achievement of at least a B grade average across all subjects, as reflected in their latest school reports. Applicants are invited to outline their personal achievements and aspirations and describe unique experiences as a contemporary First Nations Australian. Shortlisted candidates are invited to attend interviews as part of the selection process.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; First Nations Australian identification; Consistent achievement of minimum B grade average across all subjects; Demonstrated personal achievements and clear aspirations; Reflection of strong performance in latest school reports; Interview participation for shortlisted candidates; Commitment to college values and Student Code of Conduct
special_conditions: Scholarship offered on a one-year basis with option to reapply. Applicants must provide written outline of personal achievements, aspirations, and experiences as contemporary First Nations Australians. Shortlisted applicants required to participate in interviews. Minimum B grade average requirement across all subjects. Three-year minimum tenure commitment required.

school_name: The Hills Montessori School - Wairoa Campus
The Hills Montessori School - Yultiwirra Campus

overview: The Hills Christian Community School recognises eligible low-income families through the SA Government's School Card scheme. Successful applicants receive a 50% discount on tuition fees, making education more accessible for families experiencing financial hardship.
description: The Hills Christian Community School offers a 50% discount on tuition fees for families who successfully qualify for the SA Government's School Card scheme. This financial assistance program is designed to support low-income families whose combined family gross income falls within the income limits set for independent schools in South Australia. The discount applies specifically to the tuition fee component of school fees. Families who are unable to pay fees or other costs are encouraged to contact the Business Manager as soon as possible to discuss potential solutions and arrangements. The school emphasises a supportive approach to families experiencing financial difficulties and is willing to facilitate specific arrangements where necessary to ensure eligible students can continue their education.
eligibility_criteria: Family must meet SA School Card income limits for independent schools; Combined family gross income must be below established thresholds; Application must be completed and submitted through the School Card scheme process; Assessed by the Department for Education through Centrelink verification
special_conditions: Discount applies only to tuition fees, not other charges such as composite fees or services. Families must maintain communication with the school if experiencing difficulties. The school works with families to develop payment plans and alternative arrangements where needed. Income limits vary by family size and circumstances and are set by AISSA and CESA for independent schools.

overview: Tenison Woods College offers scholarships for full or partial remission of tuition fees to students entering Middle School or Senior School for the following year. Scholarships are awarded to students whose family would otherwise find it difficult to meet the cost of school fees and who show a commitment to a holistic education, preparing them for life, service, leadership and membership of the greater community.
description: Tenison Woods College provides need-based scholarships offering full or partial remission of tuition fees to incoming students entering Middle School (Year 6) or Senior School (Year 9) for the following year. The college's scholarship program is designed to remove barriers for children in need, including those under the care of South Australia's Minister for Child Protection. Selection is based on multiple factors including evidence of extracurricular and community involvement, good application towards academic studies, and financial, socioeconomic and/or compassionate circumstances. Nearly 60 students benefit from these fully supported placements annually, changing the trajectory of young lives. The selection process involves submission of an application form demonstrating the family's financial situation alongside evidence of the student's commitment to the school's ethos and values.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Middle School or Senior School for the following year; Family would otherwise find it difficult to meet the cost of school fees; Demonstrated commitment to holistic education, life, service, leadership and community membership; Evidence of extracurricular and community involvement; Good application towards academic studies; Financial, socioeconomic and/or compassionate circumstances
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Contact the Business Manager for further information about upcoming scholarships and application details.

program_name: District Council of Grant Mayor Scholarship
overview: Operating since 2010, the District Council of Grant Mayor Scholarship gifts $500 scholarships to Year 10-12 students who have demonstrated academic achievement, together with a commitment to the values and ethos of the school and local community and have an exemplary attendance record. This award recognises students showing leadership qualities and strong school commitment.
description: The District Council of Grant Mayor Scholarship has been operating since 2010 as an annual award programme that recognises outstanding Year 10-12 students at Tenison Woods College. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated academic achievement combined with a commitment to the values and ethos of the school and local community, along with an exemplary attendance record. The award value is $500 per recipient. Nominations are invited from students between February and April each year and are assessed by members of the Senior School. This program celebrates students who excel academically while maintaining strong engagement with their school community and demonstrating leadership qualities aligned with the college's mission.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 10, Year 11, or Year 12; Demonstrated academic achievement; Commitment to values and ethos of the school and local community; Exemplary attendance record
special_conditions: Nominations are invited from students between February and April each year and are assessed by members of the Senior School. Award recipients are presented with the scholarship during a school assembly.

overview: Tenison Woods Catholic School offers reduced school fees for eligible low-income families. This financial assistance program supports families who would otherwise find it difficult to meet the cost of school fees and ensures that Catholic education is accessible to all families in the community.
description: Tenison Woods Catholic School provides fee concessions for eligible low-income families, with the 2025 reduced fee set at $1,795 (compared to the standard full fee of $2,990). The school works closely with families to tailor flexible payment options and support arrangements based on individual circumstances.
