Families are encouraged to apply for enrolment even if full fees are not immediately affordable, as the school offers a range of options for fee concessions and flexible payment arrangements. Selection is based on financial need as demonstrated through family circumstances assessment. Eligibility is determined on a case-by-case basis through discussion with the school principal during the enrolment process.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating financial hardship or low-income status; Must be enrolling a child in Reception through Year 6; Application required during enrolment process; Individual circumstances assessment conducted by school principal
special_conditions: Families are encouraged to apply for enrolment even if the fees are not initially affordable. School works closely with families to determine flexible payment options tailored to individual circumstances. Fee concessions are assessed on a case-by-case basis by the school principal.

overview: Tenison Woods Catholic School offers free tuition for children commencing Reception in Term 3. This program removes the financial barrier for families whose children turn five between May 1 and October 31, making Catholic primary education accessible without fees during the mid-year school entry.
description: Tenison Woods Catholic School provides free school fees for all children who commence Reception in Term 3. This includes both Term 3 and Term 4 of the enrolment year, with no school fees charged. Mid-year Reception enrolments starting from Term 3 are completely free of charge, removing the cost barrier for families enrolling their child mid-year. Children who turn five between May 1 and October 31 are eligible to begin Reception in Term 3. The confirmation fee of $200 applies but is credited to the first fee account. This represents full fee remission for the mid-year intake period.
eligibility_criteria: Child must turn five years of age between 1 May and 31 October; Must commence Reception in Term 3; Open to all students regardless of academic or other criteria
special_conditions: Enrolment must commence in Term 3. Confirmation fee of $200 is non-refundable if enrolment is cancelled prior to commencement but is credited to the first invoice. Children commencing in Term 3 receive a minimum of 6 terms in Reception before progressing to Year 1.

overview: The 'Capturing the Spirit' Award is presented to two graduating Year 6 students who have demonstrated exceptional commitment to the values and spirit of Julian Tenison Woods and Saint Mary MacKillop. This award recognises students who embody the school's Josephite tradition and Catholic values throughout their primary school education.
description: The 'Capturing the Spirit' Award is presented annually at the Year 6 Graduation dinner to two graduating students who throughout their time at Tenison Woods Catholic School have reflected the spirit of Julian Tenison Woods and Saint Mary MacKillop. Recipients are selected based on their demonstrated commitment to the school's Catholic values, faith-based leadership, and embodiment of the Josephite charism 'In Omnibus Caritas' (In all things love). The award recognises students who have exemplified compassion, service to the community, and a commitment to making a positive difference. Two awards are presented each year to worthy students at the graduation ceremony. The award is non-monetary but carries significant prestige within the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 graduating students only; Must have demonstrated reflection of Julian Tenison Woods and Saint Mary MacKillop's spirit and values; Must show commitment to Catholic faith and school community; Selected by school leadership
special_conditions: Presented at Year 6 Graduation dinner. Selection is at the discretion of school leadership based on student demonstration of values throughout their primary education. Award is honour-based and recognises character and values alignment rather than academic achievement.

overview: Tenison Woods Catholic School presents Inspiring Excellence Awards to recognise one student per year level who demonstrates exceptional commitment to their learning. These awards are given at the end of year celebrations to acknowledge students who show positive engagement with their education across all year groups from Reception to Year 6.
description: The Inspiring Excellence Award is presented annually to one student in each year level (Reception through Year 6) who demonstrates a positive commitment to their learning. Recipients are selected by school leadership, with the Principal presenting the awards at the end-of-year celebrations. The award recognises students who show dedication, enthusiasm, and positive engagement with their studies and school community. Seven awards are presented each year, one per year level. The award celebrates academic commitment and learning engagement rather than purely academic achievement scores. Recipients are invited to accept their award at a formal ceremony during year-end events.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled students in years Reception to Year 6; Must demonstrate positive commitment to learning; Selected by school leadership on annual basis; One award per year level
special_conditions: One award is presented per year level. Selection is at the discretion of school leadership based on demonstrated commitment to learning throughout the year. Award is presented at end-of-year celebrations by the Principal. The award recognises learning engagement and positive commitment rather than academic excellence alone.

overview: The Hindmarsh Shield is a prestigious award recognising one student for all-round excellence and achievement. The winner is selected from among the recipients of the Inspiring Excellence Award and represents the highest achievement of the year.
description: The Hindmarsh Shield is an all-rounder award that recognises exceptional all-round achievement and excellence among Tenison Woods Catholic School students. The shield is awarded annually to one student who is selected from among the year-level recipients of the Inspiring Excellence Award. This award represents the school's recognition of outstanding commitment to learning and represents the highest honour in the school's awards system. The Hindmarsh Shield carries significant prestige within the school community and is presented at the year-end celebrations. The award is non-monetary and recognises exceptional overall commitment and achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Must be recipient of Inspiring Excellence Award for their year level; Selected from among year-level award recipients; Determined by school leadership at year-end
special_conditions: Recipient must be an Inspiring Excellence Award recipient. The Hindmarsh Shield represents the pinnacle of the school's awards recognising all-round excellence and commitment. Selection is made by school leadership based on overall achievement and demonstrated commitment.

overview: Tyndale Christian School offers the High Performance Athlete Programme to elite student athletes in Years 9-12 seeking to develop their sporting excellence and pursue US College Sports Scholarships. The programme provides advanced athletic training, specialised facilities, and dedicated support from an on-site US Sports Scholarship Manager.
description: The High Performance Athlete Programme is designed for elite athletes seeking to elevate their sporting performance and explore opportunities in health and sports industries, with particular focus on pursuing College Scholarships in the United States. The programme is available at no additional cost to current school fees for selected students. It includes access to a specialised Human Performance Centre facility, a comprehensive selection of sports-related academic subjects in the Senior School curriculum including Physical Education, Active Learning, Scientific Studies – Human Performance, Nutrition, Biology, and an exclusive Certificate III in Fitness program on campus. Students receive detailed, step-by-step support from an on-site US Sports Scholarship Manager who provides assessment and guidance through the complex US college application process. Various opportunities exist to represent the school in sporting events, and the programme includes mentoring from experienced staff.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 9, 10, 11, and 12; demonstrated high sporting ability and commitment to athletic excellence; willingness to participate in advanced training and specialised curriculum; selection via interview with Head of Sports, Pathways Centre Leader, and senior teaching staff
special_conditions: Applications for the elite programme open in late Term 3 and spots are limited. Prospective students will have an interview with the Head of Sports, Pathways Centre Leader, and senior teaching staff. Tyndale Christian School has a proud tradition of producing US Sports Scholarship recipients, and the programme is continually expanding.

overview: Trinity College Senior offers the Brad & Sally Collings Hockey Scholarship to support students who contribute to College life and demonstrate a passion for hockey. The scholarship recognises students who combine strong academic results with commitment to the sport and the College community.
description: This scholarship seeks to support a student who contributes to the richness of College life, enjoys team work, strives to do their best academically and has a passion for hockey. The aim of the scholarship is to foster diversity by ensuring an education at Trinity is available to a wide range of students who may require financial support to continue to pursue these interests. The scholarship will entitle the successful applicant a 50% Fee Reduction of tuition fees for Years 11 and 12. The scholarship was established by Brad and Sally Collings and their daughters' old scholars Sophie (2017), Maggie (2019) and Breanna (2021) who were all keen hockey players and generous volunteers to College life.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be in Year 10 or Year 11 at the time of application; Must be enrolled at Trinity College Senior the following year; Must demonstrate strong academic results; Must demonstrate a keen interest in hockey; Scholarship will consider the financial circumstances of the applicant; A current recipient may reapply in Year 11
special_conditions: Short listed applicants will be notified and may be required to attend an interview. All information supplied by applicants, including all financial details, will remain strictly confidential. The scholarship is awarded at the discretion of the Head of Trinity College. An annual review of the successful recipient will occur in December each year so that satisfactory progress is maintained.

overview: Trinity College Senior offers the Trinity College Indigenous Scholarship to support Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students who aspire to tertiary study. The scholarship recognises students with strong academic results and commitment to the College community who may require financial support.
description: This scholarship seeks to support an Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander student who aspires to future tertiary study and would benefit from financial support to help achieve this goal. The Trinity College Indigenous Scholarship was established by Professor Helen Thorne and Doctor Rupert Thorne. The scholarship will consider financial circumstances in order to promote academic excellence and support tertiary aspirations. Successful applicants will receive 50% Fee Reduction on tuition fees for Years 11 and 12, subject to satisfactory progress. Applicants must meet specific criteria regarding Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander status and demonstrate extensive commitment to the College.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be in Year 10 or Year 11 at the time of application; Must be enrolled in Trinity College Senior the following year; Must demonstrate strong academic results; Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must demonstrate a genuine intention to undertake future tertiary study; Must demonstrate an extensive commitment to the College and its endeavours; A current recipient may reapply in Year 11 or Year 12
special_conditions: Short listed applicants will be notified and may be interviewed by a panel which comprises of Trinity College senior staff and a Foundation Board representative. The scholarship will be awarded at the discretion of the Head of Trinity College. No award shall be made if, in the opinion of the Head, no candidate is of sufficient merit. Scholarship subject to annual review in December each year.

overview: Trinity College Senior offers the AON Scholarship to support students with excellence in engineering and innovation. The scholarship recognises students who excel in mathematics, science and technology and demonstrate a strong passion for pursuing careers in engineering.
description: This scholarship seeks to support a student who enjoys problem solving, team work, is entrepreneurial, a strong communicator, enjoys creative thinking and implementing ideas that make a difference. It aims to support a student who excels in science, mathematics or technology and has a passion for engineering and innovation and may require financial support to continue to pursue these interests. The scholarship will entitle the successful applicant a 50% Fee Reduction of tuition fees for Years 11 and 12. The applicant must demonstrate strong academic results in mathematics, science and technology subjects. The applicant must demonstrate a strong interest in pursuing a career in engineering.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be in Year 10 or Year 11 at the time of application; Must be enrolled at Trinity College Senior the following year; Must demonstrate strong academic results in mathematics, science and technology subjects; Must demonstrate a strong interest in pursuing a career in engineering; Scholarship will consider the financial circumstances of the applicant; A current recipient may reapply in Year 11; If currently attending a school other than Trinity College an Application for Enrolment must be completed online
special_conditions: Short listed applicants will be notified. The selection committee will make its selection on the basis of the application, an interview and presentation by the candidate. The scholarship will be awarded at the discretion of the Head of Trinity College in conjunction with an AON representative. No award shall be made if, in the opinion of the Head, no candidate is of sufficient merit. Annual review required in December each year.

overview: Unity College offers Academic Scholarships to prospective Year 8 students. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and potential, supporting students who demonstrate exceptional ability across academic and co-curricular pursuits.
description: Academic scholarships are awarded to prospective Year 8 students in the form of partial remission of tuition fees for the student's five years attendance at Unity College. An application process is undertaken to select scholarship recipients. Applicants must provide school reports, NAPLAN results, and copies of academic and sporting/music/other certificates of achievement, along with references from the current school principal, teachers, coaches or mentors. The selection process involves assessment of these supporting documents and references to identify students demonstrating exceptional academic ability.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective Year 8 students; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Requires NAPLAN results; Must provide references from current school principal, teachers, coaches or mentors; Open to all genders at this co-educational school
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for five years at Year 8. Recipients must maintain attendance and academic engagement throughout their time at the college.

overview: Unity College offers Academic Scholarships to new or existing Year 11 students. The scholarship recognises strong academic achievement and commitment, providing financial support for students entering or continuing in Year 11.
description: Academic scholarships are offered for new or existing students at Year 11 in the form of part remission of tuition fees. The scholarships are offered for two years at Year 11. An application process is undertaken to select scholarship recipients based on presentation of supporting evidence such as school reports, NAPLAN results, copies of academic and sporting/music/other certificates of achievement, and references from the current school principal, teachers, coaches or mentors. The selection criteria recognise overall academic performance and co-curricular achievements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new or existing Year 11 students; Must demonstrate solid academic performance; Requires school reports and achievement documentation; Must provide references from school principal, teachers, coaches or mentors; Open to all genders at this co-educational school
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for two years at Year 11. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and continue enrolment at the college.

overview: Unity College offers Academic Scholarships to new or existing Year 12 students. The scholarship supports students in their final year of secondary education who demonstrate strong academic achievement.
description: Academic scholarships are offered for new or existing students at Year 12 in the form of part remission of tuition fees. The scholarships are offered for one year at Year 12. An application process is undertaken to select scholarship recipients. Applicants must provide school reports, NAPLAN results, copies of academic and sporting/music/other certificates of achievement, and references from the current school principal, teachers, coaches or mentors. The selection process assesses overall academic ability and co-curricular involvement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new or existing Year 12 students; Must demonstrate academic ability; Requires school reports and certificates of achievement; Must provide references from school principal, teachers, coaches or mentors; Open to all genders at this co-educational school
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for one year at Year 12. Recipients must maintain engagement and satisfactory academic progress during their final year.

overview: Underdale High School offers the Stretch U Academy to enrich and extend academic learning for students ready to challenge themselves. This specialized program provides aspiring high achievers the opportunity to accelerate their learning and join a community where academic excellence is championed.
description: The Stretch U Academy is a selective entry program designed for students demonstrating high intellectual potential and academic capability. Students participate in enriching and extending academic learning experiences within a structured and supportive environment. The program is open to students outside the Underdale zone seeking specialized academic pathways. Applicants must complete an application process and successful admission is contingent on demonstrating readiness to challenge themselves academically. The program operates for the duration of a student's enrollment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students outside Underdale zone; Demonstrated high academic capability; Readiness to challenge themselves academically; Year 7 entry through primary school application or Year 8-12 entry through enrolment process
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students outside the Underdale catchment zone can seek enrolment via Stretch U Academy. Year 7 entry is made through application via the student's primary school during Year 6. Year 8-12 entry is managed by Underdale High School with availability contingent on subject availability and opportunity for student success.

overview: Underdale High School offers a specialist Football (Soccer) Academy providing an elite sports program for talented football players. The Academy supports students pursuing pathways in the sporting industry through specialized coaching and high-performance training.
description: The Football Academy is a selective entry program designed for talented soccer players seeking a structured pathway to develop technical skills, tactical understanding, and peak performance. The program combines specialized sports training with academic curricula, offering assessment through both practical football components and theoretical Australian Curriculum tasks. Students receive high-level technical, physical, social, and psychological player development while completing their South Australian Certificate of Education. The Academy aims to produce players with elite-level understanding of sport science, injury prevention, nutrition, fitness, and mental preparation. The program operates for the duration of the student's enrollment at Underdale High School.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students outside Underdale zone; Demonstrated talent in football/soccer; Commitment to elite sports development; Previous school reports required; Evidence of sporting commitment and coaching reference required; Successful completion of trial at school; Year 7-12 entry available
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Written application (Football Academy Application Form) required with deadline. Previous school reports and samples of school work must support application. Evidence of sporting commitment including football coach reference required. Applicants invited to participate in trial at Underdale High School. Applications for 2026 program open start of Term 1 and close end of Term 2. Only students accepted into Football Academy are permitted to wear Football Academy uniform. Uniform orders placed twice yearly with 8-week turnaround time. Students must maintain appropriate attendance and conduct standards.

overview: Underdale High School offers a Tennis Academy for driven young tennis players seeking to elevate their game. The program provides specialized coaching and high-performance training with Adelaide's best tennis coaches through newly launched facilities in 2026.
description: The Tennis Academy is a selective entry program launching in 2026 to provide specialized tennis training and high-performance coaching for dedicated young players. Under the leadership of Head Coach Luke Sebastiani (Tennis Australia Club Professional with 17 years of coaching experience and high-performance accreditation), the program develops technically sound, mentally tough athletes who move efficiently and confidently. The program combines elite coaching principles with academic curricula, supporting students' tennis development alongside their education. Coach Sebastiani's expertise includes work with top 100 ATP and WTA professional players and extensive experience in player development from grassroots to national competition levels. The program operates for the duration of student enrollment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students outside Underdale zone; Demonstrated dedication to tennis; Commitment to high-performance training; Year 7 entry through primary school application or Year 8-12 entry through Underdale enrolment process; Successful trial completion required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Tennis Academy launched in 2026. Students can seek admission to the specialized program after enrolment is complete and after successful trial, if places are available. Students outside the Underdale zone can seek enrolment via Tennis Academy. Head Coach Luke Sebastiani brings elite international coaching experience and professional player insights.

program_name: Integrated Gifted and Talented Education Program
overview: Underdale High School offers an Integrated Gifted and Talented Program that identifies and caters for students with high intellectual potential. The program provides differentiated learning experiences designed to meet the unique needs of gifted learners within Years 8-10.
description: The Gifted and Talented Program is an integrated selective entry program for students demonstrating high intellectual potential. The program operates within mainstream classes but provides differentiated instruction and enriched learning experiences tailored to gifted learners. Identification for the program is based on demonstrated intellectual ability and academic performance. The program operates during Years 8 to 10, providing students with extended and accelerated learning opportunities across subject areas. The program is designed to nurture students' intellectual, academic, and social-emotional growth through enriched curriculum experiences.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated high intellectual potential; High academic performance; Selected through school identification process; Enrollment in Years 8-10; Commitment to accelerated learning program
special_conditions: Program operates within Years 8-10. Students identified through school's assessment and selection processes. Differentiated instruction provided alongside regular curriculum. Selective entry program requiring demonstrated intellectual capability.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Gawler Place Campus
overview: University Senior College offers the Level 3 ACER Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in the Scholarship Selection Test. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and excellence in standardised testing.
description: The Level 3 ACER Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 11 (new students only) based entirely on the excellence of their results in the Scholarship Selection Test (SST) administered by the Australian Council for Education Research (ACER). Scholarship remission commences from tuition fee remissions of 25%, with the possibility of full scholarship in exceptional circumstances. Testing takes place in May for current Year 9 students who are entering into Year 11 the following year. Results will be distributed to parents in July. Whether a scholarship is offered or not, all students will have the opportunity to proceed to the interview stage to discuss future enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 entry only; New students only; Must be current Year 9 students; Assessed entirely on ACER test results
special_conditions: Continuation of the scholarship is subject to the recipient making good academic progress and displaying conduct of a high standard. Full scholarship may be awarded in exceptional circumstances at the discretion of the ACER scholarship committee.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Gawler Place Campus
overview: University Senior College offers the Level 2 ACER Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in the Scholarship Selection Test. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and excellence in standardised testing.
description: The Level 2 ACER Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 10 (new students only) based entirely on the excellence of their results in the Scholarship Selection Test (SST) administered by the Australian Council for Education Research (ACER). Scholarship remission commences from tuition fee remissions of 25%, with the possibility of full scholarship in exceptional circumstances. Testing takes place in May for current Year 9 and 10 students who are entering into Year 10 the following year. Results will be distributed to parents in July. Whether a scholarship is offered or not, all students will have the opportunity to proceed to the interview stage to discuss future enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 entry only; New students only; Must be current Year 9 students; Assessed entirely on ACER test results
special_conditions: Continuation of the scholarship is subject to the recipient making good academic progress and displaying conduct of a high standard. Full scholarship may be awarded in exceptional circumstances at the discretion of the ACER scholarship committee.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Gawler Place Campus
overview: University Senior College offers the All-Rounder Scholarship to students who demonstrate the ability to significantly contribute to the College in a range of areas. This scholarship recognises students with demonstrated excellence across multiple disciplines including academic, sporting, performing arts, leadership and community involvement.
description: The All-Rounder Scholarship is available to students entering Years 10, 11 or 12 in 2026 and is available to both new and current students. Applicants are required to submit documentation indicating ability in a minimum of three of the following areas: Academic, Public Speaking, Performing Arts, Leadership, Sport, Social Justice, and STEM. Scholarship remission is set at the discretion of the College and typically commences from tuition fee remissions of 25%. The scholarship recognises and rewards students who balance academic achievement with broader contributions to college life. Application requires submission of supporting documentation including school reports, references from teachers and community leaders, evidence of leadership roles, involvement in community groups and clubs, and state or national selection in sport or performing arts.
eligibility_criteria: Years 10, 11 or 12 entry; Ability demonstrated in minimum 3 of the following areas: Academic, Public Speaking, Performing Arts, Leadership, Sport, Social Justice, STEM; Available to new and current students; Must be eligible for enrolment at University Senior College
special_conditions: Applicants must submit a completed scholarship application form and supporting documentation including: copies of two most recent school reports, NAPLAN results, references from teachers and community leaders, evidence of leadership roles, evidence of involvement in community groups and clubs, evidence of state or national selection in sport or performing arts, and academic certificates of achievements. A shortlist of candidates will be selected for interviews with the Principal and Dean of Student Experience. Continuation is subject to good academic progress and conduct of a high standard.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Gawler Place Campus
overview: University Senior College offers the Principal's Scholarship to select students who demonstrate outstanding ability and potential. This scholarship is awarded at the discretion of the Principal based on school reports and references, recognising both academic achievement and broader contribution to the college community.
description: Each year a select number of Principal's Scholarships are awarded to both current and external students who are entering either Year 10, Year 11, second semester Year 11 or Year 12 in 2026. These scholarships are awarded to students on the basis of school reports and references, with scholarship remission set at the discretion of the Principal. The applicant's financial circumstances are able to be taken into consideration in the selection process. The scholarship recognises students with demonstrated ability and potential, particularly those who may benefit from additional financial support to pursue their studies at the College. Application requires submission of a completed scholarship application form and supporting documentation including school reports, references, and evidence of financial circumstances if applicable.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10, Year 11, second semester Year 11, or Year 12 entry; Current or external students; Must be eligible for enrolment at University Senior College; Selection based on school reports and references; Financial circumstances may be considered
special_conditions: Scholarship remission is set at the discretion of the Principal. Applicant's financial circumstances are able to be taken into consideration. Applicants must submit a completed scholarship application form and supporting hard copy documentation. Continuation is subject to good academic progress and conduct of a high standard.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Gawler Place Campus
program_name: University Senior College Old Scholars' Association (USCOSA) Scholarship
overview: University Senior College offers the USCOSA Scholarship to reward students demonstrating high academic ability and strong motivation to succeed. This scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate both academic excellence and financial need, with support from the University Senior College Old Scholars' Association.
description: USCOSA Scholarships are offered each year to multiple students where possible, commencing from tuition fee remissions of 25%. In exceptional circumstances, the USCOSA committee may provide a full scholarship. The scholarship is intended to support academically able students who demonstrate financial hardship and motivation to complete their SACE successfully and continue on to tertiary education. Applicants must be enrolled or eligible for enrolment at University Senior College. Selection is based on academic transcripts or clear potential for future high academic performance, evidence of financial hardship, and demonstrated motivation to complete SACE and pursue tertiary education. Applications from students who have attended public schools are welcomed. The selection process includes submission of supporting documentation and interviews with the USCOSA committee.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10, 11 or 12 entry; Current or new students; Must be enrolled or eligible for enrolment at University Senior College; Demonstrated financial hardship; High level of academic ability or clear potential for future performance; Strong motivation to complete SACE and continue to tertiary education; Applications from public school students welcomed
special_conditions: Applicants must be enrolled or eligible for enrolment at University Senior College. Must demonstrate financial hardship as evidenced in supporting letter of application. Must demonstrate high level of academic ability as evidenced in academic transcripts or clear potential for future high academic performance. Must demonstrate strong degree of motivation to complete their SACE successfully and continue on to tertiary education. A shortlist of candidates will be selected for interviews with the Principal and Dean of Student Experience. Continuation of the scholarship is subject to the recipient making good academic progress and displaying conduct of a high standard. Full scholarship may be provided in exceptional circumstances at discretion of USCOSA committee.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Gawler Place Campus
overview: University Senior College offers Hardship Scholarships to support students experiencing financial difficulties. This program provides tuition fee remissions on application at the discretion of the Principal to ensure deserving students can access education at the College.
description: Hardship Scholarships are available for Year 10, 11 or 12 entry and are available to both new and current students. Fee remissions are available on application at the discretion of the Principal. Applicants must provide further details in writing and evidence of financial circumstances. This program is designed to provide financial assistance to students who demonstrate both academic capability and financial need, ensuring that capable students are not prevented from attending the College due to financial hardship. The application process is flexible and assessment is conducted on a case-by-case basis by the Principal, taking into account individual circumstances and the level of demonstrated need.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10, 11 or 12 entry; Current or new students; Evidence of financial hardship required; Provision of written details about financial circumstances required
special_conditions: Fee remissions are available on application at the discretion of the Principal. Applicants must provide further details in writing and evidence of financial circumstances. Application is assessed on a case-by-case basis. Continuation subject to good academic progress and conduct of a high standard.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Jordan Campus
overview: University Senior College offers the Level 2 ACER Scholarship to recognise exceptional academic achievement among Year 10 entry students. This merit-based scholarship is awarded exclusively based on excellence in the Scholarship Selection Test administered by the Australian Council for Education Research (ACER).
description: The Level 2 ACER Scholarship is designed for new students entering Year 10 and is awarded entirely on the excellence of results in the Scholarship Selection Test (SST) administered by ACER. Financial benefits include a tuition fee remission awarded by the College. The scholarship is determined by ACER test performance, with no other assessment criteria. Testing takes place in May for current Year 9 students entering Year 10 the following year, with results distributed to parents in July. All students, whether offered a scholarship or not, have the opportunity to proceed to interview stage to discuss future enrolment. The continuation of the scholarship is subject to the recipient making good academic progress and displaying conduct of a high standard.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 9 students entering Year 10; New students only; Eligible to sit ACER Level 2 test; Open to all students regardless of school sector
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must make good academic progress and maintain high conduct standards. Continuation of the scholarship is subject to annual review. Whether a scholarship is offered or not, all students have the opportunity to proceed to interview stage to discuss future enrolment.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Jordan Campus
overview: University Senior College offers the Level 3 ACER Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in Year 11 entry students. This scholarship is awarded exclusively on the basis of excellence in the ACER Scholarship Selection Test.
description: The Level 3 ACER Scholarship targets new students entering Year 11 and is awarded entirely on the excellence of results achieved in the Scholarship Selection Test (SST) by ACER. The financial benefit is a tuition fee remission, with the amount determined by test performance and set by the College. Testing takes place in May for current Year 10 students entering Year 11 the following year. Results are distributed to parents in July. All applicants, regardless of scholarship outcomes, are given the opportunity to progress to interview stage to discuss enrolment at University Senior College. The scholarship is subject to the recipient maintaining good academic progress and conduct standards throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 10 students entering Year 11; New students only; Eligible to sit ACER Level 3 test; Open to all students regardless of school sector
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients must demonstrate good academic progress and maintain conduct of a high standard. Continuation is subject to annual review. All students proceeding to interview stage regardless of scholarship award. ACER tests are held in May with results distributed in July.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Jordan Campus
overview: University Senior College offers the All-Rounder Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate the ability to significantly contribute to the college in a range of areas beyond academics. The scholarship celebrates well-rounded students with demonstrated excellence in multiple discipline areas.
description: The All-Rounder Scholarship is available to students entering Years 10, 11 or 12 and recognises students who demonstrate the ability to contribute significantly to University Senior College across multiple areas. Applicants must submit documentation indicating ability in a minimum of three of the following areas: Academic, Public Speaking, Performing Arts, Leadership, Sport, Social Justice, or STEM. Financial benefits include a tuition fee remission as determined by the College. The application process requires submission of supporting documentation including school reports, NAPLAN results, references from teachers and community leaders, evidence of leadership roles, involvement in community groups, state or national selection in sport or performing arts, and academic achievement certificates. Applications are assessed through a competitive process with successful applicants selected for interviews with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 10, 11 or 12; New and current students eligible; Demonstrated ability in minimum three areas: Academic, Public Speaking, Performing Arts, Leadership, Sport, Social Justice, or STEM; Open to all students
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate ability in a minimum of three specified areas. Complete application with All-Rounder Scholarship Information Pack, USC Scholarship Application Form, and supporting documentation required. Shortlisted candidates selected for interviews with Principal and Dean of Student Experience.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Jordan Campus
overview: University Senior College offers the Principal's Scholarship to select students who demonstrate strong academic potential and personal qualities. Each year, a select number of Principal's Scholarships are awarded to both current and external students entering various year levels.
description: The Principal's Scholarship is available annually to students entering Year 10, Year 11, second semester Year 11, or Year 12. These scholarships are awarded to students on the basis of school reports and references, with scholarship remission set at the discretion of the Principal. Applicant financial circumstances may be taken into consideration as part of the assessment process. Each year, a select number of scholarships are awarded based on comprehensive assessment of school performance records and supporting references from educators and mentors. The financial benefit is a tuition fee remission, the amount of which is determined by the Principal based on individual circumstances and merit.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 10, Year 11, second semester Year 11, or Year 12; New and current students eligible; Strong school reports required; References from current school required
special_conditions: Scholarship remission set at the discretion of the Principal. Applicant financial circumstances may be considered. A select number awarded each year. Assessment based on school reports and references. Continuation subject to maintaining good academic progress and conduct standards.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Jordan Campus
program_name: University Senior College Old Scholars' (USCOSA) Scholarship
overview: University Senior College offers the USCOSA Scholarship, administered by the University Senior College Old Scholars' Association, to support students of demonstrated academic ability who face financial hardship. The scholarship recognises both academic merit and financial need, prioritising applications from public school students.
description: The USCOSA Scholarship is available annually to students entering Year 10, 11 or 12 and is available for both new and current students. Scholarships commence from tuition fee remissions of 25%, with the potential for full scholarship in exceptional circumstances as determined by the USCOSA committee. Applicants must demonstrate both financial hardship and a high level of academic ability, as evidenced in academic transcripts or clear potential for future high academic performance, plus strong motivation to complete their SACE successfully and continue to tertiary education. The application process requires submission of an application letter (500-1000 words) addressing the selection criteria, copies of relevant documentation, and hard copy submission to the Chairperson of the University Senior College Old Scholars' Association. Applications from students who have attended public schools are specifically welcomed.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 10, 11 or 12; Must be enrolled or eligible for enrolment at University Senior College; Demonstrated financial hardship; High level of academic ability or clear potential for future academic performance; Strong motivation to complete SACE and continue to tertiary education; Applications from public school students welcomed
special_conditions: Scholarships commence from 25% tuition fee remission with potential for full scholarship in exceptional circumstances. Applicants must demonstrate both financial hardship and academic ability. Continuation of scholarship is subject to the recipient making good academic progress and maintaining conduct of a high standard. Priority given to applications from public school students.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Jordan Campus
overview: University Senior College offers Hardship Scholarships to support students facing financial difficulty. Fee remissions are available on application and determined at the discretion of the Principal based on individual circumstances.
description: Hardship Scholarships are available to students entering Year 10, 11 or 12 for both new and current students. Fee remissions are available on application at the discretion of the Principal. Applicants must provide detailed written information about their financial circumstances and supporting documentation to substantiate the claimed hardship. The amount of fee remission is determined on a case-by-case basis by the Principal, who considers the evidence provided and the individual circumstances of each applicant. This program is designed to ensure financial barriers do not prevent access to education at University Senior College for deserving students.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 10, 11 or 12; New and current students eligible; Demonstrated financial hardship; Written evidence of financial circumstances required
special_conditions: Fee remissions available on application at discretion of the Principal. Applicants must provide written details and evidence of financial circumstances. Decisions made on individual case-by-case basis. Continuation of scholarship subject to maintaining good academic progress and conduct standards.

school_name: University Senior College at Adelaide University - Jordan Campus
overview: University Senior College partners with Wirltu Yarlu to provide access to senior education for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. This Federal Government initiative is designed to support Indigenous students' pathways to tertiary education.
description: The Wirltu Yarlu Indigenous Access Program is a Federal Government policy initiative designed to provide access to senior education for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. Applicants are assessed by Wirltu Yarlu in conjunction with a staff member from University Senior College. Selection is based on comprehensive assessment including written application, student support assessment, literacy and numeracy assessment, and selection interview. Students interested in studying at CASM (likely music-related) must also undertake a musical audition as part of the selection process. Fee remissions are available through this program. For complete details, applicants are directed to the Wirltu Yarlu website.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Assessed through Wirltu Yarlu and University Senior College partnership; Selection based on written application; literacy and numeracy assessment; interview; musical audition required for CASM students
special_conditions: Assessment conducted by Wirltu Yarlu in conjunction with University Senior College staff. Selection based on written application, support assessment, literacy and numeracy assessment, and interview. Musical audition required for CASM pathway students. Further information available on Wirltu Yarlu website.

program_name: Summit Program – A Pathway for High Ability Learners
overview: Unley High School offers the Summit Program as an enrichment and extension program for high ability learners from Year 7 to Year 10. The program engages in a future-focused learning approach that aims to prepare students across all curriculum areas with skills and capabilities to thrive in a rapidly changing world. Students participate in enriched programs across the four core subjects: English, humanities and social science, mathematics and science.
description: The Summit Program is an enrichment and extension program for high ability learners that runs from year 7 to 10. High ability learners require a learning environment that fosters wellbeing and learning outcomes consistent with their abilities. The program engages in a future focused learning approach that aims to prepare students across all curriculum areas and year levels with skills and capabilities to thrive in a rapidly changing and interconnected world. The program inspires students to be leaders of their own learning and school community, through collaboration and the use of critical thinking to solve complex problems and become mindful global citizens. Students participate in enriched programs for their four core subjects (English, humanities and social science, mathematics and science). Although some students may be more naturally inclined to particular subjects, the program offers a well rounded curriculum that provides enrichment across all learning areas. Languages are compulsory throughout the student's enrolment in the Summit Program from year 7 – 9. There is no tuition fee; this is included as part of standard secondary school enrolment at a government school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to high ability learners; Admission is merit-based, considering academic performance, teacher endorsements, and diagnostic evaluations; Year 7, 8, 9, 10 entry points; Age-appropriate academic ability required
special_conditions: Admission to the Summit Program is merit-based, considering academic performance, teacher endorsements, and diagnostic evaluations. Languages are compulsory throughout enrolment from Year 7–9. Students are recommended to continue a language during senior years of schooling. Year 10 maths plus (pre-methods maths) is strongly recommended in preparation for stage 1 maths methods or stage 1 specialist maths.

program_name: Special Interest Rowing Program (HPE Rowing – Year 7)
overview: Unley High School offers the Health and Physical Education (HPE) Rowing program as a core HPE alternative for Year 7 to Year 10 students. The program is part of the school's Special Interest Rowing Program selection process and allows students to combine curriculum study with participation in the Unley High School Rowing Club. HPE Rowing involves both practical on-water training and theory lessons through the lens of rowing skill training and physical preparation.
description: Health and Physical Education (HPE) Rowing is a core HPE alternative offered by Unley High School for Year 7 to Year 10. Prospective Year 7 students complete a talent identification process in Year 6 to gain entry into Year 7, known as the Special Interest Rowing Program selection process. Students undertake between 1 and 3 on-water training sessions each week depending on their age group. The subject fee applicable to HPE Rowing is $600 per year for Years 7-9 (full year subject) and $300 for Year 10 (1 semester). This charge covers all compulsory elements of the course, including the cost of consumables, coaches, transport, minor equipment repairs and replacement, etc. Students enrolled in HPE Rowing are expected to participate in rowing as an extracurricular activity. Come and Try sessions are run during November and December, allowing families to experience the sport before committing further. Applications for the Year 7 class open in August and close in December of the preceding year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must complete talent identification process in Year 6; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Accepted through the special options selection process; Students are encouraged to participate in Come and Try sessions between November-December
special_conditions: Students are expected to participate in rowing as an extracurricular activity. Families experiencing financial hardship can contact the school to discuss alternative arrangements. Applications are through an official application form sent with enrolment offer, or by contacting the Rowing Manager at dl.0797.rowing@schools.sa.edu.au. Come and Try sessions are held at the Torrens Boatshed (on-water) and at the School's Fitness Centre (land-based). Out of zone applications for Year 7 HPE Rowing Class have closed.

overview: Unley High School is the first public secondary school in South Australia to introduce the French Bilingual/Binational Program (FBBP). The program is offered from Year 7 to Year 10 and provides bilingual/binational education with up to four subjects delivered in French following a harmonised curriculum. The program supports both French background and additional language learners to develop a high level of proficiency in French and intercultural understanding.
description: The French Bilingual/Binational Program (FBBP) at Unley High School delivers a sustainable, innovative, inclusive, continuous, high-quality 'Label Franc Education' accredited program from Year 7 onwards. The program offers up to four subjects delivered in French following a harmonised curriculum, blending the content of French and Australian government curricula with French as the media for teaching and learning. Subject areas offered include Français, Histoire, Géographie, Mathématiques and Sciences, with Français being the main language of instruction. The program supports both French background and additional language learners to develop a high level of proficiency in French and intercultural understanding while expanding learning outcomes across the curriculum and developing enhanced literacy, social, emotional and cognitive skills including critical and creative thinking, problem solving and international mindedness. The FBBP has a selective entry process, in accordance with the Department for Education's procedure for special interest programs, allowing a number of out of zone students to apply for enrolment. There is no tuition fee; this is included as part of standard secondary school enrolment at a government school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 entry; Requires age-appropriate proficiency in French; Must be able to meet curriculum expectations successfully in both French and English; Selective entry process managed by school leadership; Out of zone students may apply; Details about selective entry criteria and enrolment process available in Term 1 each year
special_conditions: Selective entry process in accordance with Department for Education's procedure for special interest programs. Students require age-appropriate proficiency to access the teaching and learning program and must meet curriculum expectations successfully in both French and English. The program is accredited by 'Label Franc Education'. Details about Year 7 selective entry criteria, enrolment process and timeframes are made available on the school website each year in Term 1.

program_name: School Card Scheme – Government Financial Assistance
overview: Unley High School refers families to the School Card scheme, which offers financial assistance with school fees (materials and services charges) for students attending government schools each year. The School Card program is administered at the state level and aims to support families experiencing financial hardship to access school materials and services.
description: The School Card scheme offers financial assistance with school fees (materials and services charges) for students attending government schools each year. School Card income limits have been increased, resulting in more families being eligible for assistance. Applicants can apply for School Card assistance through the official application process on the Department for Education website. The application form is completed online and sent directly to the government school(s) where the child(ren) will attend. If two children attend different schools, both sites will receive a copy of the application form. School Card can help with school related expenses, including enrolment fees, materials and services charges. The support is means-tested based on family income and is designed to ensure all students can access the materials and services they need for their education. There is no cost for applying, and the application process is confidential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students attending Unley High School; Means-tested based on family income; Families must apply through the official School Card application process; Income thresholds determine eligibility; Non-Australian citizens and international students may have different eligibility criteria; All South Australian government school students eligible to apply
special_conditions: Means-tested support based on family income. Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the school confidentially to discuss alternative arrangements. Families should visit the official Department for Education website to apply, as applications can be submitted at any time during the school year.

overview: Urrbrae offers the Mortlock Scholarships, a unique and historically significant program recognising academic excellence across all year levels. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and are available from Year 7 through to tertiary study in agriculture-related fields.
description: The Mortlock Scholarships represent a significant bequest established in 1950 through Mr John Andrew Tennant Mortlock's will, with £10,000 bequeathed specifically for agricultural education at Urrbrae. Today, the scholarship fund is worth over £55,000 per annum and is distributed at both school and graduate levels. Over 70 students from Years 7–12 and recent Urrbrae graduates (Old Scholars) pursuing agricultural or related degrees at university receive tertiary Mortlock Scholarships in recognition of their academic achievement. The scholarships are allocated based on the interest earned from the Mortlock fund each year, enabling high-achieving students to continue their education. First awarded in 1952 to only 6 students, these scholarships have now assisted hundreds of Urrbrae students to achieve their academic and professional goals by providing opportunities to pursue higher education, engage in research and development, and gain practical experience in agriculture. Scholarships are distributed annually with recipients announced at the school presentation night.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7 to Year 12; Open to recent Urrbrae graduates (Old Scholars) pursuing agricultural or related degrees at university; Demonstrated high academic achievement; Open to all genders
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Mortlock Scholarships are awarded annually to recognise academic excellence from the previous academic year. Interest from the fund is distributed each year to allocate scholarships to high achieving students. Recipients are announced at the school presentation night ceremony.

program_name: AJ and PA McBride Agriculture Awards
overview: Urrbrae offers the AJ and PA McBride Agriculture Awards, sponsored by AJ and PA McBride Pastoral, to recognise outstanding agricultural achievement among senior students. The awards acknowledge enthusiastic and highly successful agriculture students who have excelled in their studies and demonstrated active involvement in agricultural clubs.
description: The AJ and PA McBride Agriculture Awards are sponsored by AJ and PA McBride Pastoral, a major South Australian agribusiness that celebrated its 100th year of operation in 2020. The McBride Board has generously provided annual sponsorship for these awards over the last decade. In 2022, McBride's increased the combined value of the awards from $5,000 to $10,000, allowing additional students to be recognised for their efforts. Five students from each senior school year level (Years 10, 11, and 12) receive a McBride Agriculture Award, recognising achievement in agricultural studies and active involvement in at least one Agricultural Club or agricultural work experience. Additionally, one student from each senior year level receives a McBride Excellence in Agricultural Award, recognising exemplary achievement across the selection criteria. The awards demonstrate ongoing partnership between agricultural industry and education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Years 10, 11, 12 students; Outstanding results in agricultural studies required; Active involvement in at least one Agricultural Club or agricultural work experience; Recognition of exemplary achievement in selection criteria for excellence award
special_conditions: Five students from each senior school year level receive a standard McBride Agriculture Award. One additional student from each year level receives a McBride Excellence in Agricultural Award for exemplary achievement. Combined fund value increased to $10,000 in 2022.

overview: Urrbrae offers the Peter Waite Old Scholars Award, sponsored by the Urrbrae Old Scholars Association, to recognise significant all-round contribution to the school community. The award honours a Year 12 Agriculture student who demonstrates academic success, community participation, and personal attributes valued by the school.
description: The Peter Waite Old Scholars Award is sponsored by the Urrbrae Old Scholars Association through the generosity of Peninsula Farming, which is owned and operated by Old Scholar and Association President Jamie Smith. The award recognises a Year 12 Agriculture student who has made a significant all-round contribution to the Urrbrae community, including participation in a range of community activities, successful academic achievement, and personal attributes that Urrbrae strives to instil in all students. Jamie Smith generously donated $1,000 for this award, which represents the annual value. The award is presented annually to one student at the school presentation night, acknowledging those who have contributed meaningfully across multiple dimensions of school life and demonstrated leadership and personal development.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must be enrolled in Agriculture studies; Demonstrated significant all-round contribution to Urrbrae community; Participation in a range of community activities; Successful academic achievement; Personal attributes aligned with school values
special_conditions: Award recognises all-round contribution including academic achievement, community activities, and personal attributes. Only one award presented annually to a Year 12 Agriculture student.

program_name: Royal Agricultural and Horticultural Society Education Foundation Scholarship
overview: Urrbrae offers the Royal Agricultural and Horticultural Society Education Foundation Scholarship to support recent graduates pursuing tertiary study. The scholarship recognises graduates who have achieved the highest ATAR scores and are commencing agriculture, horticulture, or allied field study at university.
description: The Royal Agricultural and Horticultural Society Education Foundation Scholarship is a major scholarship provided by the Show Foundation to support recent Urrbrae graduates with their tertiary study in agriculture, horticulture, or allied areas. The scholarship is valued at $6,000 and is awarded annually to the student from the previous academic year who achieved the highest ATAR score and subsequently commenced an agriculture or horticulture-related degree at university. This partnership between the Royal Adelaide Show and education demonstrates industry commitment to supporting agricultural careers. The scholarship assists successful Year 12 graduates to progress into higher education in agriculture-related disciplines, recognising both academic excellence in the final year of schooling and commitment to further study in the field.
eligibility_criteria: Open to recent Urrbrae graduates; Must have achieved highest ATAR score in the previous academic year; Must have commenced agriculture, horticulture, or allied field study at tertiary level; Must be pursuing university degree in agriculture-related area
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded to the graduate from the previous academic year who achieved the highest ATAR score and commenced an agriculture or horticulture-related degree. Valued at $6,000 per annum for tertiary study support.

overview: Urrbrae offers Mathematics Scholarships for Years 9 to 11 students to encourage and extend high-achieving students in mathematics. The scholarships recognise excellence in the subject and support students pursuing advanced mathematical study.
description: Urrbrae offers annual mathematics scholarships specifically designed to encourage and extend high-achieving students in Years 9 to 11. These scholarships complement the broader scholarship program and acknowledge students who demonstrate exceptional mathematical ability and potential. The scholarships are part of Urrbrae's commitment to developing academic excellence across key learning areas beyond agriculture, with specific support for students pursuing advanced study in mathematics. While specific financial value is not detailed in available sources, these scholarships provide recognition and support for mathematical achievement at secondary level. The program reflects the school's emphasis on science, technology, and mathematics alongside its agricultural focus.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Years 9, 10, 11 students; Demonstrated excellence in mathematics; High academic achievement in mathematics subject; Commitment to extending mathematical knowledge and skills
special_conditions: Mathematics scholarships are available for Years 9 to 11 only to encourage and extend students. Selection based on demonstrated mathematical excellence and achievement.

school_name: Youth Education Centre - Goldsborough Road Campus (Cavan)
